in deceit that he do not only allure the infidel to follow he but even a part of the pastor and the visible church to their destruction which be signify by his draw after he with his tail as follower of his enticement the three part of the star of heaven and cast they to the earth this dragon have wait to destroy her birth and for earnestness gape for it before it be bear and not able to prevail CHRIST have redeem with his blood and of who election he have bear witness to his father so on the other part it enrage the dragon so that he become the cruel tempter of man upon the earth aswell for that his place of accuse in heaven be take away by the mystery of the redemption which be signify by this fight as for that he know within short space he be by Christ second come to be cast down from sudden 17 the dragon therefore or the devil become more wrathful and enrage then before against the woman or the church and go about by some other way to make war against the rest of the woman seed who keep the commandment of GOD and have the testimony of CHRIST to GOD the father that they be choose and call for these be only the true posterity of the church to wit the successor in grace faith and truth 18 and I stand upon the sea shore I mean it seem to i that I stand upon the sea shore because I do wait to see come out of it which represent all people and nation such power as Satan will employ to fight against the church for the declare whereof this vision be show unto i and whereof these two last great wonder be but the introduction that by these thing past as the root I may the better understand the branch which be to bud forth thereof as follow chap. 13 argument the pope arise his description his rise cause by the ruin of the four monarchy the roman empire the rise of the false and papistical church her description her conformity with her monarch the pope the great reverence bear to the pope by many nation and not only to he but to his legate a general defection so great as there shall not be a other visible church but the popedom of the first pope who do take to himself all their blasphemous and arrogant style and then I see a beast rise out of the sea to wit from among the number of nation and people I see a monarchy choose and erect up by this dragon the devil and it have seven head and ten horn and ten diadem upon the ten horn the signification of these head and horn be declare unto i by a angel as you shall hear in the place convenient hereafter chap. 17. and upon these head be the name of blasphemy for they by the persecution of the saint and adore false god shall both by word and deed blaspheme the name of the eternal @ this beast or monarchy Daniel 7. be the four king or monarchy whereof Daniel prophesy to wit even that monarchy which present reign and have the power of the other three revive in it for it be far great than they and therefore as that monarchy of the leopard get that name because of the swiftness of the conquest and that of the lion because of the mightiness and cruelty thereof and that of the bear because of the strength and long stand thereof so this be call like the leopard to wit in shape whereof come her agility head like a lion because his strength be in the head as you hear already and legged like a bear because in the bear leg consist his great strength and durableness this proportion signify that this monarchy be far great than all the rest and all their power be revive in it as I say before @ and I perceive that one of the head of the beast have be deadly wound but the wound thereof be heal and the whole earth follow this beast with a great wonder this be to signify unto i that it be not of this beast that I be ordain to forewarn you for the worst of this beast be almost past already and this monarchy shall be within short space destroy but this beast or monarchy be show unto i because out of the ruin thereof shall rise in that same seat where it be that heretical monarchy whereof I be to forewarn you which be signify by the deadly wound it get on the head which be heal again for as the phoenix revive of she own ash as profane story make mention so out of the ash of this empire shall rise and be revive a other which shall grow so mighty that the whole earth that be without shall with amazement reverence obey and follow it as you hear present declare @ and they adore the dragon who give power to the beast for they shall give themselves over to the work of darkness which be to serve and adore the devil who raise up this beast to make war against the seed of the woman chap. 12. as you hear before and they also adore the devil in his instrument by reverence that beast and monarchy erect by he and they say who be like unto the beast or who may fight with he for this monarchy shall be so strong in worldly power as the world shall think it so far in strength above all other power that it be impossible to overcome it especial that the little stone which be cut without hand out of the mountain mention by Daniel Daniel 2. shall ever destroy it which notwithstanding at the last shall bruise it in piece @ and there be a mouth give unto it to speak great thing and blasphemy it be say in Daniel Daniel 7.11 that his mouth shall speak in magnificency and utter word against the sovereign to wit this monarchy and king thereof shall extol himself far above all live creature and shall usurp far high style than ever be hear of before by the which and by his false doctrine together he shall so derogate from the honour of GOD and usurp so all power only proper unto he as it shall be great word against he and blasphemy of his name and there be power give he to do to wit GOD shall permit his tyranny to increase and persecute the saint the space of two and obscure in that place as the plague of the sin against the second table to wit this great beast here mention and the other reveal a vow and open enemy of Christ church shall both gather their force to against God and speak injurious word against his name his tabernacle to wit his which be the church militant and they that dwell in heaven for his reign shall be so great that he shall not only blaspheme the name of God in such sort as you hear already and persecute the member of Christ that shall be on the earth in his day but likewise upbraid with calumny the soul of the saint depart @ and for that effect he be permit by God to make war against the saint and he give he power to overcome they corporal and to rule over all tribe tongue and nation so great shall his monarchy and power be @ and so all the indweller of the earth shall adore he to wit a great part of they shall reverence he who name be not write in the book of life which be the lamb that be slay which book be write before this false church make that all small or great rich or poor bond or free in short all man of whatsoever degree shall take the character or seal of this monarchy into their right hand or into their since you see that this defection shall be so general beware of every one that shall say lord lord thereby to deceive you for you see by this that false prophet shall for a time so triumph as they scatter for no more be there a midst betwixt God and the devil nor betwixt the reward thereof heaven and hell and as one of these two master we must of necessity follow so of the same necessity to one of these two place must we go and then I see in the wilderness a woman even Babylon that whore sit on a scarlet colour and bloody beast even as she be sit before in the likeness of a man upon a pale horse in the four seal and this beast be full of blasphemy and have seven head and ten horn as you hear before @ and the woman be clothe with purple and scarlet and precious stone and pearl and have in her hand a golden cup for this monarchy and the monarch thereof shall aswell be corporal clothe with these colour and deck with precious stone as also these colour which be fine of all other and these precious stone signify that this monarchy and the seat thereof shall be most glorious and glance to the eye of the world as I say before which shall not only be the true church by appearance of outward glory but even retain many of the general point of religion which be signify by her golden cup but this cup be full of abomination and of the uncleanness of her spiritual whoredom for albeit in many point she shall retain the truth which shall abuse man and allure they to she yet shall she mix and poison this truth with her own abominable and heretical invention and tradition and with the uncleanness of her spiritual adultery whereof you have hear before @ and on her forehead be write a mystery Babylon the great to wit spiritual Babylon as you hear before the mother of the whoredom and the abomination of the earth for from she shall proceed the great and in a manner the only chief abuse and heresy colour and clothe with the show and title of Christiantie with the which these who shall outward say lord lord shall ever be infect with until the consummation and as a mother she shall not only breed but shall be the chief nourisher and maintainer of they and this be call a mystery because although this abuse shall be public as be signify by be write on her forehead yet none shall consider the abuse thereof but only such who eye it shall please God to illuminate for that effect @ and I see the woman drunken with the blood of the saint and of the martyr and witness of jesus the saviour to wit she shall greedy and cruel shed their blood without all measure reason or pity as you have often hear before and when I thus see she I wonder at she marvelous and I can not conjecture the meaning of the seven head and ten horn that the beast have on who she sit @ and the angel who have now show she unto i as you now have hear see i thus wonder say unto i marvel not for I will reveal unto thou the mystery of this woman and of the beast she ride upon which have seven head and ten horn @ this beast that thou have see or monarchy be for it be the four monarchy which be very great and flourish and be not for it be now so far decay that in a manner it be not and it be to rise again out of the bottomless pit as you hear in the five trumpet how foone the wound of the head shall be heal whereof you hear before and it shall go to perdition as you often have hear already and the indweller of the earth shall wonder who name be not write in the book of life before the foundation of the world be lay of this wonder you hear before they shall wonder I say at this beast which be to wit in great power and be not to wit in a manner as you present hear and yet be I mean do stand though far decay from the former greatness @ take good heed unto this that I declare unto thou for herein shall the true wisdom of man be try to wit in know by this my description what particular empire and tyranny I speak of and the seven head of this beast signify aswell seven material hill whereupon the seat of this monarchy be situate as also seven king or diverse form of magistrate that this empire have have and be to have hereafter 10 five of they have be already one be present and make the six another shall follow it and make the seven but it be not yet come and when it come it shall remain but a very short space 11 and this beast which be to wit so great and be not for now it be decay as thou present have hear it be the eight and yet one of the seven for this beast which rise out of the ruin of the four monarchy as you hear before in respect it use a heretical tyranny over the conscience of man by that new form of empire be different from any of the rest and so be the eight and yet because this form of government shall have the same seat which the rest have and use as great tyranny and great upon the world and shall use the same form in civil government which one of the seven use therefore because it be so like they I call it one of the seven 12 and the ten horn which thou see signify ten king to wit the great number of subaltern magistrate in all the province under that monarchy who have not yet receive their kingdom for under all the diverse sort of government that shall be in it except the last and heretical sort these subaltern power shall be but in the rank of subject but they shall take their kingly power with the beast to wit at the very time that this apollyon shall rise out of the ash of the four beast or monarchy the king of the earth shall become his slave and subaltern magistrate whereas the subject be only the power of that monarchy before so as the horn or power of this beast be but of subject before it be wound but after the heal of it the worldly king and ruler shall become the power and horn of it 13 these shall have one counsel and shall give their strength and power to the beast to wit these king shall all willing yield obedience to Babylon and shall employ their whole force for the maintenance of that monarchy and the persecution of the saint 14 for they shall fight with the lamb in his member albeit all in vain for in the end the lamb shall overcome they because he be lord of lord and king of all king and these that be with he and follow he be call choose and faithful 15 he also say unto i the water that thou see this whore sit upon be the people multitude nation and tongue that have subject themselves to her empire 16 but as touch these ten horn thou see thus far I foretell unto thou although that for a time these king shall be slave and servant to Babylon and shall be her instrument to persecute the saint the time shall come before the consummation that they shall hate the whore who abuse they so strong and long and shall make she to be alone for they shall withdraw from she their subject the nation that be her strength and shall make her naked for they shall discover the mystery of her abomination and shall eat her flesh and burn she with fire to wit they shall spoil she of her riches power and glory and so destroy she 17 but do not thou wonder at this for God give they in their heart to wit permit they to be abuse by she for a space that they may do what please she and consent to all her unlawful policy and pretence and give their kingdom unto this beast until the word of God may be accomplish to wit they shall submit their very crown and take the right thereof from she unto the fullness of time here prophesy at what time God shall raise they up as you hear to destroy Babylon for the heart of the great king as well as of the small subject be in the hand of the lord to be his instrument and to turn they as it shall please he to employ they 18 and this woman or whore which thou see be that great city and seat of this beast or monarchy which bear rule over the king of the earth as thou have hear already but although it be one seat yet diverse and a great number of king or head thereof shall succeed into it one to another all uphold a heretical religion and false worship of God and one form of government as the four monarchy do out of the which this do spring as you have hear chap. 18 argument the sorrow of the earth for the destruction of the popedom the profit that worldly man have by his stand the great riches and wealth of that church the pope by his pardon make merchandise of the soul of man heaven and the saint rejoice at his destruction albeit the earth and the worldling lament for the same and then I see another angel come down from heaven have great power so that the earth shine with his glory for so soon as God by one of the seven angel who have the phial have more plain describe unto i this woman sit on the beast than he do before he now appoint this other angel who be Christ to declare unto i and proclaim to the world as be signify by his come down to the earth for that cause the just condemnation of Babylon according to her sin @ and he cry out with a loud voice say it be fall it be fall Babylon that great city and it be make the dwell place of unclean spirit and the habitation of all unclean and hateful fowl to wit it shall be destroy and that great city the seat of that monarchy shall be desolate for ever even as it be prophesy of jerusalem @ because all nation have drink of the vine of her whoredom and the king of the earth have commit whoredom with she and the merchant of the earth be become rich by the great wealth of her delight in so great a worldly glory and pomp do that monarchy shine @ and I hear another voice from heaven to wit the voice of the holy spirit say go forth from she my people to wit all the choose lest you be participant of her sin and of the plague which be to fall upon she for they for if but outward you haunt with she and seem to bear with her abomination you shall be account guilty of her sin for if you will have Christ to profess you public at the latter day before his father and his angel and reward both your body and soul with eternal felicity you must not be ashamed to serve he both in body and soul before man and this warning I give you beforehand to make you inexcusable who will otherwise do @ for her sin be come to such a height as they have touch the heaven and God be mindful of they than not only haunt not with she as I have say for it be not enough not to do evil but @ raise yourselves up against she and render the like that she have do to you yea pay she with the double of she own work and in the cup which she propine unto other render she the double to wit trouble and destroy she by all mean and in all thing even as she trouble and destroy other before and according to her pride and wantonness recompense she with torment woe and wail @ for she say in her mind I fit a queen or be a stable monarch neither be I a widow or shall ever be desolate nor shall ever see dolour or taste destruction @ and therefore because she thus build her felicity upon her worldly strength by worldly instrument shall she be plague with death with dolour with hunger and burn with fire to wit after suffer all sort of torment she shall in the end be utter destroy for strong be the lord God who shall condemn she @ and then shall the king of the earth who be her horn and have commit whoredom and riotousness with she before weep and lament for pity when they see the smoke of her burn for although some of themselves shall be the destroyer as you hear before yet shall her destruction be so great as their heart shall pity the work of their hand when they shall see the great smoke of her destruction 10 and they shall stand far off from her torment to wit her torment shall put they in memory of their guiltiness of her sin which shall affray they wonderful and shall say in great admiration alas alas for that great city Babylon that strong city who judgement and destruction be all come in one hour and at once 11 and the merchant of the earth shall weep and mourn for she because their merchandise will no more be buy for her pomp shall make the merchant rich by get ready sale of all fine ware 12 such as gold silver precious stone pearl fine linen purple silk and scarlet for her garment and all kind of vessel to do her idolatrous service of ivory costly wood brass iron or marble stone 13 cinnamon and all kind of odour for her church with ointment and incense for the same purpose and the fine flower of wheat and all kind of victual and cattle and sheep for her sumptuous banquet and horse and chariot and slave for her triumph and procession and soul of man for she shall have many that shall be merchant unto she of the soul of man by sell for money pardon give by that monarch which shall be think to have power to save redeem and free man soul but you shall hear more short of this hereafter 14 and the fruit of the desire of thy soul o Babylon shall go from thou to wit thy joy and delight shall all turn to sorrow and all fat and fair thing be go from thou to wit thou shall leave all profit and pleasure neither shall thou ever find they any more for thou shall be destroy for ever 15 and so the merchant of these stuff be make rich by the buy and sell of they they shall stand afar off from thy torment and weep and wail 16 say alas alas for that great city that be clothe with fine linen purple and scarlet and be of so glister a pomp as be gild with gold and deck with precious stone and pearl 17 for lo now how in one hour all her riches and pomp be evanish and all the governor and owner of ship and all the multitude of man in the ship and all the mariner in they and all these who gain their live upon the sea shall stand afar off for fear 18 and cry see the smoke of her burn say with a great admiration who be like in power or shine glory to this city 19 and for pity of her decay and sorrow for want by that mean the carry to she from all other country all sort of merchandise they shall cast dust and ash upon their head and say alas alas for that great city wherein be make rich all these that have ship upon the sea by the price and trade she make we have and now she be make desolate in one hour 20 but although the earthly man be sorrowful for her fall as you have hear because they want their earthly commodity and pleasure thereby which she who religion be earthly to wit found upon man tradition and invention and maintain by earthly pomp and power do make they enjoy yet rejoice you heaven for her fall and you holy apostle and prophet be glad thereof for God in punish she have revenge your cause 21 then for confirmation of this prophecy of her destruction I see a strong angel take a great stone like a millstone and cast it in the sea say even with such a force shall Babylon that great city be cast down and the very place thereof shall no more be find as jeremy prophesy of corporal Babylon 22 and the sound of harper and musician and player on pipe and trumpet shall no more be hear in thou for no joy nor mirth shall any more be in that monarchy or the seat thereof nor no craftesman of any craft shall be find in thou neither shall the grind of the mill be hear any more in thou for that city or seat and monarchy shall no more be inhabit 23 and the light of a candle shall be no more find in thou and the voice of the husband and the wife shall no more be hear in thou for as it shall not be inhabit any more by the wicked so neither shall the godly dwell therein so accurse shall it be so as the lamp of the five virgin shall not burn there neither shall Christ and his spouse the true church any more be there although that during the stand of that monarchy some choose though few and secret be and at all time shall be even within that city the seat thereof who merchant be the great man of the earth and with who witchcraft all nation wereseduced 24 and the blood of the prophet and of the saint be find in she and of all they that be slay upon the earth to wit this plague of destruction shall just fall upon she aswell for that she make her messenger or ambassador who be great in power as you hear before to be the seller of her pardon prayer sacrament merit and even of the sin and soul of man as you have present hear and so by that mean and the like bewitch as it be and abuse many nation as also for that she have cruel persecute and murder the saint so as the blood of all the saint since Abel who willing sacrifice their life for the love of God truth and for the testimony of his son shall be lay upon her head and impute unto she in follow fulfil and exceed the rage of former tyrant oppress and persecute the church of God chap. 19 argument the saint praise God for joy that the pope be destroy the glorious form of Christ second come set down at large the pope and his church be condemn for ever then according to the voice speak to the heaven and prophet and apostle there to wit that they shall rejoice as much for the fall of Babylon as the unregenerate man do lament therefore as you have hear according I say to this exhortation I hear the voice of a great multitude in heaven say hallelujah which be if you interpret it praise God with a loud voice salvation honour glory and power be only with our lord God @ for true and just be his judgement and he have condemn that great whore who have defile the earth with her whoredom and he with his hand have revenge upon she the blood of his servant @ then for the second time they say hallelujah for the smoke of her destruction go up in all world to come for she shall never rise again but shall be burn with a perpetual fire @ and likewise for thanksgiving for the same the four and twenty elder fall down upon their face before God and adore he and the four beast also adore God sit upon his throne and all the beast and elder say with one voice amen hallelujah @ and I hear a voice come from the throne to wit from one of the four beast that support it say praise our god all you his servant and all you that fear he small and great @ and then conforme to that direction I hear as it have be the sound or voice of a great multitude and as it have be the sound of many water and as the sound of great thunder to wit the voice of all the creature in heaven who sound in greatness may be compare to the noise of many water or to the roar of the thunder and they say all in one voice hallelujah because our lord God almighty have now reign by destroy Babylon and her follower @ let we therefore rejoice and be glad and render he all glory for the marriage of the lamb be come to wit the latter day be at hand and his wife have make herself ready for he to wit his church be now purify from among the wicked @ and it be give unto she to clothe herself with pure and bright linen which be the justification of the saint for as fine linen be a pure bright white and precious stuff so be the saint clothe with that precious undefiled and glorious garment of righteousness through imputation and this our garment of justification with the which we shall be clothe at the latter day must only come of his righteousness so as you present hear it must be give we by he for as of ourselves we can think a good thought so can we merit nothing but eternal death and when we have do all the good work we can we must think ourselves but improfitable servant as Christ himself say @ then the same voice to wit the voice of the angel that show i these thing say to i write and leave in record to all posterity happy be they that be call to the supper of the lamb marriage whereof thou thyself hear he speak parabolical for those who be call shall never again be cast off but be choose for ever and he say unto i these word of God be true which I bid thou write to leave to posterity that God himself have give this comfortable promise which I have special will thou to witness to thy brother because it will come to pass in the late day that this whore and heretical Babylon shall dissuade all her follower from trust this promise and so drive man to a uncertainty of their election 10 and I fall down at this angel foot to have adore he so all flesh be give of itself to adore some visible thing which be idolatry such be the corruption of our flesh if it be not hold up by grace from above but he do reprove i and say beware thou do it not for although I be a more excellent creature of God than thou be yet be I but thy fellow servant and so one of thy brother bear the testimony of JESUS in heaven to be his servant and creature as thou do in earth adore therefore God only for no creature must either be pray to or adore nor no mediation can come but by Christ only and think i not a god for prophesy thus unto thou for the witness of Christ be the spirit of prophecy for that gift be common to other aswell as to i and it be the same spirit of prophecy albeit not the same gift of it that foretell thing to come which give grace to all the elect to bear true and constant record of Christ 11 then I see thereafter the form of the day of judgement for I see the heaven open and lo a white horse come down from they of this white horse you hear in the first seal and he that sit upon he to wit Christ be call faithful and true for by give judgement he be now to perform his promise and he be also call he that just judge and fight for he be present to judge the world and to condemn perpetual all the reprobate 12 and his eye be like the flame of fire as you hear in the begin of this epistle and on his head be many diadem for now he be to reign eternal over all the kingdom of the earth as the elder do sing in the seven trumpet and he have a name write upon he which no man do know but himself for the mystery of his name of Redemptor be so profound as no creature be able to comprehend it by wisdom and therefore I hear himself say that no angel no not himself in so far as he be man do foreknow the day of his last come which shall be the fulfil of that mystery 13 and he be clothe with a garment dip in blood wherewith the garment of the soul of martyr be wash as you hear in the five seal and he be name the word of God as I do show you in the begin of my evangel 14 and the host of angel and saint in heaven follow he upon white horse clothe in white and pure linen whereof you hear already 15 and from his mouth come forth a sharp sword as you hear in the begin of this epistle that he may strike the gentile therewith for he shall rule they with a rod of iron as David say and he tread to wit give command and power to tread the lake or sea of the vine of the fury and wrath of God almighty as you hear in the seven trumpet 16 and he have upon his garment and upon his thigh as the strong part of his body this name write the king of king and lord of lord 17 and I see a angel stand in the sun that there he may be see public of all and that the whole world may take heed to that which he be to proclaim and he cry with a loud voice to all the fowl fly through the midst of heaven come and gather yourselves to the supper of the lord 18 to eat the flesh of king of tribune of mighty man of horse and of their rider in short come eat the flesh of all freeman and slave great and small this be to declare that the day of judgement be come wherein shall that destruction ensue signify by fowl eat their flesh because fowl use to eat the flesh of dead man unbury which shall overwhelm all sort of man except always these that be mark who be sundry time except before as you hear 19 then I see that beast to wit Babylon together with the king of the earth who take her part and their army gather together to make war with he that sit upon the white horse and with his army 20 but the beast be take together with the false prophet or false church which by her false miracle seduce the nation that do bear the character of the beast and adore his image as you hear before and they be both cast quick in the lake of fire burn with brimstone 21 and the rest be slay by the sword which come out of his mouth that sit upon the horse and the fowl be fill with their flesh for how soon Christ shall come to judgement then shall all the enemy of God be destroy and so full victory obtain of this battle whereof you hear in the six trumpet and six phial and shall hear far hereafter and chief Babylon and the false church shall be cast into hell because they merit double punishment for the abuse of man although they shall not also want their damnation that follow they as be signify by their slaughter with the sword of his mouth whereof you hear in the begin of this epistle and by the fowl eat their flesh as you present perceive chap. 20 argument the sum and recapitulation of all the former vision to wit the first estate of the church in all purity after Christ the heresy and special the popedom that follow the destruction thereof in their greatestrage the latter day the salvation of the elect and condemnation of all other the spirit of God have now show unto i the estate of the church militant with the special temptation and trouble of the same from the death of Christ to the consummation of the world and their joyful deliverance and victory at that time by the first six seal and next more ample by the seven seal wherein be the seven trumpet and three her great temptation and trouble more clear and at large by the vision of the woman persecute by the dragon and last the clear and ample description and damnation of Babylon that great persecuter the sorrow of the earth and joy of heaven therefore this vision now that you shall present hear be next show unto i to serve for a sum as it be and a short recapitulation of the whole prophecy so often reiterate before which be here divide in three part first the happy estate of Christ church though not in the eye of the world from his first come to a long time after as be declare by the first seal next the grievous trouble and temptation unto the which she shall be subject thereafter as be declare by the three and four seal and by the three four five and six blast of the trumpet and three the destruction of all her enemy her joyful deliverance and the consummation as be declare by the six seal the seven trumpet the seven phial and the come down of the white horse which in my last word before these you hear describe but special in this vision be declare the punishment at the latter day of the devil himself before the destruction only of his instrument be mention as you former hear the vision than be this @ I see a angel come down from heaven and he have the key of the bottomless pit and a great chain in his hand @ and he take the dragon to wit the ancient serpent who be the devil and Satan to wit the tempter and bind he for the space of a thousand year @ and do cast he in the bottomless pit and close he in there that it shall not be open that he may come forth and seduce the nation till the space of a thousand year be complete and pass for thereafter he must be loose for a short space @ then I see seat and person sit upon they and judgement or power of judge be give unto they and I also see the soul of they who be behead or otherwise put to death for the testimony of Christ and the word of God and adore not the beast nor take his image neither his character on their forehead nor on their hand these shall live and reign with Christ the space of the thousand year you hear @ but the rest of the dead shall not revive till the space of these year be complete this be the first resurrection @ bless and holy be he that be partaker of the first resurrection for over such the second death shall have no power but they shall be priest of God and Christ and shall reign with he for ever this be the first part of the division whereof I present tell you to wit Christ by his passion do bind the devil who before be rage in the world and close he in hell by the remove of the veil of blindness from the whole earth which remain so the space of a thousand year to wit a long space and all that time the devil remain bind and cast into hell by Christ who only have power of it so as in all that space the nation be not seduce for the efficacy of heresy be not yet in and the saint and church visible shall so increase albeit in the midst of persecution all this time and so retain the purity of the truth as by the glory of their constancy and patience in the time of their persecution they shall as it be reign over the earth and by their martyrdom be judge thereof for it be call Christ reign and the saint upon the earth when his word and true professor thereof shine visible therein as I have say and these be they who adore not the beast to wit they be the elect who be predestinate before all beginning to be preserve from all infection and heresy which be general represent by this part of they that the beast or Babylon shall raise and maintain as the great and most perilous that ever shall be raise by Satan and the honourable sit of the saint and soul of martyr be show to i to assure i that how soon the soul of any faithful man be part from the body it ascend immediate unto heaven there abide in all glory the rejoin again of his glorify body at the latter day coniunct to possess all glory in heaven eternal like as by the contrary the reprobate soul how soon it part from the body of the wicked go down immediate to hell there abide in all torment the knit again with his curse body at the latter day there joint to be subject to eternal pain neither be there any rest place by the way for any of they and the rest of the dead to wit all the wicked shall not be revive while this space be complete for the wicked shall neither during this space nor at any time thereafter taste of the regeneration which be the first resurrection and second birth as Christ say to Nicodemus and therefore as I say already bless and happy be they who be partaker of the first resurrection for the second death to wit hell shall have no power of they but they shall be priest of God and Christ and reign with he these thousand year to wit they shall eternal in heaven offer up that eucharistical sacrifice of praise to God and so be join in fellowship with the choose which be upon the earth in that aforesaid time this first part of this vision be begin already now follow the next part. @ and when these happy day be expire then shall the devil be loose out of his prison @ and he shall go forth with great liberty to seduce the nation which be in the four airth of the earth to wit he shall not only after the spread of many heresy cause a general blindness and defection but also make a great persecution upon the faithful church by gather gog and Magog to battle against they who number be like the sand of the sea to wit after innumerable trouble at last he shall gather to the great day of the battle of the lord of the which you hear in the six trumpet and six phial and last immediate before this vision gog and Magog to wit two great seat of monarchy and tyranny over the church who both at one time shall rise in the latter day and both at another time shall be destroy by the blast of Christ breath as you shall hear whereof the one be the avow and profess enemy of GOD and his CHRIST but the other be Babylon the hypocritical and most dangerous adversary of these two you hear in the six trumpet and so these two although pride and envy shall still keep a root malice betwixt they yet they shall both with innumerable force make war against the true church as Herod and Pilate do band themselves against Christ notwithstanding the particular dislike which be betwixt they it be these and their force that must fight against the saint at arma-geddon as you hear in the six phial and the special drawer on of this battle shall be the three frog who be the last vermin breed of the smoke of the bottomless pit as you also hear in the say phial @ these great force than go up upon the earth for the devil raise they out of the bottomless pit and they spread themselves upon the breadth of the earth so great be their number and compass the tent or dwelling of the saint and the holy city for they be prepare to invade the true church on all side and by all mean but the fire come down from heaven and devour they for God by his almighty power even when their power be great and nothing so like as a apparent root out of all the faithful do miraculous confound all the adversary of his church and now come in the three and last part of this vision to wit the description of the consummation 10 for I do see the devil who seduce these wicked cast into a lake of fire and brimstone to wit in hell out of the which he shall never come again where also the beast and the false prophet be as you hear before here now I see the devil punish eternal to my great comfort for trouble the church where before I see only his instrument punish as I say in the begin of this vision and he and his instrument shall be torment there day and night to wit incessant for ever and ever 11 then I see a great white throne and one sit thereupon in all glory and brightness to wit JESUS CHRIST now come from heaven to judge the earth and from his sight flee the earth and the heaven and their place be not find for the whole earth and much of the heaven shall be destroy and renew at his last come 12 and I see all the dead great and small stand in GOD his sight for than be the resurrection of the dead who at that time must be judge and the book be open to wit the counsel and secret of all man heart and another book to wit the book of life be open to the effect that all those who name be write into it to wit predestinate and elect for salvation before all beginning may there be select for eternal glory and the dead be judge out of these thing which be write in the book according to their work for as God be a spirit so judge he the thought of man and so by faith only justify he which notwithstanding be do according to his work because they as the fruit of faith can be separate from it and bear witness of the same to man in the earth 13 and the sea give up all the dead she have for all the dead must then rise as I have show already and death and hell give up all they have for not only the body but even the soul of the wicked shall be judge there and every one be judge according to his work as I present do show you 14 and hell and death be cast in the lake of fire which be the second death to wit hell and death shall then be close up for ever within themselves and shall never again come forth to trouble the saint for death which be the last enemy shall be abolish from holy jerusalem for ever 15 and whosoever name be not find write in the book of life be cast into the lake of fire for not only the public evil doer but even whosoever be not predestinate for salvation shall at that time be cast into hell for there be no midway but whosoever gather not with Christ he scatter as I show before chap. 21 argument a large and glorious description of the church triumphant in heaven and of all the member of that holy and eternal jerusalem now the spirit of God have by this last vision make a sum and recapitulation of all the former as you have hear he by this follow and last vision declare and glorious describe the reward of all they who constant persevere unto the end in the true service of God notwithstanding all the assault of Satan which you have hear dilate the reward be then to be eternal inheritor of holy jerusalem as you shall present hear @ for I see a new heaven and a new earth it be over this new heaven and new earth that the faithful shall reign king and priest for ever as you hear before and the first heaven and the first earth go away neither be the sea any more for all shall be burn with fire at the consummation which fire shall renew they and take away their corruption and mutablitie relieve they from the servitude of death to the liberty of the glory of the son of God who notwithstanding shall not dwell there but in heaven @ and even I John see the holy new city jerusalem come down from heaven make ready of God like a bride that be deck for her bridegroom for this holy church triumphant shall come down in all shine glory to meet Christ her husband when he shall have judge the world as you have hear before to be incorporate and join with he for ever @ and I hear a mighty voice from heaven say for confirmation of this happy conjunction loe the tabernacle of God and his dwell place be with man and he will now dwell with they for ever and they shall be his people and he shall be a god with they and their god @ and God shall wipe all tear from their eye for they shall feel no more any sorrow as you have often hear before and death shall be no more neither shall any sorrow cry or dolour ever be in that church triumphant for the first be go away and a hearer only but as a what glorious and eternal reward do abide all the faithful 10 and so he take i up in the spirit to a high and great mountain for it become well that so glorious a sight shall be show upon so eminent a place and there he do show i a great city to wit that holy jerusalem come down from heaven and from God as you hear before 11 and it have the glory of God in it and the light or brightness of it be like unto the glitter of a most precious stone yea even like the green jasper in flourish eternity and like the clear crystal in shine brightness 12 and this city have a great and high wall to hold out all they who have not the mark of the lamb as you shall hear after and to protect the citizen from all blast of trouble for all tear will then be wipe from their eye as you hear before and this city have also twelve gate and in they twelve angel and their name be write upon they which be the name of the twelve tribe of the son of Israel 13 and there be three gate towards the east three towards the west three towards the south and three towards the north to signify that out of all part and place of the world and whatsoever thy vocation be if thou call to God with a upright heart thou shall find that the entrance into the city be equal distribute about the same 14 and the wall of the city have twelve foundation whereupon be write the twelve name of the apostle of the lamb these twelve angel of the twelve gate and twelve foundation of the wall be the four and twenty elder of who you hear in the begin of this my epistle the twelve angel of the twelve gate be the twelve patriarch who be the first teacher of the way and so the guide to this holy jerusalem for by the law which they represent we must first begin to know the truth and to know ourselves and the twelve foundation be we the twelve apostle for upon our doctrine be that wall found which hedge in the saint in a eternal security and debar all other 15 and the angel who speak with i have a golden reed in his hand to measure therewith the city and the gate and the wall of the same thereby to signify the just proportion and symmetry that shall be among all the part of this holy city 16 and this city be foursquare because of the gate towards the four part of the earth to receive indifferent the comer out of any of they as you hear before and it be alike long and broad to signify the infinite bound thereof and he measure the city with his reed and it come to twelve thousand furlong this number also express the great bound of this city for it be here use for a number of perfection as sundry time before and this city be alike in length breadth and height for all the part of it be alike large 17 and the angel do measure the wall of it and it be a hundred and four and forty cubit of height this number be correspondent to the number of saint who be stand with the lamb on mount Zion as you hear before and the measure wherewith this be measure be the measure of the man which be the measure of the angel this city be measure with the measure of CHRIST God and man to teach we that he be only the architect of this spiritual city which he measure by his cubit and not by the cubit of any man 18 and the fabric of the wall of the city be compose of jasper to signify that the wall thereof shall stand eternal and the city itself be of pure gold and like to clear glass whereon no filth will remain 19 and the twelve foundation of the city be deck with all kind of precious stone the first foundation be of jasper the second of saphire the three of Chalcedonie the four of emerald 20 the five of a sardonix the six of a Sardius the seven of a chrysolite the eight of a beryl the nine of a topaz the ten of a the eleven of a hyacinth the twelve of a amethyst these twelve sundry stone one for excellency of our labour in the earth these twelve precious stone allude also to the twelve precious stone in Aaron breastplate 21 and the twelve gate be of twelve pearl and every gate of a sundry pearl this signify the like of the patriarch and the market place of the city be of pure gold and like the glister glass signify thereby as by a evident token that see the market place which be the common place of every town of this spiritual city be of so fine and bright stuff that no base and unclean thing shall be in any part thereof 22 and I see no temple in it for the lord God almighty even the lamb be the temple of it for no other shall be there wherein God must be praise but the person of Christ in who all the faithful shall be incorporate as I say before 23 and this city shall need no the glory thereof earthly but celestial and spiritual 24 and the gentile which be save shall walk in that light and the king of the earth shall bring their glory unto that city for all the faithful two thing one to consider our estate and other to conform our action according thereunto our estate be we be threefold besiege first spiritual by the heresy of the Antichrist second corporal and general as member of that church the which in the whole they persecute three all man shall be lawful arm spiritual and bodily to fight against the Antichrist and his upholder corporal and particular by this present army our action then conform to our estate be these first to call for help at God his hand next to assure we of the same see we have a sufficient warrant his constant promise express in his word three since with good conscience we may be in the tent of the saint and belove city stand in our defence encourage one another to use lawful resistance and concur or join one with another as warrior in one camp and citizen of one belove city for maintenance of the good cause God have clad we with and in defence of our liberty native country and life for since we see God have promise not only in the world to come but also in this world to give we victory over they let we in assurance hereof strong trust in our god cease to mistrust his promise and fall through incredulity or unbelief for then be we worthy of double punishment for the strong they wax and the near they come to their light the fast approach their wrack and the day of our delivery for kind and love true and constant careful and watchful mighty and revenge be he that promise it to who be praise and glory for ever amen a meditation upon the 25 26 27 28 and 29 verse of the 15 chap. of the first book of the chronicle of the king write by the most Christian king and sincere professor of the truth JAMES by the grace of God king of England France Scotland and Ireland defender of the Faith the text 25 so David and the elder of Israel and the captain of thousand go to bring up the ark of the covenant of the lord from the house of Obed-Edom with joy 26 and because that God help the levite that bear the ark of the covenant of the lord they offer seven bullock and seven ram 27 and David have on he a linen garment as all the levite that bear the ark and the singer and Chenaniah that have the chief charge of the singer and upon David be a linen ephod 28 thus all Israel bring up the ark of the lord covenant with shout and sound of cornet and with trumpet and with Cymbales make asound with viol and with harp 29 and when the ark of the covenant of the lord come into the city of David Michal the daughter of Saul look out at a window and see king David dance and play and she despise he in her heart the meditation as of late when great appearance of peril be by that foreign and godless fleet I take occasion by a text select for the purpose to exhort you to remain constant rest assure of a happy deliverance so now by the great mercy of God my speech have take a evident effect I can do no less of my careful duty than out of this place cite teach you what rest on your part to be do not of any opinion I have of my ability to instruct you but that these meditation of i may after my death remain to the posterity as a certain testimony of my upright and honest meaning in this so great and weighty a cause now I come to the matter David that godly king you see have no soon obtain victory over god and his enemy the philistine but his first action which follow be with concurrence of his whole estate to translate the ark of the lord covenant to his house in great triumph and gladness accompany with the sound of musical instrument and be so bring to the king house he himself dance and rejoice before it which thing Michal the daughter of Saul and his wife perceive she contemn and laugh at her husband in her mind this be the sum the method for better understanding whereof these head be to be open up in order and apply and first what cause move David to do this work second what person concur with David in do of this work three what be the action itself and form of do use in the same four the person of Michal and five her action the first part as to the first part zeal in David and experience of God kindness towards he move David to honour God the cause move David pass all other I note two one internal the other external the internal be a fervent and zealous mind in David full dispose to extol the glory of God that have call he to be king as he say himself the zeal of thy house it eat i up psal 69.9 but more large express in the 132. psalm compose at the same time while this work be a do the external be a notable victory new obtain by the power of God over and against the philistine old and pernicious enemy to the people of God express in the last part of the 14. chapter precede by this victory or cause external the internal cause and zeal in David be so double inflame that all thing set aside in this work only he will be occupy these be the two weighty cause move he whereof we may learn first that the chief virtue which shall be in a Christian prince and which the spirit of God always chief praise in he be a fervency and constant zeal to promote the glory of God that have honour he next that where this zeal be unfeigned God leave never that person without continual pour of his blessing on he thereby to stir up into he a double measure of zeal and thankfulness towards God the church ever trouble by man have a joyful end three that the church of God never want enemy and notable victory over they to assure they at all time of the constant kindness of God towards they even when as by the cross as a bitter medicine he cure their infirmity save they from gross sin and try their faith for we find plain in the scripture that no soon god himself choose Israel to be his people but assoon and ever thereafter as long as they remain he the devil so envy their prosperity as he hound out his instrument the nation at all time to trouble and war against they yet to the comfort of his church afflict and wrack of the afflicter in the end this first be practise by Pharaoh in Egypt and after their deliverance first by the ammonite and then by the philistine continual thereafter until the rise of the monarchy who every one do exercise themselves in the same labour but to note here the rage of all profane prince and nation which exercise their cruelty upon the church of God be superfluous and tedious in respect of that which I have set down in my former meditation wherefore I only go forward then in this as this be the continual behaviour of the nation towards Israel so it be most especial in the time of David and among the rest at this time here cite at what time have new invade Israel and be drive back they will yet assemble again in great multitude to war against the people of God and not content to defend their own country as the israelite do will needs come out of the same to pursue they and so spread themselves in the valley but David by God direction bring forth the people against they who fight and according to God promise overcome they only by the hand of God and not by their power as the place itself most plain do show so the church of God may be trouble but in trouble it can perish and the end of their trouble be the very wrack and destruction of God enemy the second part now follow second the person who do concur with David in this action three rank of person concur with David in this work the spirit note three rank of they in the first be the elder of Israel in the next be the captain over thousand in the three be the priest and levite of who summary I will speak these elder be substitute under David in the kingdom and as his hand in all part of the country minister justice and judgement to the king subject and they be of two sort magistrate in wall town who in the gate of the city execute judgement and chief in tribe and father of family who in the country do judge and minister judgement as the scripture report they be not unlike to two of the estate of our kingdom the baron and the burgess the captain over thousand be godly and valiant man who under the king do rule in time of war have the custody of the king person and fight his battle these be necessary officer for David who be appoint by victory a triumph indeed far surpass the egyptian pyramid the grecian trophy or yet the roman triumphal chariot as to the harmony and musical instrument that accompany this holy ark I trust no man be ignorant of the custom that be among the jew in praise God with all kind of instrument as David psalm innumerable time bear witness but in case some will demand wherefore the church of God shall more extraordinary rejoice at one time then at another see we be assure that all god action towards we be for our weal either spiritual or corporal suppose we can at every time comprehend it I answer that although I must confess that sentence to be most orthodox yet must I also confess that whensoever it shall please God to manifest by outward sign to the world as at one time more than another the great love to his church by some notable work for their deliverance we be then of duty bind in the high degree to praise God as well for confirm of the weak one among we as for stop of the mouth and dash of the proud wicked without we to make the glory of his name as far as in we lie to resound the manifold example of the saint of God through the whole scripture prove this more than evident beside the example of the prayer of sundry of the godly who for the glory of his name more than for their particular weal have pray he to give public testimony of his love towards they so do Moses joshua David Elias and innumerable other of the prophet and servant of God as to the dance of David dance play and such like action we know be of themselves indifferent and good or evil according to their use and the intention of the us and therefore be use at this time with a comely zeal for the set forth of God glory be not only to be bear with and excuse but even most high to be praise and commend although that Michal dispraise the same moreover it be to be mark that David in this do do nothing without the special motion of the spirit of God as a extraordinary work which so full possess his soul at this present that forgetful as it be of the action of his body he give his whole person over to be govern as it please he always seek in all the honour and glory of his god without respect the nature of the hypocrite and interior enemy of the church who although in their particular not concern religion there will be none in show more friendly to the godly than they yet how soon matter of religion or concern the honour of God come in hand o then be they no long able to contain or bridle their passion even as here Michal defend her husband even in the particular betwixt he and her own father but his dance before the ark to the honour of God she can no wise abide now thus far be say for the methodical open up of the text the application of the purpose to we it rest only to pursuer and we as Israel the defender of our native soil and country next have they not now at the last even like the philistine come out of their own soil to pursue we and spread themselves to that effect upon the great valley of our sea presumptuous threaten the destruction and wrack of we but three have not our victory be far more notable than that of Israel and have not the one be as well wrought by the hand of God as the other for as God by shake the top of the mulberry tree with his mighty wind put the philistine to flight have he not even in like manner by brangle with his mighty wind their timber castle scatter and shake they asunder to the wrack of a great part and confusion of the whole now that we may resemble Israel as well in the rest of this action what triumph rest we to make for the crown of this bless comedy even to bring among we the ark with all rejoice what be the ark of christian under grace but the lord jesus Christ who with joy we bring among we when as receive with sincerity and gladness the new testament in the blood of Christ our saviour in our heart we believe his promise and in word and deed we bear witness thereto before the whole world and walk so in the light as it become the son of the same this be the worthy triumph of our victory that we can make and although there will doubtless be many michal among we let we rejoice and praise God for the discovery of they assure ourselves they be never of we account all they to be against we that either rejoice at the prosperity of our enemy or rejoice not with we at our miraculous deliverance for all they that gather not with we they scatter and let we also diligent and wary try out these crafty michal for it be in that respect that Christ recommend unto we the wisdom of serpent not thereby to deceive and betray other no God forbid but to arm we against the deceit and treason of hypocrite that go about to trap we and lest that these great benefit which God have bestow upon we be turn through our unthankfulness into a great curse in serve for testimony at the latter day against we to the procure of our double stripe let we now to conclude bring in the ark among we in two respect before mention see we have already receive the gospel first by constant remain in the purity of the truth which be our most certain covenant of salvation in the only merit of our saviour and next let we so reform our defile life as become regenerate christian to the great glory of our god the utter deface of our adversary the wicked and our unspeakable comfort both here and also for ever in form of a dialogue divide into three book write by the high and mighty prince JAMES by the grace of GOD king of England Scotland France and Ireland defender of the faith etc.  the preface to the reader the fearful abound at this time in this country of these detestable slave of the devil the witch or enchanter have move i belove reader to dispatch in post this follow treatise of i not in any wise as i protest to serve for a show of my learning and engine but only move of conscience to press thereby so far as I can to resolve the doubt heart of many both that such assault of Satan be most certain practise and that the instrument thereof merit most severe to be punish against the damnable opinion of two principal in our age whereof the one call scot a Englishman be not ashamed in public print to deny that there can be such a thing as witchcraft and so maintain the old error of the sadducee in deny of spirit the other call Wierus a german physician set out a public apology for all these crafts-folke whereby procure for their impunity he plain betray himself to have be one of that profession and for to make this treatise the more pleasant and facile i have put it in form of a dialogue which I have divide into three book the first speak of Magie in general and necromancy in special the second of sorcery and witchcraft and the three contain a discourse of all these kind of spirit and spectre that appear and trouble person together with a conclusion of the whole work my intention in this labour be only to prove two thing as I have already say the one that such devilish art have be and be the other what exact trial and severe punishment they merit and therefore reason I what kind of thing be possible to be perform in these art and by what natural cause they may be not that I touch every particular thing of the devil power for that be infinite but only to speak scholasticke since this can be speak in our language i reason upon leave species and to be comprehend therein as for example speak of the power of in the first book and six chapter I say that they can sudden cause be bring unto they all kind of dainty dish by their familiar spirit since as a thief he delight to steal and as a spirit he can subtle and sudden enough transport the same now under this may be comprehend all particular depend thereupon such as the bring wine out of a wall as we have hear oft to have be practise and such other which particular be sufficient prove by the reason of the general and such like in the second book of witchcraft in special and five chapter i say and prove by diverse argument that witch can by the power of their master cure or cast on disease now by these same reason that prove their power by the devil of disease in general be aswell prove their power in special as of weaken the nature of some man to make they unable for woman and make it to abound in other more than the ordinary course of nature will permit and such like in all other particular sickness but one thing I will pray thou to observe in all these place where I reason upon the devil power which be the different end and scope that God as the first cause and the devil as his instrument and second cause shoot at in all these action of the devil as God hangman for where the devil intention in they be ever to perish either the soul or the body or both of they that he be so permit to deal with God by the contrary draw ever out of that evil glory to himself either by the wrack of the wicked in his justice or by the trial of the patient and amendment of the faithful be waken up with that rod of correction have thus declare unto thou then my full intention in this treatise thou will easy excuse I doubt not aswell my pretermit to declare the whole particular rite and secret of these unlawful art as also their infinite and wonderful practice as be neither of they pertinent to my purpose the reason whereof be give in the hinder end of the first chapter of the three book and who like to be curious in these thing he may read if he will hear of their practice Daemonomanie collect with great diligence then write with judgement together with their confession that have be at this time apprehend if he will know what have be the opinion of the ancient concern their power he shall see it well describe by Hyperius and Hemmingius two late german writer beside innumerable other neotericke theologue that write large upon that subject and if he will know what be the particular rite and curiosity of these black art which be both unnecessary and perilous he will find it in the four book of Cornelius Agrippa and in Wierus whom-of i speak and so wish my pain in this treatise belove reader to be effectual in arm all they that read the same against these above mention error and recommend my good will to thy friendly acceptation i bid thou hearty farewell JAMES r. x. daemonology in form of a dialogue first book argument the exord of the whole the description of Magie in special chap. i. argument prove by the scripture that these unlawful art in have be and may be put in practice PHILOMATHES and EPISTEMON reason the matter PHILOMATHES sure very glad to have meet with you this day for I be of opinion that you can better resolve i of some thing whereof I stand in great doubt nor any other whom-with I can have meet Epi in what I can that you like to spear at i I will willing and free tell my opinion and if I prove it not sufficient I be hearty content that a better reason carry it away then phi. what think you of these strange news which now only furnish purpose to all man at their meeting I mean of these witch Epi sure they be wonderful and I think so clear and plain confession in that purpose have never fall out in any age or country phi. no question if they be true but thereof the doctor doubt Epi what part of it doubt you of phi. even of all for aught I can yet perceive and namely that there be such a thing as witchcraft or witch and I will pray you to resolve i thereof if you may for I have reason with sundry in that matter and yet can never be satisfy therein Epi I shall with good will do the best I can but I think it the difficiller since you deny the thing itself in general for as it be say in the logic school always for that part that witchcraft and witch have be and be the former part be clear prove by the scripture and the last by daily experience and confession phi. I know you will allege i saul but that as appear will not make much for you Epi not only that place but diverse other but I marvel why that shall not make much for i phi. the reason be these first you may consider that Saul be trouble in spirit and have fast long before as the text testify and be come to a woman that be bruit to have such knowledge and that to inquire so important news he have so guilty a conscience for his heinous offence and special for that same unlawful curiosity and horrible defection and then the woman cry out upon the sudden in great admiration for the uncouth sight that she allege to have see discover he to be the king though disguise and deny by he before it be no wonder I say that his sense be thus distract he can not perceive she feign of her voice he be himself in another chalmer and see nothing next what can be or be raise the spirit of Samuel profane and against all theology the devil in his likeness as unappeirant that either god will permit he to come in the shape of his saint for then can never the prophet in those day have be sure what spirit speak to they in their vision or then that he can foretell what be to come thereafter for prophecy proceed only of GOD and the devil have no knowledge of thing to come Epi yet if you will mark the word of the text you will find clear that Saul see that apparition for give you that Saul be in another chalmer at the make of the circle and conjuration needful for that purpose as none of that craft will permit any other to behold at that time yet it be evident by the text that how soon that once that unclean spirit be full rise she call in upon Saul for it be say in the text that Saul know he to be Samuel which can not have be by the hear tell only of a old man with a mantle since there be many more old man dead in Israel nor Samuel and the common weigh of that whole country be mantil as to the next that it be not the spirit of Samuel I grant in the prove whereof you need not to insist since all christian of whatsoever religion agree upon that and none but either mere ignorant or necromancier or witch doubt thereof and that the devil be permit at sometime to put himself in the likeness of the saint it be plain in the scripture 1. cor. 11.14 where it be say that Satan can transform himself into a angel of light neither can that bring any inconvenience with the vision of the prophet since it be most certain that God will not permit he so to deceive his own but only such as first wilful deceive themselves by run unto he who God then suffer to fall in their own snare and just permit they to be illude with great efficacy of deceit because they will not believe the truth as Paul say and as to the devil foretell of thing to come it be true that he know not all thing future but yet that he know part the tragical event of this history declare it which the wit of woman can never have fore-spoken not that he have any prescience which be only proper to God or yet know any thing by look upon God as in a mirror as the good angel do he be for ever debar from the favourable presence and countenance of his creator but only by one of these two mean either as be worldly wise and teach by a continual experience ever since the creation judge by likelihood of thing to come according to the like that have pass before and the natural cause in respect of the vicissitude of all thing worldly or else by god employ of he in a turn and so foresee thereof as appear to have be in this 1. suppose they will to rid themselves out of the same phi. then this sin be a sin against the holy ghost Epi it be in some but not in all phi. how that be not all these that run direct to the devil in and heavenly science which be first use among the chaldee through their ignorance of the true divinity be esteem and repute among they as a principal virtue and therefore be name unjust with a honourable style which name the greek imitate general import all these kind of unlawful art and this word necromancy be a greek word compound of (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) which be to say the prophecy by the dead this last name be give to this black and unlawful science by the figure synecdoche because it be a principal part of that art to serve themselves with dead carcase in their divination phi. what difference be there betwixt this art and witchcraft Epi sure the difference vulgar put betwixt they be very merry and in a manner true for they say that the witch be servant only and slave to the devil but the necromancier be his master and commander phi. how can that be true that any man be special addict to his service can be his commander Epi yea they may be but it be only for it be not by hunte for phi. a very inaequitable contract forsooth but I pray you discourse unto i what be the effect and secret of that art Epi that be a over large field you give i yet I shall do my goodwill the most summarily that I can to run through the principal point thereof as there be two sort of folk that may be entice to this art to wit learned or unlearned so be there two mean which be the first steerer up and feeder of their curiosity thereby to make they to give themselves over to the same which two mean I call the devil school and his rudiment the learned have their curiosity waken up and feed by that which I call his school this be the astrology iudiciar for diverse man have attain to a great perfection in learning and yet remain over-bare alas of the spirit of regeneration and fruit thereof find all natural thing common aswell to the stupid pedant as unto they they assay to vindicate unto they a great name by not only know the course of thing heavenly but likewise to climb to the knowledge of thing to come thereby which at the first face appear lawful unto they in respect the ground thereof seem to proceed of natural cause only they be so allure thereby that find their practice to prove true in sundry thing they study to know the cause thereof and so mount from degree to degree upon the slippery and uncertain scale of curiosity they be at last entice that where lawful art or science fail to satisfy their restless mind even to seek to that black and unlawful science of Magie where find at the first that such diverse form of circle and conjuration right join thereunto will raise such diverse form of spirit to resolve they of their doubt and attribute the do thereof to the power inseparable tie or inherent in the circle and many word of God confuse wrap in they blind glory of themselves as if they have by their quickness of engine make a conquest of Pluto dominion and be become emperor over the stygian habitacle where in the mean time miserable wretch they be become in very deed bondslave to their mortal enemy and their knowledge for all that they presume thereof be nothing increase except in know evil and the horror of hell for punishment thereof that craft teach they many point of their craft and practicque be common betwixt the magician and they for they serve both one master although in diverse fashion and as I divide the necromancer into two sort learned and unlearned so must I deny they in other two rich and of better account poor and of base degree these two degree now of person that practise this craft answer to the passion in they which I tell you before the devil use as mean to entice they to his service for such of they as be in great misery and poverty he allure to follow he by promise unto they great riches and worldly commodity such as though rich yet burn in a desperate desire of revenge he allure they by promise to get their turn satisfy to their heart contentment it be to be note now that that old and crafty enemy of we assail none though touch with any of these two extremity except he first find a entresse ready for he either by the great ignorance of the person he deal with join with a evil life or else by their carelessness and contempt of God and find they in a utter despair for one of these two former cause that I have speak of he prepare the way by feed they crafty in their humour and fill they further and further with despair while he find the time proper to discover himself unto they at which time either upon their walk solitary in the field or else lie pansing in their bed but always without the company of any other he either by a voice or in likeness of a man inquire of they what trouble they and promise they a sudden and certain way of remedy upon condition on the other part that they follow his advice and do such thing as he will require of they their mind be prepare beforehand as I have already speak they easy agree unto that demand of he and sign set a other tryist where they may meet again at which time before he proceed any further with they he first persuade they to addict themselves to his service which be easy obtain he than discover what he be unto they make they to renounce their god and baptism direct and give they his mark upon some secret place of their body which remain soar unhealed while his next meeting with they and thereafter ever insensible howsoever it be nip or prick by any as be daily prove to give they a proof thereby that as in that do he can hurt and heal they so all their ill and well do thereafter must depend upon he and beside that the intolerable dolour that they feel in that place where he have mark they serve to waken they and not to let they rest while their next meeting again fear lest otherways they may either forget he be as new prentice and not well enough found yet in that fiendly folly or else remember of that horrible promise they make he at their last meeting they may skunner at the same and press to call it back at their three meeting he make a show to be careful to perform his promise either by teach they way how to get themselves revenge if they be of that sort or else by teach they lesson how by most vile and unlawful mean they may obtain gain and worldly commodity if they be of the other sort chap. 3 ARGV the witch action divide into two part the action proper to their own
