powder-traitor yet be they not join with they in treasonable course against their sovereign whereby all quiet mind papist be put out of despair and I give a good proof that I intend no persecution against they for conscience cause but only desire to be secure of they for civil obedience which for conscience cause they be bind to perform 3. but the devil can not have devise a more malicious trick for interrupt this so calm and clement a course than fall out by the send hither and publish a of the pope countermand all they of his profession to take this oath thereby sow new seed of jealousy between i and my popish subject by stir they up to disobey that lawful commandment of their sovereign which be ordain to be take of they as a pledge of their fidelity and so by their refusal of so just a charge to give i so great and just a ground for punishment of they without touch any matter of conscience throw themselves needless into one of these desperate strait either with the loss of their life and good to renounce their allegiance to their natural sovereign or else to procure the condemnation of their soul by renounce the catholic faith as he allege 4. and on the other part although disparity of religion the pope be head of the contrary part can permit no intelligence nor intercourse of messenger between i and the pope yet there be no denounce war between we he have by this action break the rule of common civility and justice between christian prince in thus condemn i unheard both by account i a persecutor which can be but imply by exhort the papist to endure martyrdom as likewise by so strait command all those of his profession in England to refuse the take of this oath thereby refuse to profess their natural obedience to i their sovereign for if he think himself my lawful judge wherefore have he condemn i unheard and if he have nothing to do with i and my government as indeed he have not why do he to meddle between i and my subject especial in matter that mere and only concern civil obedience and yet can in his great fury and avow quarrel against the late queen do no more injury unto she then he have in this case offer unto i without so much as a pretend or a allege cause for what difference there be between the command subject to rebel and lose they from their oath of allegiance as do and the command of subject not to obey in make profession of their oath of their dutiful allegiance as this pope have now do no man can easy discern 5. but to draw near unto his Brene wherein certain he have take more pain than he need by set down in the say the whole body of the oath at length whereas the only name of the title thereof may as well have serve for any answer he have make thereunto make that be the flat and general condemnation of the whole oath to serve for all his refutation therein have as well in this respect as in the former deal both undiscreet with i and injurious with his own catholic with i in not refute particular what special word he quarrel in that oath which if he have do it may have be that for the fatherly care I have not to put any of my subject to a needless extremity I may have be content in some sort to have reform or interpret those word with his own catholic for either if I have so do they have be thereby full ease in that business or at least if I will not have condescend to have alter any thing in the say oath yet will thereby some appearance or shadow of excuse have be leave unto they for refuse the same not as seem thereby to swerve from their obedience and allegiance unto i but only be stay from take the same upon the scrupulous tenderness of their conscience in regard of those particular word which the pope have note and condemn therein and now let we hear the word of his thunder pope Paulus the five to the english catholic well-beloved son the pope his first salutation and apostolical benediction the tribulation and calamity which you have continual sustain for the keep of the catholic faith have always afflict we with great grief of mind but for as much as we understand that at this time all thing be more grievous our affliction hereby be wonderful increase for we have hear how you be compel by most grievous punishment set before you to go to the church of heretic to frequent their assembly to be present at their sermon true we do undoubted believe that they which with so great constancy and fortitude have hitherto endure most cruel persecution and almost infinite misery that they may walk without spot in the law of the lord will never suffer themselves to be defile with the communion of those that have forsake the divine Law yet notwithstanding be compel by the zeal of our pastoral office and by our fatherly care which we do continual take for the salvation of your soul we be enforce to admonish and desire you that by no mean you come unto the church of the heretic or hear their sermon or communicate with they in their rite lest you incur the wrath of God for these thing may you not do without endamage the worship of God and your own salvation as likewise you can without most evident and grievous wrong of God honour bind yourselves by the oath which in like manner we have hear with very great grief of our heart be administer unto you of the tenor under-written viz. i a. b. do true and sincere acknowledge the oath profess testify and declare in my conscience before God and the world that our sovereign lord king JAMES be lawful king of this realm and of all other his majesty dominion and country and that the pope neither of himself nor by any authority of the church or sea of Rome or by any other mean with any other have any power or authority to depose the king or to dispose of any of his majesty kingdom or dominion or to authorize any foreign prince to invade or annoy he or his country or to discharge any of his subject of their allegiance and obedience to his majesty or to give licence or leave to any of they to bear arm raise tumult or to offer any violence or hurt to his majesty royal person state or government or to any of his majesty subject within his majesty dominion also I do swear from my heart that notwithstanding any declaration or sentence of excommunication or deprivation make or grant or to be make or grant by the pope or his successor or by any authority derive or pretend to be derive from he or his sea against the say king his heir or successor or any absolution of the say subject from their obedience I will bear faith and true allegiance to his majesty his heir and successor and he and they will defend to the uttermost of my power against all conspiracy and attempt whatsoever which shall be make against his or their person their crown and dignity by reason or colour of any such sentence or declaration or otherwise and will do my best endeavour to disclose and make know unto his majesty his heir and successor all treason and traitorous conspiracy which I shall know or hear of to be against he or any of they and I do further swear that I do from my heart abhor detest and abjure as impious and heretical this damnable doctrine and position that prince which be excommunicate or deprive by the pope may be depose or murder by their subject or any other whatsoever and I do believe and in conscience be resolve that neither the pope nor any person whatsoever have power to absolve i of this oath or any part thereof which I acknowledge by good and full authority to be lawful minister unto i and do renounce all pardon and dispensation to the contrary and all these thing I do plain and sincere acknowledge and swear according to these express word by i speak and according to the plain and common sense and understanding of the same word without any equivocation or mental evasion or secret reservation whatsoever and I do make this recognition and acknowledgement hearty willing and true upon the true faith of a Christian so help i god. which thing since they be thus it must evident appear unto you by the word themselves that such a oath can be take without hurt of the catholic faith and the salvation of your soul see it contain many thing which be flat contrary to faith and salvation wherefore we do admonish you that you do utter abstain from take this and the like oath which thing we do the more earnest require of you because we have experience of the constancy of your faith which be try like gold in the fire of perpetual tribulation we do well know that you will cheerful undergo all kind of cruel torment whatsoever yea and constant endure death itself rather than you will in any thing offend the majesty of god. and this our confidence be confirm by those thing which be daily report unto we of the singular virtue valour and fortitude which in these last time do no less shine in your martyr than it do in the first beginning of the church stand therefore your loin be gird about with verity and have on the breastplate of righteousness take the shield of faith be you strong in the lord and in the power of his might and let nothing hinder you he which will crown you and do in heaven behold your conflict will finish the good work which he have begin in you you know how he have promise his disciple that he will never leave they orphan for he be faithful which have promise hold fast therefore his correction that be be root and ground in charity whatsoever you do whatsoever you endeavour do it with one accord in simplicity of heart in meekness of spirit without murmur or doubt for by this do all man know that we be the disciple of CHRIST if we have love one to another which charity as it be very great to be desire of all faithful christian so certain be it altogether necessary for you most bless son for by this your charity the power of the devil be weaken who do so much assail you since that power of he be especial uphold by the contention and disagreement of our son we exhort you therefore by the bowel of our lord JESUS CHRIST by who love we be take out of the jaw of eternal death that above all thing you will have mutual charity among you sure pope Clement the eight of happy memory have give you most profitable precept of practise brotherly charity one to another in his letter in form of a to our well-beloved son m. George arch priest of the kingdom of England date the 5. day of the month of October 1602. put they therefore diligent in practice and be not hinder by any difficulty or doubtfulness we command you that you do exact observe the word of those letter and that you take and understand they simple as they sound and as they lie all power to interpret they otherwise be take away in the mean while we will never cease to pray to the father of mercy that he will with pity behold your affliction and your pain and that he will keep and defend you with his continual protection who we do gentle greet with our apostolical benediction date at Rome at s. mark under the signet of the fisherman the ten of the calends of October 1606. the second year of our popedom the answer to the first first the pope express herein his sorrow for that persecution which the catholic sustain for the faith sake wherein beside the main untruth whereby I be so injurious use I must ever avow and maintain as the truth be according to my own knowledge that the late queen of famous memory never punish any Papist for religion but that their own punishment be ever extort out of her hand against her will by their own misbehaviour which both the time and circumstance of her action will manifest make proof of for before his excommunication give she over for a prey and set her subject at liberty to rebel it be well know she never meddle with the blood or hard punishment of any catholic nor make any rigorous law against they and since that time who listen to compare with a indifferent eye the manifold intend invasion against her whole kingdom the foreign practice the internal public rebellion the private plot and machination poison murder and all sort of device daily set abroach and all these ware continual foster and foment from Rome together with the continual corrupt of her subject as well by temporal bribe as by fair and specious promise of eternal felicity and nothing but book upon book public set forth by her fugitive for approbation of so holy design who list I say with a indifferent eye to look on the one part upon those infinite and intolerable temptation and on the other part upon the just yet moderate punishment of a part of these heinous offender shall easy see that that bless defunct lady be as free from persecution as they shall free these hellish instrument from the honour of martyrdom 5. but now have sacrifice if I may so say to the mane of my late predecessor I may next with saint Paul just vindicate my own fame from those innumerable calumny spread against i in testify the truth of my behaviour towards the papist wherein I may true affirm that whatsoever be her just and merciful government over the papist in her time my government over they since have so far exceed she in mercy and clemency as not only the papist themselves grow to that height of pride in confidence of my mildness as they do direct expect and assure promise to themselves liberty of conscience and equality with other of my subject in all thing but even a number of the best and faithfully of my say subject be cast in great fear and amazement of my course and proceed ever prognosticate and just suspect that sour fruit to come of it which show itself clear in the powder-treason how many do I honour with knighthood of know and open recusant how indifferent do I give audience and access to both side bestow equal all favour and honour on both profession how free and continual access have all rank and degree of papist in my court and company and above all how frank and free do I free recusant of their ordinary payment beside it be cuident what strait order be give out of my own mouth to the judge to spare the execution of all priest notwithstanding their conviction join thereunto a gracious proclamation whereby all priest that be at liberty and not take may go out of the country by such a day my general pardon have be extend to all convict priest in prison whereupon they be set at liberty as good subject and all priest that be take after send over and set at liberty there but time and paper will fail i to make enumeration of all the benefit and favour that I bestow in general and particular upon papist in recount whereof every scrape of my pen will serve but for a blot of the pope ingratitude and injustice in mete i with so hard a measure for the same so as I think I have sufficient or at least with good reason wipe the * * etc. tear from the pope eye for complain upon such persecution who if he have be but politicke wise although he have have no respect to justice and verity will have in this complaint of he make a difference between my present time and the time of the late queen and so by his commend of my moderation in regard of former time may have have hope to have move i to have continue in the same clement course for it be a true say that allege kindness upon noble mind do ever work much and for the main untruth of any persecution in my time it can never be prove that any be or be put to death since I come to the crown for cause of conscience except that now this discharge give by the pope to all catholic to take their oath of allegiance to i be the cause of the due punishment of many which if it fall out to be let the blood light upon the pope head who be the only cause thereof as for the next point contain in his concern his discharge of all papist to come to our church or frequent our rite and ceremony I be not to meddle at this time with that matter because my errand now only be to publish to the world the injury and injustice do unto i in discharge my subject to make profession of their obedience unto i the intendment of this discourse now as to the point where the oath be quarrel it be set down in few but very weighty word to wit that it ought to be clear unto all catholic that this oath can be take with safety of the catholic faith and of their soul health since it contain many thing that be plain and direct contrary to their faith and salvation to this the old say father upon the philosopher may very fit be apply nay indeed for how the profession of the natural allegiance of subject to their prince can be direct opposite to the faith and salvation of soul be so far beyond my simple read in divinity as I must think it a strange and new assertion to proceed out of the mouth of that pretend general pastor of all christian soul I read indeed and not in one or two or three place of scripture that subject be bind to obey their prince for conscience sake whether they be good Augustine speak of julian say julian be a unbelieve emperor be he not a a oppressor and a idolater Christian soldier serve that unbelieve emperor when they come to the cause of CHRIST they will acknowledge no lord but he that be in heaven when he will have they to worship idol and to sacrifice they prefer GOD before he but when he say go forth to fight invade such a nation they present obey they distinguish their eternal lord from their temporal and yet be they subject even unto their temporal lord for his sake that be their eternal lord and master 1 1 Tertull. scap. Gregory write to mauritius about a certain law that a soldier shall not be receive into a monastery militia the almighty God say he hold he guilty that be not upright to the most excellent emperor in all thing that he do or speak and then call himself the unworthy servant of his godliness go on in the whole epistle to show the injustice of that law as he pretend and in the end conclude his epistle with these word I be subject to your command have cause the same law to be send through diverse part of your dominion and because the law itself do not agree to the law of the almighty God I have signify the same by my letter to your most excellent lordship so that on both part I have pay what I ought because I have yield obedience to the emperor and have not hold my peace in what I think for God now how great a contrariety there be betwixt this ancient pope action in obey a emperor by the publication of his decree which in his own conscience he think unlawful and this present pope prohibition to a king subject from obedience unto he in thing most lawful and mere temporal I remit it to the reader indifferency and answerable to the father speak the counsel in their decree as the council of 6 6 council Arelatense Carolo mag. can. 26. Arles submit the whole council to the emperor in these word these thing we have decree to be present to our lord the emperor beseech his clemency that if we have do less than we ought it may be supply by his wisdom if any thing otherwise then reason require it may be correct by his judgement if any thing be find fault with by we with reason it may be perfect by his aid with God favourable assistance but why shall I speak of Charles the great to who not one council but six several counsel Frankfurt Arles Tours chalons Ments and Rheims do whole submit themselves and not rather speak of the decree make against Nestorius and his follower may by his power have their full force and validity as appear manifest in the epistle of the general council of Ephesus write I also read that christsaid his 2 2 2 John 18 36. kingdom be not of this world bid give to 3 3 3 matt. 22.21 Cesar what be Caesar and to God what be god and I ever hold it for a infallible maxim in divinity that temporal obedience to a temporal magistrate do nothing repugn to matter of faith or salvation of soul but that ever temporal obedience be against faith and salvation of soul as in this be allege be never before hear nor read of in the christian church and therefore I will have wish the pope before he have set down this commandment to all papist here that since in he be the power by the infabillity of his spirit to make new article of faith when ever it shall please he he have first set it down for a article of faith before he have command all catholic to believe and obey it I will then conclude the answer to this point in a dilemma either it be lawful to obey the sovereign in temporal thing question or not if it be lawful as I never hear nor read it doubt of then why be the pope so unjust and so cruel towards his own catholic as to command they to disobey their sovereign lawful commandment if it be unlawful why have he neither express any one cause or reason thereof nor yet will give they leave nay rather he shall command and persuade they in plain term not to live under a king who unto they ought no obedience and as for the vehement exhortation unto they to persevere in constancy answer to the pope exhortation and to suffer martyrdom and all tribulation for this cause it require no other answer then only this that if the ground be good whereupon he have command they to stand than exhortation to constancy be necessary but if the ground be unjust and naught as indeed it be and I have in part already prove then this exhortation of he can work no other effect then to make he guilty of the blood of so many of his sheep who he do thus wilful cast away not only to the needless loss of their life and ruin of their family but even to the lay on of a perpetual slander upon all papist as if no zealous Papist can be a true subject to his prince and that the profession of that religion and the temporal obedience to the civil magistrate be two thing repugnant and incompatible in themselves but evil information and untrue report which be carry so far as between this and Rome can but increase by the way may have abuse the pope and make he dispatch this so rash for that great city queen of the world and as themselves confess 1 1 Eusebius oecumenius and Leo hold that by Babylon in 1. pet. 5.13 Rome be mean as the rhemist themselves confess the pope or at the far a thing hasty do upon wrong information as be before say of which opinion be not only the simple sort of papist but even some among they of best account both for learning and experience whereof the archpriest himself be one but for solve of this objection the pope himself have take new pain by send forth a second only for give faith and confirmation to the former that whereas before his sin may have be think to have proceed from rashness and misinformation he will now wilful and willing double the same whereof the copy follow the second to our belove son the english catholic Paulus p. P.V tus belove son salutation and apostolical benediction it be report unto we that there be find certain among you who when as we have sufficient declare by our letter date the last year on the ten of the calends of October in the form of a that you can with safe conscience take the oath which be then require of you and when as we have further strait command you that by no mean you shall take it yet there be some I say among you which dare now affirm that such letter concern the forbid of the oath be not write of our own accord or of our own proper will but rather for the respect and at the instigation of other man and for that cause the same man do go about to persuade you that our command in the say letter be not to be regard sure this news do trouble we and that so much the more because have have experience of your obedience most dear belove son who to the end you may obey this holy sea have godly and valiant contemn your riches wealth honour liberty yea and life itself we shall never have suspect that the truth of our apostolic letter can once be call into question among you that by this pretence you may exempt yourselves from our commandment but we do herein perceive the subtlety and craft of the enemy of man salvation and we do attribute this your backwardness rather to he then to your own will and for this cause we have think good to write the second time unto you and to signify unto you again that our apostolic letter date the last year on the ten of the calends of October concern the prohibition of the oath be write not only upon our proper motion and of our certain knowledge but also after long and weighty deliberation use concern all those thing which be contain in they and that for that cause you be bind full to observe they reject all interpretation persuade to the contrary and this be our mere pure and perfect will be always careful of your salvation and always mind those thing which be most profitable unto you and we do pray without cease that he that have appoint our lowliness to the keep of the flock of Christ will enlighten our thought and our counsel who we do also continual desire that he will increase in you our belove son faith constancy and mutual charity and peace one to another all who we do most love bless with all charitable affection date at Rome at saint mark under the signet of the fisherman the x. of the calends of September 1607. the three year of our popedom the answer to the second now for this I may just reflect his own phrase upon he in term it to be the craft of the devil for if the devil have study a thousand year for to find out a mischief for our catholic hear he have find it in this that now when many catholic have take their oath and some priest also yea the archpriest himself without compunction or stick they shall not now only be bind to refuse the profession of their natural allegiance to their sovereign which may yet have be some way colour upon diverse scruple conceive upon the word of the oath but they must now renounce and forswear their profession of obedience already swear and so must as it be at the three instance forswear their former two oath first close swear by their birth in their natural allegiance and next clear confirm by this oath which do nothing but express the same so as no man can now hold the faith or procure the salvation of his soul in ENGLAND that must not abjure and renounce his bear and swear allegiance to his natural sovereign and yet it be not sufficient to ratify the last year by a new one come forth this year but that not only every year but every month may produce a new monster the great and famous writer of the controversy the late un-iesuited cardinal Bellarmine must add his talon to this good work by blow the bellows of sedition and sharpen the spur to rebellion by send such a letter of he to the archpriest here as it be a wonder how passion and a ambitious desire of maintain that monarchy shall charm the wit of so famous learn a man the copy whereof here follow to the very reverend Mr. GEORGE blackwell archpriest of the english ROBERT Bellarmine cardinal of the holy church of Rome greeting reverend sir and brother in CHRIST it be almost forty year since we do see one the other but yet I have never be unmindful of our ancient acquaintance neither have I cease see I can do you no other good to commend your labour most painful in the lord vineyard in my prayer to God and I doubt not but that I have live all this while in your memory and have have some place in your prayer at the lord altar so therefore even unto this time we have abide as s. John speak in the mutual love one of the other not by word or letter but in deed and truth but late message which be bring unto we within these few day of your bond and imprisonment have enforce i to break off this silence which message although it seem heavy in regard of the loss which that church have receive by their be thus deprive of the comfort of your pastoral function among they yet withal it seem joyous because you draw near unto the glory of martyrdom than the which gift of God there be none more happy that you who have feed your flock so many year with the word and doctrine shall now feed it more glorious by the example of your patience but another heavy tiding do not a little disquiet and almost take away this joy which immediate follow of the adversary assault and peradventure of the slip and fall of your constancy in refuse a unlawful oath neither true most dear brother can that oath therefore be lawful because it be offer in sort temper and modify for you know that those kind of modification be nothing else but sleight and subtlety of Satan that the catholic faith touch the primacy of the sea apostolic may either secret or open be shoot at for the which faith so many worthy martyr even in that very England itself have resist unto blood for most certain it be that in whatsoever word the oath be conceive by the adversary of the faith in that kingdom it tend to this end that the authority of the head of the church in England may be transfer from the successor of s. Peter to the successor of king Henry the eight for that which be pretend of the danger of the king life if the high priest shall have the same power in England which he have in all other christian kingdom it be altogether idle as all that have any understanding may easy perceive for it be never hear of from the church infancy until this day that ever any pope do command that any prince though a heretic though a ethnic though a persecutor shall be murder or do approve of the fact when it be do by any other and why I pray you do only the king of England fear that which none of all other the prince in Christendom either do fear or ever do fear but as I say these vain pretext be but the trap and stratagem of Satan of which kind I can produce not a few out of ancient story if I go about to write a book and not a epistle one only for example sake I will call to your memory s. gregorius nazianzenus in his first oration against julian the emperor report that he the more easy to beguile the simple christian do insert the image of the false god into the picture of the emperor which the roman do use to bow down unto with a civil kind of reverence so that no man can do reverence to the emperor picture but withal he must adore the image of the false god whereupon it come to pass that many be deceive and if there be any that find out the emperor craft and refuse to worship his picture those be most grievous punish as man that have contemn the emperor in his image some such like thing i think I see in the oath that be offer to you which be so crafty compose that no man can detest treason against the king and make profession of his civil subjection but he must be constrame perfidious to deny the primacy of the apostolic Sea but the servant of Christ and especial the chief priest of the lord aught to be so far from take a unlawful oath where they may endamage the faith that they ought to beware that they give not the least suspicion of dissimulation that they have take it lest they may seem to have leave any example of prevarication to faithful people which thing that worthy Eleazar do most notable perform who will neither eat swine flesh nor so much as fain to have eat it although he see the great torment that do hang over his head lest as himself speak in the second book of the maccabee many young man may be bring through that simulation to prevaricate with the law neither do Basil the great by his example which be more fit for our purpose carry himself less worthy towards Valens the emperor for as Theodoret write in his history when the deputy of that heretical emperor do persuade saint Basil that he will not resist the emperor for a little subtlety of a few point of doctrine that most holy and prudent man make answer that it be not to be endure that the least syllable of God word shall be corrupt but rather all kind of torment be to be embrace for the maintenance of the truth thereof now I suppose that there want not among you who say that they be but subtlety of opinion that be contain in the oath that be offer to the catholic and that you be not to against the king authority for such a little matter but there be not want also among you holy man like unto Basil the great which will open avow that the very lest syllable of God divine truth be not to be corrupt though many torment be to be endure and death itself set before you among who it be meet that you shall be one or rather the standard bearer and general to the rest and whatsoever have be the cause that your constancy have quail whether it be the suddenness of your apprehension or the bitterness of your persecution or the imbecility of your old age yet we trust in the goodness of God and in your own long continue virtue that it will come to pass that as you seem in some part to have imitate the fall of Peter and marcellinus so you shall happy imitate their valour in recover your strength and maintain the truth for if you will diligent weigh the whole matter with yourself true you shall see it be no small matter that be call in question by this oath but one of the principal head of our faith and foundation of catholic religion for hear what your apostle saint Gregory the great have write in his 24. epistle of his 11. book let not the reverence due to the apostolic sea be trouble by any man presumption for then the state of the member do remain entire when the head of the faith be not bruise by any injury therefore by saint Gregory testimony when they be busy about disturb or diminish or take away of the primacy of the apostolic sea then be they busy about cut off the very head of the faith and dissolve of the state of the whole body and of all the member which self same thing s. le^^^^th ^^th confirm in his three sermon of his assumption to the popedom when he say our lord have a special care of Peter and pray proper for Peter faith as though the state of other be more stable when their prince mind be not to be overcome whereupon himself in his epistle to the bishop of the province of Vienna do not doubt to affirm that he be not partaker of the divine mystery that dare depart from the solidity of Peter who also say that who think the primacy to be deny to that sea he can in no sort lessen the authority of it but by be puff up with the spirit of his own pride do cast himself headlong into hell these and many other of this kind I be very sure be most familiar to you who beside many other book have diligent read over the visible monarchy of your own sander a most diligent writer and one who have worthy deserve of the church of England neither can you be ignorant that these most holy and learned man John bishop of Rochester and tho. Moor within our memory for this one most weighty head of doctrine lead the way to martyrdom to many other to the exceed glory of the english nation but I will put you in remembrance that you shall take heart and consider the weightiness of the cause not to trust too much to your own judgement neither be wise above that be meet to be wise and if peradventure your fall have proceed not upon want of consideration but through human infirmity and for fear of punishment and imprisonment yet do not prefer a temporal liberty to the liberty of the glory of the son of God neither for escape a light and momentany tribulation lose a eternal weight of glory which tribulation itself do work in you you have fight a good fight a long time you have well-near finish your course so many year have you keep the faith do not therefore lose the reward of such labour do not deprive yourself of that crown of righteousness which so long ago be prepare for you do not make the face of so many you both brother and child ashamed upon you at this time be fix the eye of all the church yea also you be make a spectacle to the world to angel to man do not so carry yourself in this your last act that you leave nothing but lament to your friend and joy to your enemy but rather on the contrary which we assure hope and for which we continual pour forth prayer to God display glorious the banner of faith and make to rejoice the church which you have make heavy so shall you not only merit pardon at God hand but a crown farewell quite you like a man and let your heart be strengthen from Rome the 28. day of September 1607. your very reverendships brother and servant in Christ Robert Bellarmine cardinal the answer to the cardinal letter and now that I be to enter into the field against he by refute his letter I must first use this protestation that no desire of vainglory by match with so learned a man make i to undertake this task but only the care and conscience I have that such smooth Circe charm and guild pill as full of exterior eloquence as of inward untrewth may not have that public passage through the world without a answer whereby my reputation may unjust be darken by such cloudy and foggy mist of untrewth and false imputation the heart of unstaid and simple man be mislead and the truth itself smother but before I come to the particular answer of this letter a great mistake of the state of the question and case in hand I must here desire the world to wonder with i at the commit of so gross a error by so learned a man as that he shall have pain himself to have set down so elaborate a letter for the refutation of a quite mistake question for it appear that our english fugitive of who inward society with he he so great vaunt have so fast hammer in his head the oath of supremacy which have ever be so great a scar unto they as he think by his letter to have refute the last oath have in place thereof only pay the oath of supremacy which be most in his head as a man that be earnest carry in his thought upon another matter than he be present in do will often name the matter or person he be think of in place of the other thing he have at that time in hand for as the oath of supremacy be devise for put a difference between papist and they of our profession so be this oath the difference between the oath of supremacy and this of allegiance which he will seem to impugn ordain for make a difference between the civil obedient papist and the perverse disciple of the powder-treason yet do all his letter run upon a inuective against the compulsion of catholic to deny the authority of s. Peter successor and in place thereof to acknowledge the successor of king Henry the eight for in k. Henry the eight time be the oath of supremacy first make by he be Thomas Moor and Roffensis put to death partly for refuse of it from his time till now have all the prince of this land profess this religion successive in effect maintain the same and in that oath only be contain the king absolute power to be judge over all person aswell civil as ecclesiastical exclude all foreign power and potentate to be judge within his dominion whereas this last make oath contain no such matter only meddle with the civil obedience of subject to their sovereign in mere temporal cause and that it may the better appear that whereas by name he seem to condemn the last oath yet indeed his whole letter run upon nothing but upon the condemnation of the oath of supremacy I have here think good to set down the say oath leave it then to the discretion of every indifferent reader to judge whether he do not in substance only answer to the oath of supremacy but that he give the child a wrong name i a b. do utter testify and declare in my conscience that the king highness be the only supreme governor of this realm and all other his highness dominion and country aswell in all spiritual or ecclesiastical thing or cause as temporal and that no foreign prince person prelate state or potentate have or aught to have any jurisdiction power superiority preeminence or authority ecclesiastical or spiritual within this realm and therefore I do utter renounce and forsake all foreign jurisdiction power superiority and authority and do promise that from henceforth I shall bear faith and true allegiance to the king highness his heir and lawful successor and to my power shall assist and defend all jurisdiction privilege preeminence and authority grant or belong to the king highness his heir and successor or unite and annex to the imperial crown of the realm so help i God and by the content of this book and that the injustice as well as the error of his gross mistake in this point may yet be more clear discover I have also think good to insert here immediate after the oath of supremacy the contrary conclusion to all the point and article whereof this other late oath do consist whereby it may appear what unreasonable and rebellious point he will drive my subject unto by refuse the whole body of that oath as it be conceive for he that shall refuse to take this oath must of necessity hold all or some of these proposition follow that I king JAMES be not the lawful king of this kingdom and of all other my dominion that the pope by his own authority may depose i if not by his own authority yet by some other authority of the church or of the sea of Rome if not by some other authority of the church and sea of Rome yet by other mean with other help he may depose i that the pope may dispose of my kingdom and dominion that the pope may give authority to some foreign prince to invade my dominion that the pope may discharge my subject of their allegiance and obedience to i that the pope may give licence to one or more of my subject to bear arm against i that the pope may give leave to my subject to offer violence to my person or to my government or to some of my subject that if the pope shall by sentence excommunicate or depose i my subject be not to bear faith and allegiance to i if the pope shall by sentence excommunicate or depose i my subject be not bind to defend with all their power my person and crown if the pope shall give out any sentence of excommunication or deprivation against i my subject by reason o^ that sentence be not bind to reveal all conspiracy and treason against i which shall come to their hear and knowledge that it be not heretical and detestable to hold that prince be excommunicate by the pope may be either depose or kill by their subject or any other that the pope have power to absolve my subject from this oath or from some part thereof that this oath be not administer to my subject by a full and lawful authority that this oath be to be take with equivocation mental evasion or secret reservation and not with the heart and good will sincere in the true faith of a christian man these be the true and natural branch of the body of this oath the affirmative of all which negative touch the pretend council of Lateran see Plat. in Innocen 3 do neither concern in any case the pope supremacy in spiritual cause nor yet be ever conclude and define by any complete general council to belong to the pope authority and their own school doctor be at irreconciliable odds and jar about they and that the world may yet far see we and the whole state set down of this oath the oath of allegiance confirm by the authority of ancient counsel do not proceed from any new invention of our own but as it be warrant by the word of GOD so do it take the example from a oath of allegiance decree a thousand year ago which a famous council then together with diverse other counsel be so far from condemn as the pope now have do this oath as I have think good to set down their own word here in that purpose whereby it may appear that I crave nothing now of my subject in this oath which be not express and careful command then by the counsel to be obey without exception of person nay not in the very particular point of equivocation the ancient counsel provide for equivocation the difference between the ancient counsel and the pope counsel of the catholic which I in this oath be so careful to have eschew but you shall here see the say counsel in their decree as careful to provide for the eschew of the same so as almost every point of that action and this of we shall be find to have relation and agreeance one with the other save only in this that those old counsel be careful and strait in command the take of the same whereas by the contrary he that now vaunt himself to be head of all counsel be as careful and strait in the prohibition of all man from the take of this oath of allegiance the word of the council be these hear our sentence whosoever of we Concil. Tolet. 4 can 47. 633. or of all the people throughout all Spain shall go about by any mean of conspiracy or practice to violate the oath of his fidelity which he have take for the preservation of his country or of the king life or who shall attempt to put violent hand upon the king or to deprive he of his kingly power or that by tyrannical presumption will usurp the sovereignty of the kingdom let he be accurse in the sight of God the father and of his angel and let he be make and declare a stranger from the catholic church which he have profane by his perjury and a alien from the company of all christian people together with all the complice of his impiety because it behoove all those that be guilty of the like offence to under-lie the like punishment which sentence be three several time together and almost in the same word repeat in the same canon after this the synod desire that this sentence of they now this three time rehearse may be confirm by the voice and consent of all that be present then the whole clergy and people answer whosoever shall carry himself presumptuous against this your definitive sentence let they be anathema that be let they be utter destroy at the lord come and let they and their complice have their portion with judas Iscarioth amen and in the five 1 1 Concil. one point save one with our purpose which be that julian be a emperor and I a King first julian be a one that have renounce the whole christian faith which he have once profess and become a ethnic again or rather a atheist whereas I be a Christian who never change that religion that I drink in with my milk nor ever I thank GOD be ashamed of my profession julian deal against christian only for the profession of Christ cause I deal in this cause with my subject only to make a distinction between true subject and falese-hearted traitor julians end be the overthrow of the christian my only end be to maintain christianity in a peaceable government julians drift be to make they commit idolatry my purpose be to cause my subject to make open profession of their natural allegiance and civil obedience julians mean whereby he go about it be by craft and ensnare they before they be aware my course in this be plain clear and void of all obscurity never refuse leave to any that be require to image but as well I as all the subject of my profession be so far from guilt in this point as we be count heretic by you because we will not commit idolatry so as in the main point of all be the great contrariety for julian persecute the christian because they will not commit idolatry and you count i a persecutor because I will not admit idolatry so as to conclude this point this old sentence may well be apply to Bellarmine in use so unapt a similitude and therefore his uncharitable conclusion do not right follow that it seem unto he that some such thing shall be subtle or fraudulent include scripture and some be never apply but to CHRIST in that sense as he apply it have be better to have be leave out of so holy and mortify a letter to conclude then this present discourse I hearty wish all indifferent reader of the breve and letter not to judge by the speciousness of the word but by the weight of the matter not look to that which be strong allege but judicious to consider what be just prove and for all my own good and natural subject that their heart may remain establish in the truth that these foreign enticement may not seduce they from their natal and natural duty and that all aswell stranger as natural subject to who eye this discourse shall come may wise and unpartial judge of the verity as it be naked here set down for clear these mist and cloud of calumny which be injust heap upon i for which end only I hearty pray the courteous reader to be persuade that I take occasion to publish this discourse a premonition to all most mighty monarch king free prince and states of Christendom to the most sacred and invincible prince rodolphe the 2 by God clemency elect emperor of the roman king of Germany Hungary Boheme DALMATIE SCLAVONIE etc. archduke of AUSTRIA duke of Burgundy stiria Carinthia CARNIOLA and WIRTEMBERG etc. earl of TYROLIS etc. and to all other right high and mighty king and right excellent free prince and state of Christendom our love brother cousin ally confederate and friend JAMES by the grace of GOD king of great Britain france and IRELAND professor maintainer and defender of the true Christian catholic and apostolic faith profess by the ancient and primitive church and seal with the blood of so many holy bishop and other faithful crown with the glory of martyrdom wish everlasting felicity in CHRIST our saviour to you most sacred and invincible emperor right high and mighty king right excellent free prince and state my love brother and cousin to you I say as of right belong do I consecrate and direct this warn of i or rather preamble to my reprint apology for the oath of allegiance for the cause be general and concern the authority and privilege of king in general and all supereminent temporal power and if in whatsoever society or corporation of man either in corporation of city or in the corporation of any mechanic craft or handie-worke every man be careful to maintain the privilege of that society whereunto he be swear nay they will rather cluster all in one make it a common cause expose themselves to all sort of peril then suffer the least breach in their liberty if those of the base sort of people I say be so curious and zealous for the preservation of their common privilege and liberty as if the mean among they be touch in any such point they think it concern they all then what shall we do in such a case who GOD have place in the high throne upon earth make his lieutenant and vicegerent and even seat we upon his own throne to execute his judgement the consideration hereof have now move i to a case unto you which do not so near touch i in my particular as it do open a breach against our authority I speak in the plural of all king and privilege in general and since not only all rank and sort of people in all nation do inviolable observe this maxim but even the civil law by which the great part of Christendom be govern do give they a interest how much more than have you interest in this cause not be or to you but with you and indeed you all or at lest and since this cause be common to we all both the civil law and the municipal law of all nation permit and warn they that have a common interest to concur in one for the defence of their common cause yea common sense teach we with the poet a wake then while it be time and suffer not by your long sleep the string of your authority to be cut in and one and one to your general ruin which by your unite force will rather make a strong rope for the enemy to hang himself in with Achitophel then that he shall ever be able to break it as for this apology of i it be true that I think good to set it first out without put my name unto it but never so as I think to deny it remember well my own word but take out of the scripture in the begin of the preface to the reader in my (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) that nothing be so bid which shall not be open etc. promise there which with GOD his grace I shall ever perform never to do that in secret which I shall need to be ashamed of when it shall come to be proclaim in public in deed I think it fit for two respect that this my apology shall first visit the world without have my name write in the forehead thereof first because of the matter and next of the person that I meddle with the matter it be a treatise which I be to write contain reason and discourse in divinity for the defence of the oath of allegiance and refutation of the condemner thereof I think it not comely for one of my place to put my name to book concern scholastic disputation who calling be to set forth decree in the imperative mood for I think myself as good a man as the pope by his reverence for who these my answerer make the like excuse for that his breve be so summary without yield any reason unto they my next reason be the respect of the person who with I meddle wherein although I short answer the pope breve yet the point I most labour be the refutation of Bellarmine letter I be never the man I confess that can think a cardinal a meet match for a king especial have many hundred thousand of my subject of as good birth as he as for his church dignity his cardinalship I mean I know not how to rank or value it either by the warrant of God his word or by the ordinance of emperor or king it be indeed only a new papal erection tolerate by the sleep connivance of our predecessor I mean still by the plural of king but notwithstanding of this my forbear to put my name unto it some ambassador of some of you my love brother and cousin who this cause do neerely concern can witness that I make present of some of those book at their first print unto they and that avowed in my own name as also the english paragraphist or rather perverse Pamphleter parson since all his description must run upon a p. have true observe that my arm be affix in the frontispiece thereof which use not to be in book of other man do whereby his malice in pretend his ignorane that he may pay i the sound be the more inexcusable but now that I find my spare to put my name unto it have not procure my spare by these answerer who have neither spare my person direct in name i nor indirect by rail upon the author of the book it be now high time for i no long to conceal nor disavow myself as if I be ashamed of my own deed and therefore that you may the better understand the nature of the cause I will begin at the first ground thereof the never enough wonder at and abhor powder-treason though the repetition thereof grieve I know the gentle heart jesuite parson this treason I say be not only intend a against i and my posterity but even against the whole house of parliament plot only by papist and they only lead thereto by a preposterous zeal for the advancement of their religion some of they continue so obstinate that even at their death they will not acknowledge their fault but in their last word immediate before the expire of their breath refuse to condemn themselves and crave pardon for their deed except the romish church shall first condemn it and soon after it be discover that a great number of my popish subject of all rank and sex both man and woman as well within as without the country have a confuse notion and a obscure knowledge that some great thing be to be do in that parliament for the weal of the church although for secrecy cause they be not acquaint with the particular certain form of prayer have likewise be set down and use for the good success of that great errand add hereunto that diverse time and from diverse priest the archtraitour themselves receive the sacrament for confirmation of their heart and observation of secrecy some of the principal jesuite likewise be find guilty of the foreknowledge of the treason itself of which number some flee from their trial other be apprehend as holy garnet himself and owldcorne be and just execute upon their own plain confession of their guilt if this treason now clad with these circumstance do not minister a just occasion to that parliament house who they think to have destroy courageous and zealous at their next sit down to use all mean of trial whether any more of that mind be yet leave in the country I leave it to you to judge who God have appoint his high depute judge upon earth and among other thing for this purpose this oath of allegiance so unjust impugn be then devise and enact and in case any sharp law be then make against the papist that be not obedient to the former law of the country if you will consider the time place and person it will be think no wonder see that occasion do so just exasperate they to make severe law than otherwise they will have do the time I say be the very next sit down of the parliament after the discovery of that abominable treason the place be the same where they shall all have be blow up and so bring it fresh to their memory again the person be the very parliament man who they think to have destroy and yet so far have both my heart and government be from any bitterness as almost never one of those sharp addition to the former law have ever yet be put in execution and that you may yet know further for the more convince these libeler of wilful malice who impudent affirm that this oath of allegiance be devise for deceive and entrap of papist in point of conscience the truth be that the low house of parliament at the first frame of this oath make it to contain that the pope have no power to excommunicate i which I cause they to reform only make it to conclude that no excommunication of the pope can warrant my subject to practice against my person or state deny the deposition of king to be in the pope lawful power as indeed I take any such temporal violence to be far without the limit of such a spiritual censure as excommunication be so careful be I that nothing shall be contain in this oath except the profession of natural allegiance and civil and temporal obedience with a promise to resist to all contrary uncivil violence this oath now ground upon so great and just a occasion set forth in so reasonable term and ordain only for make of a true distinction between papist of quiet disposition and in all other thing good subject and such other papist as in their heart maintain the like violent bloody maxim that the powder-traitour do this oath I say be publish and put in practice breed such evil blood in the pope head and his clergy as come forth prohibit all catholic from take the same as a thing clean contrary to the catholic faith and that the take thereof can stand with the salvation of their soul there come likewise a letter of cardinal Bellarmine to blackwell to the same purpose but discourse more at length upon the say oath whereupon after I have enter in consideration of their unjust impugn that so just and lawful a oath and fear that by their untrue calumny and sophistry the heart of a number of the most simple and ignorant of my people shall be mislead under that fair and deceitful cloak of conscience I think good to set forth a apology for the say oath wherein I prove that as this oath contain nothing but matter of civil and temporal obedience due by subject to their sovereign prince so this quarrel therewith be nothing but a late usurpation of pope against the warrant of all scripture ancient counsel and father upon the temporal power of king wherewith only my apology do meddle but the publish of this book of i have bring such two answerer or rather railer upon i as all the world may wonder at for my book be first write in english a english oath be the subject thereof and the use of it proper belong to my subject of England and immediate thereafter be translate into latin upon a desire that some have of further publish it abroad it come home to i now answer in both the language and I think if it have be set forth in all the tongue that be at the confusion of Babel it will have be return answer in they all again thus may a man see how busy a bishop the devil be and how he omit no diligence for vent of his poison ware but herein their malice do clear appear that they pay i so quick with a double answer and yet have never answer their own archpriest who have write a book for the maintenance of the same oath and of the temporal authority of king allege a cloud of their own schoolman against they as for the english answerer my unnatural and fugitive subject I will neither defile my pen nor your sacred eye or ear with the describe of he who ashame nay abhor not to rail nay to rage and spew forth blasphemy against the late queen of famous memory a subject to rail against his natural sovereign by birth a man to rail against a lady by sex a holy man in outward profession to insult upon the dead nay to take Radamanthus office over his head and to sit down and play the judge in hell and all his quarrel be that either her successor or any of her servant shall speak honourable of she curse be he that curse the anoint of God and destroy may he be with the destruction of Korah that have sin in the contradiction of Korah without may such dog and swine be cast forth I say out of the spiritual jerusalem as for my latin answerer I have nothing to say to his person he be not my subject he stand or fall unto his own lord but sure I be they two have cast lot upon my book since they can not divide it the one of they my fugitive to rail upon my late predecessor but a rope be the fit answer for such a historian the other a stranger think he may be bold both to pay my person and my book as indeed he do which how just either in matter or manner we be now to examine but first who shall be the true author of this book I can but guess he call himself cardinal beauties chaplain a 1 1 be a proper word to express the true meaning of throw evangelist indeed full of throward divinity a obscure author utter unknown to i be yet little know to the world for any other of his work and therefore must be a very desperate fellow in begin his apprentisage not only to refute but to rail upon a King but who will consider the carriage of the whole book shall find that he write with such authority or at the least so little spare either king in general or my person in particular and with such a greatness 1 1 p. 46. and 2 2 p. 63. as it shall appear or at least be very probable that it be the master and not the man labour especial in one place where he quarrel i for cast up his and pie unto he pag. 69. he there gross forget himself say thereby make this author to be one person with Bellarmine but let it be the work of a indeed and not of a personate cardinal yet must it be the cardinal deed since master be the cardinal man and do it in his master defence the errand then be the cardinal and do by his own man it can but be account as his own deed especial since the english answerer do four time promise that Bellarmine or one by his appointment shall sufficient answer it and now to come to his matter and manner of answer sure if there be no more but his unmannerly manner it be enough to disgrace the whole matter thereof for first to show his pride in his printer preface of the politan edition of this he must equal the cardinal greatness with i in every thing for though he confess this master to be a obscure man yet be the cardinal chaplain he be sufficient enough forsooth to answer a english book that lack the name of a author as if a personate obscure name for author of a cardinal book be a meet match for answer a king book that lack the name of a author and a cardinal chaplain to meet with the dean of the king chapel who parson with the cardinal have as it seem agree upon to entitle to be the author of my apology and not only in the preface but also through the whole book do he keep this comparative greatness he must be as short in his answer as I be in my book he must refute all that I have say against the pope second with equal brevity and upon one page almost as I have do i and because I have set down the substance of the oath in fourteen article in just as many article must he set down that act of parliament of i wherein the oath be contain and yet have he content himself with his own pride by the demonstration of his own greatness without further wrong of i it have be the more tolerable but what cause give I he to farce his whole book with injury both against my person and book for whereas in all my apology I have never give he a foul word and especial never give he the lie he by the contrary give i nine time the lie in express term and seven time charge i with falsehood which phrase be equivalent with a Lye and as for all other word of reproach as of such like reproach I say I doubt if there be a page in all his book free except where he idle set down the pope breve and his own letter and in case this may only seem to touch the unknown author of the book who notwithstanding he know well enough as I show before he spare not my person with my own name sometime say pag. 47. that pope Clement think i to be incline to their religion sometime that I be a Puritan in Scotland pag. 98. and a persecutor of protestant in one place he conclude pag. 87. in another place pag. 98. in another place he say and in another place after that he have compare and rank i with julian the apostate he conclude catholicus not sit pag. 97. if this now be mannerly deal with a king I leave it to you to judge who can but resent such indignity do to one of your quality and as for the matter of his book it well fit indeed the manner thereof for he never answer direct to the main question in my book for whereas my apology handle only two point as I tell you before one to prove that the oath of allegiance do only meddle with the civil and temporal obedience due by subject to their natural sovereign the other that this late usurpation of pope over the temporal power of prince be against the rule of all scripture ancient counsel and father he never improve the first but by a false inference that the oath deny the pope power of excommunication direct since it deny his authority in depose of king and for the second point he bring no proof to the contrary but and and that no catholic ever doubt of it so as I may true say of he that he either understand not or at least will not seem to understand my book in never direct answer the main question as I have already say and so may I just turn over upon himself that doom of ignorance which in the begin of his book he rash pronounce upon i say that I neither understand the pope breve his letter nor the oath itself and as he delight to repeat over and over I know not how oft and triumph in this wrong inference of he that to deny the pope power to depose king be to deny the pope primacy and his spiritual power of excommunication so do he upon that ground of give the pope so ample a power over king to throne or dethrone they at his pleasure and yet only subject christian king to that slavery as I doubt not but in your own honour you will resent you of such indignity the rather since it concern so many of you as profess the romish religion far more than i for since he account i a heretic and like julian the apostate I be consequent and none of the pope flock and so be in the case of ethnic prince over who he confess the pope have no power but you be in the pope fold and you that great pastor may lead as sheep to the slaughter when it shall please he and as the ass ear must be horn if the lion listen so to interpret it so must you be remove as scab sheep from the flock if so the pope think you to be though your skin be indeed never so sound thus have he set such a new goodly interpretation upon the word of CHRIST as if it be as much to say as depose christian king and that give the pope power to dispense with all sort of oath vow penalty censure and law even with the natural obedience of subject to their sovereign lord much like to that new coin gloss that his brother 1 1 Senten card. baron excom. they ought they no subjection even in temporal matter but only and in their own discretion for the preservation of peace and good order because I say cite this place of he in my book I tell with admiration that he free all churchman from any subjection to king even those that be their bear subject he be angry with this phrase and say it be a addition for breed envy unto he and raise of hatred against he for say he although Bellarmine affirm general that churchman be not subject to earthly king yet do he not insert that particular clause though they be bear and dwell in their dominion as if the word of churchman and earthly king in general import not as much for layicke as well as churchman be subject to none but to their natural sovereign and yet do he not stick to confess that he mean it though it be not fit he say to be express and thus quarrel he i for reveal his print secret but who hatred do he fear in this be it not you who have interest but king in withdraw of due subjection from king and when the great monarch among you will remember that almost the three part of your subject and of your territory be churchman and church-living I hope you will then consider and weigh what a feather he pull out of your wing when he denude you of so many subject and their possession in the pope favour nay what brier and thorn be leave within the heart of your dominion when so populous and potent a party shall have their birth education and livelihood in your country and yet owe you no subjection nor acknowledge you for their sovereign so as where the churchman of old be content with their tithe of every man good the pope now will have little less than the three part of every king subject and dominion and as in this place so throughout all the rest of his book he do nothing but amplify the pope power over king and exaggerate my unreasonable rigour for press this oath which he will needs have to be nothing but a renew oath of supremacy in more subtle and crafty term only to rob the pope of his primacy and spiritual power make his temporal power and authority over prince to be one of the chief article of the catholic Faith but that it may the better appear unto you that all my labour and intention in this errand be only to meddle with that due temporal obedience which my subject owe unto i and not to entrap or enthral their conscience as he most false affirm you shall first see how far other godly and christian emperor and king be from acknowledge the pope temporal supremacy over they nay have create control and depose pope and next what a number of my predecessor in this kingdom have at all occasion even in the time of the great greatness of pope resist and plain withstand they in this point and first all christian emperor be for a long time so far from acknowledge the pope superiority over they as by the contrary the pope acknowledge themselves for their vassal reverence and obey the emperor as their lord for proof whereof I remit you to
