Paris in henr. 1. 1100. king Henry the first of that name after the conquest who after he be crown give the bishopric of Winchester to William gifford and forthwith invest he into all the possession belong to the bishopric contrary to the canon of the new synod 2 2 1113. king henry also give the bishopric of Canterbury to Radulph bishop of London and give he investiture by a ring and a crosier staff also pope 3 3 1119. calixtus hold a council at Rheims whither king Henry have appoint certain bishop of ENGLAND and Normandy to go thurstan also elect archbishop of York get leave of the king to go thither give his faith that he will not receive consecration of the pope and come to the synod by his liberal gift as the fashion be win the roman favour and by their mean obtain to be consecrate at the pope hand which assoon as the king of ENGLAND know he forbid he to come within his dominion moreover king Edward the first prohibit the abbot of 4 Waltham and Deane of Paul to collect a ten of every man good for a supply to the holy land which the pope by three bull have commit to their charge and the say dean of Paul compeer before the king and his council promise for the reverence he do bear unto the king not to meddle any more in that matter without the king good leave and permission here I hope a churchman disobey the pope for obedience to his prince even in church matter but this new jesuited divinity be not then know in the world the same Edward i. implead the dean of the chapel of Vuluerhampton because the say Deane have against the privilege of the kingdom give a prebend of the same chapel to one at the pope command whereupon the say Deane compeered and put himself in the king will for his offence the say Edward i. deprive also the bishop of Durham of all his liberty for disobey a prohibition of the king so as it appear the king in those day think the churchman their subject though now we be teach other seraphical doctrine for further proof whereof John of ibstocke be commit to the goal by the say king for have a suit in the court of Rome seven year for the rectory of newchurch and Edward 2 follow the footstep of his father after give out a summons against the abbot of Walden for cite the abbot of saint Albon and other in the court of Rome give out letter for his apprehension and likewise because a certain prebend of Banburie have draw one Bevercoat by a plea to Rome without the king dominion therefore be letter of caption send forth against the say prebend and Edward 3 follow likewise the example of his predecessor because a parson of Liche have summon the prior of s. oswald before the pope at Auinion for have before the judge in England recover the arrearage of a pension direct a precept for seize upon all the good both spiritual and temporal of the say parson because he have do this in prejudice of the king and crown the say king also make one harwoden to be declare culpable and worthy to be punish for procure the pope bull against a judgement that be give by the king judge and likewise because one enter upon the priory of Barnewell by the pope bull the say be commit to the tower of London there to remain during the king pleasure so as my predecessor you see of this kingdom even when the pope triumph in their greatness spare not to punish any of their subject that will prefer the pope obedience to they even in church-matter so far be they then from either acknowledge the pope for their temporal superior or yet from doubt that their own churchman be not their subject and now I will close up all these example with a act of parliament in king Richard 2 his time whereby it be prohibit that none shall procure a benefice from Rome under pain to be put out of the king protection and thus may you see that what those king successive one to another by four generation have act in private the same be also maintain by a public Law by these few example now I hope I have sufficient clear myself from the imputation that any ambition or desire of novelty in i shall have stir i either to rob the pope of any thing due unto he or to assume unto myself any far authority then that which other christian emperor and king through the world and my own predecessor of England in especial have long ago maintain neither be it enough to say as parson do in his answer to the lord coke that far more king of this country have give many more example of acknowledge or not resist the pope usurp authority some perchance lack the occasion and some the ability of resist they for even by the civil law in the case of violent intrusion and long and wrongful possession against i it be enough if I prove that I have make lawful interruption upon convenient occasion but the cardinal think the oath not only unlawful for the substance thereof but also in regard of the person who unto it be to be swear for say he the king be not a catholic and in two or three other place of his book he stick not to call i by my name very broad a heretic as I have already tell but yet before I be public declare a heretic by the pope own law my people ought not to refuse their obedience unto i and I trust if I be but a subject and accuse by the pope in his conclave before his cardinal he will have hard prove i a heretic if he judge i by their own ancient order for first I be no apostate as the cardinal will make i not only have ever be bring up in that religion which I present profess but even my father and grandfather on that side profess the same and so can be proper a heretic by their own doctrine since I never be of their church and as for the queen my mother of worthy memory although she continue in that religion wherein she be nourish yet be she so far from be superstitious or jesuited therein that at my baptism although I be baptize by a popish archbishop she send he word to forbear to use the spittle in my baptism which be obey be indeed a filthy and a apish trick rather in scorn then imitation of CHRIST and her own very word be that she will not have a pocky priest to spit in her child mouth as also the font wherein I be christen be send from the late queen here of famous memory who be my godmother and what her religion be Pius v. be not ignorant and for further proof that that renown queen my mother be not superstitious as in all her letter whereof I receive many she never make mention of religion nor labour to persuade i in it so at her last word she command her master-houshold a scottish gentleman my servant and yet alive she command he I say to tell i that although she be of another religion then that wherein I be bring up yet she will not press i to change except my own conscience force i to it for so that I lead a good life and be careful to do justice and govern well she doubt not but I will be in a good case with the profession of my own religion thus be I no apostate nor yet a deborder from that religion which one part of my parent profess and a other part give i good allowance of neither can my baptism in the rite of their religion make i a apostate or heretic in respect of my present profession since we all agree in the substance thereof be all baptize in the name of the father the son and the holy ghost upon which head there be no variance among we and now for the point of heretic I will never be ashamed to render a account of my profession and of that hope that be in i as the apostle prescribe I be such a catholic christian as believe the three creed that of the apostle that of the council of nice and that of athanasius the two latter be paraphrase to the former and I believe they in that sense as the ancient father and counsel that make they do understand they to which three creed all the minister of England do subscribe at their ordination and I also acknowledge for orthodox all those other form of creed that either be devise by counsel or particular father against such particular heresy as most reign in their time i reverence and admit the four first general counsel as catholic and orthodox and the say four general counsel be acknowledge by our act of parliament and receive for orthodox by our church as for the father I reverence they as much and more than the suit do and as much as themselves ever crave for what ever the father for the first five hundred year do with a consent agree upon to be believe as a necessary point of salvation I either will believe it also or at least will be humble silent not take upon i to condemn the same but for every private father opinion it bind not my conscience more than Bellarmine every one of the father usual contradict other I will therefore in that case follow 1 1 lib. 2. cont. cap. 32. s. Augustine rule in judge of their opinion as I find they agree with the scripture what I find agreeable thereunto I will glad embrace what be otherwise I will with their reverence reject as for the scripture no man doubt I will believe they but even for the I hold they in the same account that the ancient do they be still print and bind with our bible and public read in our church I reverence they as the writing of holy and good man but since they be not find in the canon we account they to be or 2 2 lib. 1. verb. c. 4. which be Bellarmine own distinction and therefore not sufficient whereupon alone to ground any article of faith except it be confirm by some other place of canonical scripture conclude this point with Ruffinus who be no novelist I hope that the apocryphal book be by the father permit to be read not for confirmation of doctrine but only for instruction of the people as for the saint depart I honour their memory and in honour of they do we in our church observe the day of so many of they as the scripture do canonize for saint but I be loath to believe all the tale of the legended saint and first for the bless virgin Marie I yield she that which the angel Gabriel pronounce of she and which in her canticle she prophesy of herself that be that 3 3 luk. 1.28 she be bless among woman and 4 4 ibid. ver. 48. that all generation shall call she bless I reverence she as the mother of CHRIST who of our saviour take his flesh and so the mother of GOD since the divinity and humanity of CHRIST be inseparable and I free confess that she be in glory both above angel and man her own son that be both GOD and man only except but I dare not mock she and blaspheme against GOD call she not only but and pray she to command and control her son who be her GOD and her saviour nor yet not I think that she have no other thing to do in heaven then to hear every idle man suit and busy herself in their errand while request while command her son while come down to kiss and make love with priest and while dispute and brawl with devil in heaven she be in eternal glory and joy never to be interrupt with any worldly business and there I leave she with her bless son our saviour and she in eternal felicity as for prayer to saint mart. 11.28 CHRIST I be sure have command we to come all to he that be load with sin and he will relieve we and saint Paul have forbid we to worship angel Coloss. 2.8 23. or to use any such voluntary worship that have a show of humility in that it spare not the flesh but what warrant we have to have recourse unto these or these courtier of GOD I know not I remit that to these philosophical neoterike divine it satisfy i to pray to GOD through CHRIST as I be command which I be sure must be the safe way and I be sure the safe way be the best way in point of salvation but if the romish church have coin new article of faith never hear of in the first 500 year after CHRIST I hope I shall never be condemn for a heretic and a print pamphlet beside that impudent lie that he make of my person that I be a Puritan in Scotland which I have already refute and for the better fill up of his book with such good stuff he have also five so strange and new principle of divinity therein as they be either new or at least allow by very few of his own religion all which lie with diverse other and five strange and as I think erroneous point of doctrine with sundry falsification of history be set down in a table by themselves in the end of this my epistle have their refutation annex to every one of they but as for the anent the oath of allegiance the pick of advantage upon the wrong place of the figure in the citation or such error in the print by casual addition or omission of word that make nothing to the be that ecclesiastical dignity though by the sloth of prince as I say before it be now come to that height of usurp honour yet be in the true original and foundation thereof nothing else but the title Gregory who in his epistle six hundred year after CHRIST make the first mention of cardinal and so these now electour of the apostolic sea be long and many hundred year unknown or unheard of after the apostolic age and yet do he speak of they but in this sense as I have now describe I hope the cardinal who call he the apostle of England can blame i that be king thereof to acknowledge the cardinal in no other degree of honour than our say apostle do but how they shall now become to be so strange exalt above their first original institution that from parish-priest and deacon priest inferior they shall now come to be prince and peer to king and from a degree under bishop as both 1 1 lib. cap. 16. any thing will be just say against it not doubt but enough of my subject will reply upon these libeler and answer they sufficient wish you deep to consider and weigh your common interest in this cause of Gregory the great his obedience to the emperor mauritius not be ashamed to slander that great personage christian humility and obedience to the emperor with the title of a constrain and force for conscience sake rom. 13.5 1. pet. 2.13 and as contrary to tertullians apology for christian and all the protestation of the ancient father in that case but it be good luck for the ancient christian in the day of ethnic emperor that this profane and new conceit be then unknown among they otherwise they will have be utter destroy and root out in that time and no man to have pity they as Augustine his retraction for so he say in his preface he do in place of retract any of his former error or any matter of substance not retract but recant indeed I mean sing over again and obstinate confirm a number of the gross of they among the which the exempt of all churchman from subjection to any temporal prince and the set up not only of the pope but even of the people above their natural king be two of his main point as for the exemption of the cleric he be so greedy there to prove that point as he deny Caesar to have be Paul lawful judge Acts. 25.10 contrary to the express text and Paul plain appellation and acknowledge he his judge beside his many time claim to the roman privilege act 22.28 and avow himself a Roman by freedom and therefore of necessity a subject to the roman emperor but it be a wonder that these roman catholic who vaunt themselves of the ancienty both of their doctrine and church and reproach we so bitter of our novelty shall not be ashamed to make such a new inept gloss as this upon s. that when the schoolman come to be doctor in the church and to mar the old ground in divinity by sow in among they their philosophical distinction though they I say do maintain that whatsoever thing be tell a confessor under the veil of confession how dangerous soever the matter be yet he be bind to conceal the party name yet do none of they I mean of the old schoolman deny that if a matter be light that the danger thereof may be prevent but that no treason nor devilish plot though it shall tend to the ruin or exterminion of a whole kingdom must be reveal if it be tell under confession no not the matter so far indirect disclose as may give occasion for prevent the danger thereof though it agree with the conceit of some three or four new jesuited doctor it be such a new and dangerous to be in by this his damnable and false ground in divinity I will true inform you of garnets case which be far otherwise then this answerer allege for first it can never be account a thing under confession which he that reveal it do not discover with a remorse account it a sin whereof he repent he but by the contrary discover it as a good motion and be therein not dissuade by his confessor strange fiction the vanity of all which I will in two word discover unto you first as for the cause of their punishment I do constant maintain that which I have say in my apology that no man either in my time or in the late queen ever die here for his conscience for let he be never so devout a Papist nay though he profess the same never so constant his life be in no danger by the law if he break not out into some outward act express against the word of the law or plot not some unlawful or dangerous practice or attempt priest and popish churchman only except that receive order beyond the sea who for the manifold treasonable practice that they have kindle and plot in this country be discharge to come home again under pain of treason after their receive of the say order abroad and yet without some other guilt in they then their bare home-comming have none of they be ever put to death and next for the cruel torment and strange sort of death that they say so many of they have be put unto if there be no more but the law and continual observe custom of England these many hundred year in all criminal matter it will sufficient serve to refute all these monstrous lie for no torture be ever use here but the manacle or the rack and these never but in case of high treason and all sort of traitor die but one manner of death here whether they be Papist or Protestant traitor queen Mary time only except for then indeed no sort of cruel death be spare unexecuted upon man woman and child profess our religion yea even against the law of God and nature woman with child be put to cruel death for their profession and a live child fall out of the mother belly be throw in the same fire again that consume the mother but these tyrannous persecution be do by the bishop of that time under the warrant of the pope authority and therefore be not subject to that constant order and form of execution which as they be here establish by our law and custom so be they according observe in the punishment of all criminal for all priest and popish traitor here receive their judgement in the temporal court and so do never exceed those form of execution which be prescribe by the law or approve by continual custom one thing be also to be mark in this case that stranger be never call in question here for their religion which be far otherwise I hope in any place where the inquisition but have now too much weary you with this long discourse whereby I have make you plain see that the wrong do unto i in particular first by the pope breve and then by these libeler do as deep interest you all in general that be king free prince or state as it do i in particular I will now conclude with my humble prayer to God that he will waken we up all out of that lethargike slumber of security wherein our predecessor and we have lie so long and that we may first grave consider what we be bind in conscience to do for the plant and spread of the true worship of God according to his reveal will in all our dominion therein hear the voice of our only pastor for his sheep will know his voice John 10.27 as himself say and not follow the vain corrupt and changeable tradition of man and next that we may provident look to the security of our own state and not suffer this encroach babylonian monarch to win still ground upon we and if GOD have so merciful deal with we that be his lieutenant upon earth as that he have join his cause with our interest the spiritual liberty of the gospel with our temporal freedom with what zeal and courage may we then embrace this work for our labour herein be assure to receive at the last the eternal and inestimable reward of felicity in the kingdom of heaven and in the mean time to procure unto ourselves a temporal security in our temporal kingdom in this world as for so many of you as be already persuade of that truth which I profess though differ among yourselves in some particular point I think little persuasion shall move you to this holy and wise resolution our greatness nor our number praise be GOD be not so contemptible but that we may show good example to our neighbour since almost the half of all christian people and of all sort and degree be of our profession I mean all go out of Babylon even from king and free prince to the mean sort of people but above all my love brother and cousin keep fast the unity of faith among yourselves reject 1 1 1. tim. 1.4 question of genealogy and 2 2 c. 4.7 as Paul say let not the foolish heat of your preacher for idle controversy or indifferent thing tear asunder that mystical body whereof you be a part since the very coat of he who member we be be without a seam and let not our division breed a slander of our faith and be a word of reproach in the mouth of our adversary who make unity to be one of the special note of the true church and as for you my love brother and cousin who it have not yet please GOD to illuminate with the light of his truth I can but humble pray with that it will please GOD to open your eye that you may see what innumerable and invincible army of angel be ever prepare and ready to defend the truth of GOD act 26.29 and with s. Paul I wish that you be as I be in this case especial that you will search the scripture and ground your faith upon your own certain knowledge and not upon the report of other Abac. 2.4 since every man must be safe by his own faith but leave this to GOD his merciful providence in his due time I have good reason to remember you to maintain the ancient liberty of your crown and commonwealth not suffer any under GOD to set himself up above you and therein to imitate your own noble predecessor who even in the day of great blindness do diverse time courageous oppose themselves to the encroach ambition of pope yea some of your kingdom have in all aage maintain and without any interruption enjoy your liberty against the most ambitious pope and some have of very late have a evident proof of the pope ambitious aspire over your temporal power wherein you have constant maintain and defend your lawful freedom to your immortal honour and therefore I hearty wish you all to do in this case the office of godly and just king and earthly judge which consist not only in not wrong or invade the liberty of any other person for to that will I never press to persuade you but also in defend and maintain these lawful liberty wherewith GOD have endue you for you who GOD have ordain to protect your people from injury shall be ashamed to suffer yourselves to be wrong by any and thus assure myself that you will with a settle judgement free of prejudice weigh the reason of this my discourse and accept my plainness in good part grace this my apology with your favour and yet no long then till it shall be just and worthy refute I end with my earnest prayer to the almighty for your prosperity and that after your happy temporal reign in earth you may live and reign in heaven with he for instructor and teacher who corrupt their judgement with such dangerous position and principle that it be a easy matter to reduce the general into particular and to apply the dictate which he give out allegiance then Eleazar have to refuse the eat of swine flesh confutation this assertion imply a strange doctrine indeed that the pope breve be to be prefer before Moses law and that papist be more bind to obey the pope decree than the jew be to obey the law of God pronounce by Moses novel doctrine pag. 135. 5 churchman be exempt from the jurisdiction of secular prince and therefore be no subject to king yet ought they to observe their law concern matter temporal not by virtue of any law but by enforcement of reason that be to say not for that they be their subject but because reason will give it that such law be to be keep for the public good and the quiet of the commonwealth confutation how true friend the cardinal and his chaplain be to king that will have so many subject exempt from their power see my praemonition pag. 296 297. also pag 330. 331. etc. but as for this and the like new aphorism I will have these cunning merchant to cease to vent such stuff for ancient and catholic ware in the christian world till they have disprove their own venetian who charge they with novelty and forgery in this point a declaration concern the proceed with the state general of the unite province of the low country in the cause of d. conradus VORSTIUS to the honour of our lord and saviour JESUS CHRIST the eternal son of the eternal father the only THEANTHPOPOS MEDIATOUR and reconciler of mankind in sign of thankfulness his most humble and most oblige servant JAMES by the grace of GOD king of great Britain france and IRELAND defender of the faith do dedicate and consecrate this his declaration that it be one of the principal part of that duty which appertain unto a christian king to protect the true church within his own dominion and to extirpate heresy be a maxim without all controversy in which respect those honourable title of and Vindex keeper and avenger of both the table of the law and Nutritius nurse father of the church do right belong unto every emperor king and christian monarch but what interest a christian king may just pretend to meddle in Repub. within another state or common wealth in matter of this nature where stranger be not allow to be too curious be the point in question and whereof we mean at this time to treat for our zeal to the glory of God be the only motive that induce we as he who be the searcher of the heart and reins can witness to make sundry instance and request unto the state general of the unite province for the banishment of a wretched heretic or rather atheist out of their dominion name d. conradus Vorstius have be so ill interpret or rather wrest to a perverse sense by a sort of people who corrupt stomach turn all good nourishment into bad and pernicious humour as if it have be some vanity and desire of vain glory in we or else a ambition to encroach by little and little upon the liberty of their state which have carry we headlong into the business as both to clear our own honour from the dark mist of these false and scandalous imputation as also to make it true appear unto the christian world in what sort we have proceed herein we have think good to publish this present declaration contain as well the discourse of our whole negotiation hitherto with the state in this cause as also the reason which have move we to take it so to heart and to persevere therein as we have do and will do God will until it please he to bring it to some good and happy end in autumn last about the end of August be in our hunt progress there come to our hand two book of the say Vorstius the one entitle dedicate to the landgrave of Hessen imprint in the year 1610. the other his exegesis upon that book dedicate to the state and print in the year 1611. which book assoon as we have receive and not without much horror and detestation cast our eye only upon some of the principal article of his disputation contain in the first book and his commentary thereupon in the second God be our witness that the zeal of his glory do so transport we as to say with s. Paul we stay not one hour but dispatch a letter present to our ambassador resident with the state to this purpose follow trusty and well-beloved etc. you shall repair to the state general with all possible diligence in our name tell they that we doubt not but that their ambassador which be with we about two year since do inform they of a forewarn that we wish the say ambassador to make unto they in our name to beware in time of seditious and heretical preacher and not to suffer any such to creep into their State our principal meaning be of arminius who though himself be late dead yet have he leave too many of his disciple behind he now according to that care which we continual have of the weal of their state we have think good to send unto they a new advertisement upon the like occasion which be this that there be late come to our hand a piece of work of one Vorstius a divine in those part wherein he have publish such monstrous blasphemy and horrible atheism as out of the care that a christian prince and defender of the faith as we have ever be ought to have of the good of the church we hold not only such a scandalous book worthy to be burn but even the author himself to be most severe punish this notwithstanding we be inform that the state be so far from be sensible of so great a scandal to the church as that the most part of they have already yield he their free consent and voice for the obtain of the place of divinity reader in the university of Leyden which the abovenamed arminius of little better stuff late enjoy and though himself be dead have leave his sting yet live among they have therefore understand that the time of election will be about Michaelmas next and hold ourselves bind in honour and conscience as a christian prince and one who have vouchsafe the state our royal favour and support in respect of their religion to prevent so great a mischief so far as we be able we will and require you to let they understand how infinite we shall be displease if such a monster receive advancement in the church and if it be allege that he have recant his atheistical opinion and that thereupon he may be capable of the place you shall tell they that we think his recantation so slender a satisfaction for so foul a offence as that we hold he rather worthy of punishment or at least to be debar from all promotion wherein though we assure ourself that they will of their own discretion eschew such a viper who may make a fearful rend not only in their ecclesiastical but also in their politic state yet notwithstanding all this if they will continue their resolution to prefer he you shall then make a protestation to they in our name that we will not fail to make know to the world public in print how much we detest such abominable heresy and all allower and tolerator of they and because the state shall know upon what reason we have ground this our admonition you shall receive herewith a * * this catalogue be here purposely omit for avoid a needless repetition see the principal point thereof be contain in a little collection annex at the end of our second letter write to Wynwood catalogue of his damnable position of which no one page of the book be free give under our signet etc. for observe that so prodigious a monster begin to live among they we can do no less consider the infinite obligation which we owe unto God then to make our zeal appear against such a enemy to the essence of the deity beside the charity which we bear to the say state our neighbour and confederate profess the same religion that we do do enforce we to admonish they to eschew and prevent in time so dangerous a contagion which disperse itself may infect not only the body of their state but all Christendom also the danger whereof be so much great to our dominion then to many other by how much the province of the say state be near unto we in their situation our ambassador therefore have sufficient acquit himself of that which we give he in charge by exhort they in our name timely to prevent the danger which may ensue by enterteyn such a guest as VORSTIUS which at that time they may easy have do see he be not yet settle at Leyden neither be he lodge in the house appoint for the public reader nor be his wife and family yet arrive and therefore much more easy for they to have rid he out of their country send he back to the place from whence he come according to the old proverb it be more honest to refuse a guest then when you have once receive he to thrust he out of door yet notwithstanding all the diligence that our ambassador can use and the opportunity which at that time be offer they to discharge he all the answer he can procure from they be but this that whereas a proposition be make on the behalf of his majesty of great Britain in the assembly of the lord state general of the unite province by sir Ralph winwood his majesty ambassador and counsellor in the council of state in those country exhibit in write the 21. of the month precedent the substance thereof be first ample debate by the deputy of the state of Holland and westfrizeland and thereupon mature deliberation have the say lord state general in answer to the say proposition have most humble request and by these present do humble request his majesty to believe that as for preservation of the liberty right and privilege of the low-countries against the unjust tyrannical and bloody course contrary thereunto practise for many year upon the conscience body and fortune of the good inhabitant of all quality of those country by the spaniard and their adherent they have be constrain after a long patience many remonstrance request and other submissive proceed use in vain to take arm for their necessary defence when they see no other remedy as also to crave the assistance of his majesty particular and of other king prince and common wealth by who favour but principal by his majesty they have since continual sustain for many year with a exceed great constancy and moderation as well in prosperity as in adversity a heavy chargeable and bloody war many terrible and cruel encounter notable battle both by land and sea matchless sieges of a number of town ruin and devastation of city and country and other difficulty incident to the war so do their lordship always confess that in the chief and principal reason which have move they at first to entertain and since to maintain the say resolution have be the foresay tyranny exercise upon the conscience body and good of their people by introduction of the inquisition and constraint in matter of religion for which respect their obligation to his majesty be great increase in that after so many demonstration of affection favour and assistance in the pursuit of their just cause his majesty be yet please like a love father to assure unto they the continuance of the same royal affection and assistance by take care that the true christian reform religion be pure and sincere teach within their country aswell in church as in school for which the lord state general do most humble thank his majesty and will for their part by all lawful mean endeavour so to second his sincere and christian intention in this particular as his majesty shall receive all good contentment as concern the business of doct. Vorstius principal handle in the foresay proposition the lord state general to make the matter more plain have inform themselves first that the curator of the university of Leyden according to their duty and the ancient custom ever since the foundation of that university have diligent make inquiry for some doctor to be choose into the place of divinity professor there at that time void after mature deliberation be give to understand that at Steinford within the dominion of the count of Tecklenbourg etc. who be of the first count that in Germany have cast off the yoke of the papacy idolatry and impure religion and embrace the reform religion which to this day they maintain there do remain one doct. conradus Vorstius who have continue in that place about fifteen year a professor of true religion and a minister and that the say conradus Vorstius for his learning and other good part be much seek after by prince Maurice landgrave of Hessen with intent to make he divinity professor in some university of his country moreover that he have sufficient and to the great contentment even of those that be now become his great adversary show with a christian moderation his learning and purity in the holy knowledge of divinity against the renown jesuite Bellarmine and that the say conradus Vorstius be thereupon send for by the curator aforesaid about the begin of july 1610. which message be second by letter of recommendation from his excellency and from the deputy councelor for the state of Holland and Westfrizeland unto the say count of Tecklenburg do according take effect in the month of August follow the say election and call be countermine by certain person to who office or disposition the business do nothing at all belong which be perceive and the say Vorstius charge with some unsoundness of doctrine the curator do thereupon think fit with the good like of Vorstius himself that as well in the university of Leyden as at the hage he shall appear in his own justification to answer all accuser and accusation whatsoever at which time there be not any one that do offer to charge he in the month of May follow six minister do undertake to prove that VORSTIUS have publish false and unsound doctrine who afterward be hear in full assembly of the state of Holland and Westfrizeland in the presence of the curator and six other minister on the one part and Vorstius in his own defence on the other part and that which can be say on either side to the several point in their sever all refutation respective the say lord state have grave deliberate upon the allegation as well of the one part as of the other as also hear the opinion of the say minister after the manner and custom of the say assembly can not see any reason why the execution of that which be do by the curator lawful and according to order aught to be hinder for impeach in August follow there be send over hither certain other article wherewith Vorstius be charge and disperse in little print pamphlet among the people the say lord state enter into a new consultation and there resolve that Vorstius according both to God law the law of nature and the law write as also according to the laudable use and custom of their country shall be hear against his new accuser concern those article there lay to his charge and moreover it be general declare by the state of Holland and Westfrizeland there assemble as every one of they likewise in his own particular and the curator and bourgmaster of Leyden for their part do special declare that there be never any intention to permit other religion to be teach in the university of Leyden than the christian religion reform and ground upon the word of God and beside that if the say Vorstius shall be find guilty in any of the aforesaid point whereof he be accuse that they will not admit he to the place of professor the deputy of the say lord state of Holland and Westfrizeland further declare that they do assure believe that if his majesty of great Britain be well inform of the true circumstance of this business and of their sincere intention therein he will according to his high wisdom prudence and benignity conceive favourable of they and their proceed whereof the lord state general be no less confident and the rather for that the say deputy have assure they that the lord state of Holland and Westfrizeland their superior will proceed in this business as in all other with all due reverence care and respect unto his majesty serious admonition as become they and the lord state general do request the say lord ambassador to recommend this their answer unto his majesty with favour give at the hage in the assembly of the say lord state general 1. October 1611. but before we have receive this answer from the state some of Vorstius book be bring over into England and as it be report not without the knowledge and direction of the author and about the same time one a scholar of the late arminius who be the first in our age that infect Leyden with heresy be so impudent as to send a letter unto the archbishop of Canterbury with a book entitle and not think it sufficient to avow the send of such a book the title whereof only be enough to make it worthy the fire he be moreover so shameless as to maintain in his letter to the archbishop that the doctrine contain in his book be agreeable with the doctrine of the church of England let the church of CHRIST then judge whether it be not high time for we to bestir ourselves when as this gangrene have not only take hold among our near neighbour so as not only the next house be on fire but do also begin to creep into the bowel of our own kingdom for which cause have first give order that the say book of Vorstius shall be public burn as well in Paul churchyard as in both the university of this kingdom we think good to renew our former request unto the state for the banishment of Vorstius by a letter which we cause our ambassador to deliver unto they from we at their assembly in the hage the five of November whereunto they have refer we in their former answer the tenor of which letter be as follow high and mighty lord have understand by your answer to that proposition which be make unto you in our name by our ambassador there resident that at your assembly to be hold in November next you be resolve then to give order concern the business of that wretched d. Vorstius we have think good notwithstanding the declaration which our ambassador have already make unto you in our name touch that particular to put you again in remembrance thereof by this letter and thereby free to discharge ourselves both in point of our duty towards God and of that sincere friendship which we bear towards you first we assure ourselves that you be sufficient persuade that no worldly respect can move we to have thus importune you in a affair of this nature be draw into it only through our zeal to the glory of God and the care which we have that all occasion of such great scandal as this be unto the true reform church of God may be in due time foresee and prevent we be therefore to let you understand that we do not a little wonder that you have not only seek to provide a habitation in so eminent a place among you for such a corrupt person as this Vorstius be but that you have also afford he your licence and protection to print that apology which he have dedicate unto you a book wherein he do most impudent maintain the execrable blasphemy which in his former he have disgorge the which we be now able to affirm out of our own knowledge have since that letter which we write unto our ambassador read over and over again with our own eye not without extreme mislike and horror both his book the first dedicate to the landgrave of Hessen and the other to you we have well hope that the corrupt seed which that enemy of God arminius do sow among you some few year since who disciple and follower be yet too bold and frequent within your dominion have give you a sufficient warning afterward to take heed of such infect person see your own country man already divide into faction upon this occasion a matter so opposite to unity which be indeed the only prop and safety of your state next under God as of necessity it must by little and little bring you to utter ruin if wise you do not provide against it and that in time it be true that it be our hard hap not to hear of this arminius before he be dead and that all the reform church of Germany have with open mouth complain of he but assoon as we understand of that distraction in your state which after his death he leave behind he we do not fail take the opportunity when your last extraordinary ambassador be here with we to use some such speech unto they concern this matter as we think fit for the good of your state and which we doubt not but they have faithful report unto you for what need we make any question of the arrogancy of these heretic or rather atheistical sectary among you when one of they at this present remain in your town of Leyden have not only presume to publish of late a blasphemous book of the apostasy of the saint but have beside be so impudent as to send the other day a copy thereof as a goodly present to our archbishop of Canterbury together with a letter wherein he be not ashamed as also in his book to lie so gross as to avow that his heresy contain in the say book be agreeable with the religion and profession of our church of England for these respect therefore have we cause enough very hearty to request you to root out with speed those heresy and schism which be beginning to bud forth among you which if you suffer to have the reins any long you can expect any other issue thereof than the curse of God infamy throughout all the reform church and a perpetual rent and distraction in the whole body of your State but if peradventure this wretched Vorstius shall deny or equivocate upon those blasphemous point of heresy and atheism which already he have broach that perhaps may move you to spare his person and not cause he to be burn which never any heretic better deserve and wherein we will leave he to your own bristian wisdom but to suffer he upon any defence or abnegation which he shall offer to make still to continue and to teach among you be a thing so abominable as we assure ourselves it will not once enter into any of your thought for admit he will prove himself innocent which nevertheless he can do in most of those point wherewith he be charge yet be it but the scandal of his person which will still remain it be cause more then enough for you to remove he out of your dominion you know what be write of Caesar wife that it be not sufficient for she to be innocent but she must also be free from all occasion of suspicion how much more than ought you to be wary and cautious in a matter of so great importance as this which concern the glory of God the salvation of your soul the soul of your people and the safety of your state and not to suffer so dangerous a spark to lie kindle among you for a man may easy conjecture that fear and the horror of his own action will make he bold deny that poison which boil at his heart for what will not be deny that deny the eternity and omnipotency of God and howbeit he be innocent as we have say before the church of God be not so ill furnish with man of sufficiency for that place as that you need be unprovided of some other who shall not be subject to that scandal wherewith he be so taint as it must be a long penance and many year of probation that must wear it away but especial ought you to be very careful not to hazard the corruption of your youth in so famous a university by the doctrine of so scandalous a person who it be to be fear when he find himself once well settle there will return again to his ancient vomit we will therefore conclude with this request unto you that you will assure yourselves that the affection only which we bear unto your state have enforce we to use this liberty towards you not doubt for our part but that as this which we have write unto you proceed from the sincerity of our conscience so our good god will be please to give you a due apprehension thereof and that your resolution in a matter of so great consequence may tend to his glory to your own honour and safety to the extirpation of these spring atheism and heresy and to the satisfaction not only of we but of all the reform church who have be hitherto extreme scandalize therewith but if on the contrary part we fail of that we expect at your hand which God forbid and that you suffer hereafter such pestilent heretic to nestle among you who dare take upon they that licentious liberty to fetch again from hell the ancient heresy long since condemn or else to invent new of their own brain contrary to the belief of the true catholic church we shall then be constrain to our great grief public to protest against these abomination and as God have honour we with the title of defender of the faith not only to depart and separate ourselves from the union of such false and heretical church but also to exhort all other reform church to join with we in a common council how to extinguish and remand to hell these abominable heresy that now new begin to put forth again and furthermore for our own particular we shall be enforce strict to inhibit the youth of our dominion from repair to so infect a place as be the university of we hope and expect for better assure ourselves in the mercy of our good God that as he have a long time preserve you from your temporal enemy and at this time be beginning to establish your estate to the contentment of all your friend but especial to we who have never be want to assist you upon all occasion that the same god will not leave you for a prey to your spiritual adversary who gape at nothing but your utter destruction and in this confidence we will recommend you and the prosperity of your affair to the protection of God remain as we have ever be your good friend JAMES r. give at our palace of Westminster the 6. of October 1611. we write likewise at the same time another letter to our ambassador for his direction in the whole business the copy whereof be this which follow TRustie and well-beloved perceive by the state their answer to your proposition deliver to they in our name concern the matter of Vorstius that they have take time for their proceed with he and have some reason to think that his favourer among they be strong than be to be wish we have think good to renew our admonition unto they in this matter by a letter of our own write at good length and in earnest manner which you shall herewith receive and at the time of their meeting for this purpose present unto they in our name insist with they with all the earnestness you can both for the remove of this blasphemous monster as also that they may now at least take some such solid order as this licentious liberty of dispute or argue such unprofitable question whereby new opinion may be daily set abroach against the ground of divinity may hereafter be restrain as well at Leyden as in all the rest of their dominion and for the better strengthen of this motion we do herewith send you a note of some of the most special atheistical point wherewith his book be full farce but if contrary to our expectation all our labour can move they to give satisfaction not to we but to the whole church of God in this case then be you if no better may be to renew our protestation unto they which we send you in our former letter assure they that our first labour shall be to publish to the world their defection from the faith and true church of Christ we mean the defection of they who they maintain and harbour in their bosom though we purposely omit this point in our letter unto they for be too harsh except all other remedy be desperate but we both wish and hope for better Theobaldes 6. October 1611. but before our ambassador have opportunity to deliver our letter to the state there be not only certain people more cunning than zealous who cause a rumour to be spread among the state that we be become exceed cold in the business nay that we have almost quite give it over but also in the mean time the say Vorstius be settle at Leyden lodge in the quality of a public reader and his wife and his family there arrive as he himself witness in his book call Christiana and for his own word in his preface be these etc. that be to say since therefore God so dispose of i I have settle myself and with my whole family do now inhabit in that city and province which acknowledge your supreme authority on earth etc. our ambassador therefore have on the one side consideration of that false report which be spread abroad of our coldness in the business and on the other side observe how Vorstius be establish at Leyden after our first admonition and request make unto the state but before their assembly on the five of November he then resolve first to present unto they our letter make likewise himself a remonstrance to the same purpose which we have here set down together with a extract of certain passage collect out of the say book of Vorstius which we send unto our ambassador and be by he then show unto the state that they may discern the lion by his paw my lord if ever the king of great Britain my master have merit any thing of this state and how much he have merit in respect of his great favour and royal assistance your lordship acknowledge they with all gratitude can best witness and best judge be have sure merit at this present have by his letter full of zeal and piety which he have write unto you endenour to procure the establishment of that religion only within your province which the reform church of great Britain France and Germany by a mutual consent have general embrace for what be it to his majesty whether d. Vorstius be admit professor in the university of Leyden or not or whether the doctrine of arminius be preach in your church save that as a christian prince he desire the advancement of the gospel and as your best friend and ally the strengthen of your commonwealth who first foundation be cyment with the blood of his subject and which in his judgement can no way subsist if witting and willing you suffer the reform religion to be either by the practice of your doctor sophisticate or by their malice deprave if therefore religion be as it be the Palladium of your common wealth and that to preserve the one in her glory and perfection be to maintain the other in her purity let yourselves then be judge in how great a danger the state must needs be at this present so long as you permit the schism of arminius to have such vogue as now they have in the principal town of Holland and if you suffer Vorstius to be receive divinity professor in the university of Leyden the seminary of your burch who in scorn of the holy word of GOD have after his own fancy devise a new sect patch together of sener all piece of all sort of ancient and modern heresy the fool say in his heart there be no god but he that with open mouth of set purpose and of prepense malice have let his pen run at random to disgorge so many blasphemy against the sacred majesty of GOD this fellow shall wear the garland of all that ever yet be hear of since by the mean of the gospel the light of christian religion have shine unto the world if any man doubt of it for a proof see here what his majesty with his own hand have collect out of his writing out of his annotation but there be nothing forbid we to say that God have a body so as we take a body in the large signification they therefore do not speak circumspect enough who say that God be altogether as unchangeable in his will as he be in his essence we find it no where write that the substance of God be simple immense nay there be many place which seem to carry a contrary meaning no magnitude be actual infinite and therefore God be not actual infinite and sure if all and every event of thing be precise set down and from eternity there need not then that continual inspection and procuration which nevertheless be every where attribute unto God they therefore who teach that there be in God a certain universal knowledge in do seem to answer more full but so as they do confess likewise that there be more cause of certainty in the vision of thing present then in the vision of thing future contingent all thing which GOD have once decree and precise determine he do after such his determination exact know they but this can be affirm of all and every other thing which be or come to pass be consider several and by themselves because they have their existence not only successive in time but also contingent and oftentimes conditional out of his apology it be to be understand that the father have a certain peculiar be or as it be a limit and bound essence from whence it be easy prove that there be real certain internal accident in God that be to say if it be lawful to use such a word in the very fore-electing mind and will of God in the 16. chapter he do dangerous dissent from the receive opinion of divine concern the ubiquity of God presence in the 19 chapter pag. 99 he do attribute unto God magnitude and quantity these be in part the opinion of that great divine who they have choose to domineer in the chair at Leyden in opposition whereunto I mean not to say any thing else then that which the roman orator do once pronounce in the like case fit it be a evil and a wicked custom say he to dispute against God whether it be in earnest or in jest now my lord I address myself unto your lordship and according unto the charge which I have receive from the king my master I conjure you by the amity that be betwixt his kingdom and your province the which on his part will continue always inviolable to awaken your spirit and to have a careful eye at this assembly of Holland which be already begin that the common wealth take no harm which undoubted at one time or other will be turn upside down if you suffer such a dangerous contagion to barber so near you and not remove it out of your province assoon as possible you may the disciple of socinus with who doctrine he have be suckle in his childhood do seek he for their master and be ready to embrace he let he go be be a bird of their own feather a cover fit for such a dish on the other side the student in divinity at Leyden to the number of 56. by a dutiful remonstrance present unto the state of Holland the 16. of October the last year do most humble beseech the say state not to use their authority in compel they to receive a professor who both by the attestation of the divinity college at Basil and Heydelberg as also by manifest evidence out of his own writing be convince of a infinite number of heresy these reason therefore namely the proof of so many enormous and horrible heresy maintain in his book the instance of his majesty ground upon the welfare and honour of this country the request either of all or of the most part of your province the petition of all the minister except those only which be of arminius sect shall i think prevail so far with my lord the state of Holland and we hope will so far prevail as they will at the last apply themselves to the performance of that which both the sincerity of religion and the service of their country require at their hand furthermore I have commandment from his majesty to move you in his name to set down some certain reglement in matter of religion throughout your province that this licentious freedom of disputation may by that mean be restrain which breed nothing but faction and partake and that you will absolute take away the liberty of prophesy which Vorstius do so much recommend unto you in the dedicatory epistle of his Anti-Bellarmine the book whereof his patron do boast so much to conclude his majesty do exhort you see you have heretofore take arm for the liberty of your conscience and have so much endure in a violent and bloody war the space of forty year for the profession of the gospel that now have get the upper hand of your misery you will not suffer the follower of arminius to make your action a example for they to proclaim throughout the world that wicked doctrine of the apostasy of the saint to be short the account which his majesty do make of your amity appear sufficient by the treaty which he have make with your lordship by the succour which your province have receive from his crown by the deluge of blood which his subject have spend in your war religion be the only sowder of this amity for his majesty be by the grace of GOD defender of the faith by which title he do more value himself then by the title of king of great Britain do hold himself oblige to defend all those who profess the same faith and religion with he but if once your zeal begin to grow cold therein his majesty will then straightways imagine that your friendship towards he and his subject will likewise freeze by little and little thus much I have in charge to add unto that which his majesty in his own letter have write unto you you may be please to consider of it as the importance of the cause do require and to resolve thereupon that which your wisdom shall think fit for the honour and service of your country but our ambassador have after a delay for the space of diverse week receive this cold and ambiguous answer unto our letter and proposition that be to say that the lord state general have serious deliberate upon the proposition which be make unto they by our ambassador the five of November as also upon our letter of the six of October deliver unto they at the same time do very humble give we thanks for the continuance of our royal affection towards the welfare of their country and the preservation of the true reform christian religion therein and that the say state general as also the state of Holland and Westfrizeland in their several assembly respective have enter into consultation with all due reverence and regard unto we concern those article wherewith doctor conradus Vorstius be charge the curator of the university of Leyden do thereupon take occasion to make a order provisional that the say Vorstius shall not be admit to the exercise of his place which be according perform so as upon the matter he be then in the city of Leyden but as a inhabitant or citizen and that in case the say Vorstius shall not be able to clear himself from those accusation which be lay to his charge before or in the next assembly of the state of Holland and Westfrizeland which be to be hold in February follow the lord state general do then assure themselves that the state of Holland and Westfrizeland will decide the matter with good contentment and therefore forasmuch as at that time there can be no more do in the cause without great inconvenience and distaste to the principal town of the say province our ambassador be require to recommend thus much in the best manner he can unto we and with the most advantage to the service of their country upon the coldness therefore of this answer which he fear will give we no satisfaction he think it be now high time to consider what the last remedy may be whereof use be to be make for the advancement of this business and perceive that he have already perform all the rest of our commandment except only to protest in case of refusal and esteem such a cold answer accompany with so many delay to be no less in effect then a absolute refusal he thereupon resolve to make this protestation in their public assembly which hereafter follow my lord the historiographer who have diligent look into the antiquity of France do observe that the advocate there in time past be accustom to begin their plead with some latin sentence take out of the holy scripture I shall at this time follow their example and my sentence shall be this argue if thy brother trespass against thou go and tell he his fault between he and thou alone if he hear thou thou have win thy brother but if he hear thou not take yet with thou one or two that by the mouth of two or three witness every word may be confirm and if he refuse to hear they tell it unto the church there be not any one of you as I suppose in this assembly that will not acknowledge the brotherly love wherewith the king my master have always affect the good of your province and the fatherly care which he have ever have to procure the establishment of your State in which respect his majesty have understand that my lord the state of Holland be determine to call unto the place of divinity professor in the Vniver sitie of Leyden one doctor conradus Vorstius a person attaint by many witness of a number of heresy the shame whereof will light upon the church of God and consequent upon his majesty person and crown be therewith exceed offend and for the more timely prevention of a infinity of evil which necessary will thereupon ensue do give i in charge by express letter to exhort you which I do the 21. of September last to wash your hand from that man and not to suffer he to come within your country to this exhortation your answer be that in the carriage of this business all due observance and regard shall be have unto his majesty nevertheless so it be that his majesty have receive so little respect herein as that in stead of debar Vorstius from come into the country which even by the law of friendship his majesty may have require the proceed have be clean contrary for he be suffer to come unto Leyden have be receive there with all honour have there take up his habitation where he be treat and lodge in the quality of a public professor his majesty then perceive that his first motion have so little prevail think good to write himself a letter unto you to the same purpose full of zeal and affection persuade you by many reason there set down at length not to stain your own honour and the honour of the reform church by call unto you that wretched and wicked atheist these letter be present in this assembly the five of November a great number of the deputy of the town of Holland be then present at which time as I be command by his majesty I use some speech myself to the same effect some six week after I receive a answer to my proposition but a answer confuse ambiguous and whole impertinent by which I have reason to conceive that there be no meaning at all to send Vorstius away who be at this present in Leyden receive and acknowledge respect and treat as public professor whether it be to grace that university in stead of the decease joseph Scaliger I can tell or whether it be to give he mean to do more mischief in secret which perhaps for shame he dare not in public for these reason according unto that charge which I have receive from the king my master I do in his name and on his behalf protest in this assembly against the wrong injury and scandal do unto the reform religion by the receive and retain of conradus Vorstius in the university of Leyden and against the violence offer unto that alliance which be betwixt his majesty and your province the which be found upon the preservation and maintenance of the reform religion you have not let so much as in you lie absolute to violate in the proceed of this cause of which enormous indignity commit against the church of GOD and against his majesty person in prefer the presence of Vorstius before his amity and alliance the king my master hold himself bind to be sensible and if reparation thereof be not make and that speedy which can be by any other mean then by send Vorstius away his majesty will make it appear unto the world by some such declaration as he will cause to be print and publish how much he detest the atheism and heresy of Vorstius and all those that maintain favour and cherish they this be my charge which if I have fail to perform I have fail in my duty both towards the service of GOD which be now in question as also towards the honour of the king my master who will always be ready to maintain the purity of the reform religion though it be with the profusion of his own blood the blood of his child and subject this protestation be make the state after some deliberation frame we a answer in these term that howsoever his majesty of great Britain have not yet receive that contentment which he may expect in this business of Vorstius nevertheless they do not doubt but that at the assembly of the state of Holland in February next his majesty shall receive entire satisfaction which answer give some life to our hope that at the say assembly of the state to be hold the fifteen day of the next month of February GOD will vouchsafe so to open the eye of those of Holland as that they may be able to discern what a cockatrice egg they hatch within their bosom and that see the smooth speech of Vorstius do but verify the old proverb there lurk a snake in the grass they will at that assembly resolve to purge their territory from the poison of his heresy we mention Holland because the other province namely Frizeland and Zealand and some part of Holland likewise be already so distaste with his heresy as of themselves they have desire Holland to banish he out of the country and certain we be no less sorry then amaze that the curator of Leyden as appear by a long letter which they have write to the state ambassador resident with we can have their understanding so stupefy as to have make choice of the person of Vorstius for a man well qualify to appease the schism and trouble of their church and university and as a apt instrument of peace for to show their blindness in this they need no other answer then the issue try the action see to our great grief it can be deny but that there have be more distraction of spirit and a great division in their state since the come of Vorstius then be for many year before witness so many book and accusation write against he and his answer thereunto witness also the protestation of a great number of professor of Leyden against he and many of the principal member as well province as town of the unite body of that state who have accuse he as before we have say so as if for that purpose only they bring he unto their university they must needs acknowledge it have have a very unhappy success have now finish the discourse of our whole proceed in this cause from the begin until this present it remain that we set down the reason which persuade we to engage ourselves in in a business of this nature but we have do that already although but summary and by the way for in that place where we make mention of the book of Vorstius which be bring into our kingdom we yield three reason which move we to take this cause to heart first the zeal of God glory to who we be so much bind second charity towards our next neighbour and ally and three the just reason we have to fear the like infection within our own dominion as concern the glory of God if the subject of Vorstius his heresy have not be ground upon question of a high quality than touch the number and nature of the sacrament the point of justification of merit of purgatory of the visible head of the church or any such matter as be in controversy at this day betwixt the papist and we nay more if he have meddle only with the nature and work of GOD as the schoolman speak if we say he have soar no high pitch although we shall have be very sorry to see such heresy begin to take root among our ally and ancient confederate nevertheless we do free profess that in that case we shall never have trouble ourselves with the business in such fashion and with that fervency as hitherto we have do but this Vorstius mount aloft like a anti-s John with the wing of the eagle up to the heaven and to the throne of GOD dispute of his sacred and ineffable essence which be to be tremble at and admire but not to be search into confound infinity one of the proper attribute of GOD and immensity sometime apply to creature the essence and substance with the dispute of a first and second creation immediate and mediate make GOD to be and change eternity into eviternitie teach eternity to consist of a number of aage and in the end as a swear enemy not only to divinity but even to all philosophy both human and natural deny God to be and void of quality but have in some sort with horror be it speak that be to say some kind of diversity or multiplicity in himself yea even a begin of a certain mutability let the world then judge whether we have not occasion hereupon to be move not only as one that make profession of thereformed religion but as a christian at large yea even as a theist or a man that acknowledge a GOD or as a platonic philosopher at the least second for the charity which we owe to our neighbour and ally the charity of every Christian ought to extend to all man but especial towards they that be of the household of say the state then be not only our confederate but the principal bond of our conjunction be our uniformity in the true religion we have reason to admonish they not to permit such dangerous heresy to spring and take root among they which be once suffer can produce no other effect than the danger of their soul a rent betwixt they and all other christian church and at the last a rapture and division in their temporal state which next under God can be maintain by nothing but unity to which resolution we be the rather induce by the example of diverse other province under the dominion of the say state who do accuse Vorstius and persuade holland to send he away out of their country as before we have declare it be true that if Vorstius have be a native of Holland as John of Leyden be it have be sufficient for we to have give they a general warning of the danger and then to have refer it unto themselves to take such course therein as to they shall seem convenient but this Vorstius be a stranger and send for out of another country to instruct their youth he can challenge no such privilege by reason of his birth but that the state may lawful discharge he whensoever they please and for his profession it be without doubt less dangerous to suffer a thousand lay heretic to live in a common wealth for that be but matter of policy so long as they offend not in their speech and seduce not other then to have so much as one doctor that may poison the youth for the vessel will long time after of that liquor wherewith it be first season and what shall become of the little brook if their fountain be corrupt and from hence be derive our three reason which persuade we to meddle in this business for if general the youth of those country our near neighbour shall happen to be infect in what danger then be we especial see so many of the young sort of our subject do repair for learning sake to the university of Leyden a university of long time famous but so much the more renown for that within our remembrance it have be adorn with those two excellent personage Scaliger and junius it be furthermore to be note that the spiritual infection of heresy be so much more dangerous than the bodily infection of the plague by how much the soul be more noble than the body which cause the apostle s John when enter into a bath he meet there by chance cerinthus the heretic to turn back again upon the sudden for fear of infection now if that great apostle the belove of Christ do so much fear the infection of heresy as himself have give we a warning in one of his epistle bid he not God speed have not we then much more cause to fear the corruption of the youth of our own kingdom but we very well know that some will say Vorstius be not right understand that some consequence be violent wrest out of his word contrary to the intention of the author that those thing which he propound scholastical by way of question shall not be take for his own resolution and admit pearaduenture he may have speak in some phrase not wary enough yet that be but logomachy contention about word and ought not to be impute unto he for heresy and besy that in his last work he have sufficient purge himself from all calumny and renounce all manner of heresy to the first objection we answer that we never accuse he by consequence but that we find his own word and sentence full of heresy to the second concern his question or disquisition as he term they we say that in doubtful matter and where a man may resolve either one way or other without danger of make shipwreck of faith it be not only tolerable but very commendable to propound question or argument at leastwise in school but to devise new question upon the principal article of our faith to enter not only into the secret cabinet of GOD but to intrude ourselves into his essence to pry into his most inward part and like the physician of Pantagruel to visit with torchlight all the most hide place in the essence of GOD we may bold pronounce all these thing tend to destruction and nothing sure to edification s. Augustine speak against the curiosity of those people who will needs know what GOD do before he make the fabric of the world the jew during their integrity do bear another manner of reverence to the divinity who think themselves dead if once they shall see god. and their great lawgiver Moses can obtain no more notwithstanding his humble and instant request then to see the hinder part of god. so as to call into question or to make doubt of these high point of the essence of GOD of the trinity of the bypostaticall union in the person of CHRIST or to speak of they in other term then the church of GOD have uniform establish and in all aage successive approve as it be contain in all the orthodox creed and declare in the four first counsel be by no mean lawful and to make any question or disquisition upon these high mystery be as much in effect as to make a contrary conclusion and such a disquisition deserve the punishment of the inquision it be not good to jest with saint much less therefore with GOD and one of the first verse which our little child be teach be this let the secret of God alone and be not too curious to inquire into heaven for what difference be there I pray you to say it may be that such a lady be a whore or that there be probable argument to persuade we that she be such a one or to say absolute that she be a whore and we imagine Vorstius will not hold he for his friend that shall say it be a matter very disputable whether Vorstius be a damnable heretic and shall go quick to hell yea or no not that he do believe he to be such a one but that there be many argument probable enough to persuade a man to take he for no less the nature of man through the transgression of our first parent have lose freewill and retain not now any shadow thereof save a inclination to evil those only except who god of his mere grace have sanctify and purge from this original leprosy insomuch as it be a very perilous thing to set abroach these new and dangerous question although they be accompany with good answer for the great part of the world follow the footstep of our first parent be natural incline to choose the evil and to leave that which be good and therefore the divine poet Du Bartas speak of the destruction of Sodom and loath to name the sin for which it be destroy say thus enseigner for fear that in offend of good people tender ear I rather seem to teach they then to wish they to forbear and there be a report I know not how true it be that Bellarmine book of controversy be not very well receive in Italy because his objection be too strong and his answer too weak in which quality as also in one other whereof we will speak anon Vorstius have a certain tincture of Bellarmine to the three objection where it be allege that perhaps he have not be wary enough in some of his phrase of speech and that it be but contention for word to that we answer as before we have say that it be in no sort lawful to speak of those great mystery of the essence of God of the trinity of the hypostatical union of nature in the person of Christ or any such high point unless we use the same phrase and manner of speech which the church of GOD have always use in speak of the say mystery they that will talk of Chanaan must use the language of Chanaan and the son of Aaron be severe punish for presume to bring strange fire unto the altar by the difference of less than one letter between Siboleth and Shibboleth the ten tribe of Israel can discern their friend from their foe and that by the pronunciation only and the like difference of one little letter betwixt homoousion and homoiousion serve to make a distinction betwixt the enemy of Christ in the east church and the church orthodoxal as concern the four and last excuse namely that Vorstius have in his last book sufficient purge himself from these calumny and renounce all heresy our answer be that we will very hearty request the state in their next assembly serious and advise to consider and observe the style which he use in his writing and excuse and then shall they be able to judge what kind of spirit it be that guide his pen for to begin with the preface of his book entitle his christian and modest answer he make there so light reckon of his question beforementioned as if it be but about the tale of tobies dog for in the second page of his say preface these be
