his word that be to say every man be a liar yea more vain than vanity itself God only be true etc. which see we ought ever humble to acknowledge in all great and weighty cause most of all ought we to confess it in the most holy cause of our faith insomuch as we shall not therefore easy condemn every thing which at the first seem strange yea false and absurd unto our ear nor on the contrary side ought we forthwith to approve and that with a opinion of precise necessity whatsoever be common receive especial in matter abstruse and intricate whereof the knowledge be not necessary to salvation in such point as these if any man shall say that such a king or prince howsoever otherwise most godly and religious yea that many such king and prince nay I will not except bishop or the like doctor of the church have in some sort err I be of opinion he shall not give any just cause of offence either to the majesty of king or to the dignity of prince and bishop so as he be always ready modest to yield a reason for that which he shall affirm in which word he maintain two principle first that every man be a liar aswell in matter of faith as in any thing else and next that we must not ever esteem the vulgar opinion and that which be general receive in matter of faith to be the true nor always condemn every opinion for absurd which at the first seem unto we uncouth and new now we pray you observe that this man be not accuse of small escape and therefore be not charge with lesser peccadillo than those which before we have mention it necessary follow that in his excuse he must understand the same point whereof he be accuse and we hope by the mercy of GOD that no christian we speak in this particular as well for the papist as for ourselves shall ever be find to err in any of those main point at the least we will answer by the grace of God for one of those king who he name in general and as for his new opinion which he will so glad vent abroad the ancient faith need not be change like a old garment either in substance or fashion furthermore in the three page of his preface he use these word neither be many man always rich in knowledge then some one man let not therefore any one man arrogate all thing to himself nor let the great multitude envy a particular man for have some singularity more than his fellow the true principle and foundation of the error of the anabaptist take away by this mean all manner of government from the church for have first overthrow the monarchical power of the pope he sweep away next all manner of power both aristocratical and democratical from the church clean contrary to the apostle institution which ordain that the spirit of the prophet shall be subject to the prophet for if one particular man may take upon he such a singularity as this how shall he be subject to general Nationall and synodical counsel for straight will he say unto they sir you have no authority to judge i for I have a singular gift above you all and in the five page these be his word for I be absolute persuade that it be never his majesty meaning nor ever will be either direct or indirect by himself or by other in any sort to over-rule another man conscience which even the apostle never challenge to themselves nor do or will his majesty ever seek to tie our faith to any human authority whereby he be plain discover to be resolve not to be subject in any sort to the judgement of the church in those matter whereof he be accuse for he know too well that the ancient church have establish upon necessary consequence draw from the holy scripture both a form of belief and a form of speech concern the holy mystery aforesaid and this be the reason why he will not in these point submit himself to the judgement of any mortal man but upon this occasion in the seven page of his preface maintain his christian liberty in this manner I who curious make a separation betwixt the judgement of man and the divine mystery and especial according to christian liberty do sometime more narrow look into the bold suposition of the schoolman as if the school divine have be too venturous to explain and to defend the article aforesaid already so establish by the church but we may true wish in that point as Bellarmine do touch will God the schoolman have always so err for in the main ground of christian religion they be worthy of all commendation read Aquinas against the gentile but in matter of controversy where they be to flatter the pope in his resolution and to avow the new ordinance and tradition of their church there they yield alas unto the iniquity of the time and the mystery of iniquity which be even then in work get likewise the upper hand over they and as for this christian liberty which he do urge so much certain he do it with no other intention but only under this fair pretext to have the better mean and with more safety to abuse the world for christian liberty be never mean in the holy scripture but only in matter indifferent or when it be take for our deliverance from the thraldom of the law or from the burden of human tradition and in that sense s. Paul speak in his epistle to the why be you burden with tradition but to abuse christian liberty in presume to propound a new doctrine unto the world in point of the high and holy mystery of GOD be a most audacious rashness and a impudent arrogancy concern which s. Paul say though a angel from heaven preach unto you otherwise then that which we have preach unto you let he be accurse and saint John likewise command we that we shall not so much as say God speed to that man which shall bring we any other doctrine as we have observe before now to show that he be a forger of new opinion by which he will fain make himself singular see but his word immediate precede those which a little before we mention where he boast and be wonderful in love with a new name which he have take upon himself that be to say a main pure gospeler although indeed the word pure be never yet take in a good part for among the ancient heretic there be a sect that call themselves Catharoi and there be also another sect among the anabaptist that be call puritan from whence the precisian of our kingdom who out of self-will and fancy refuse to conform themselves to the order of our church have borrow their name and for the word gospeler although it have be assume in diverse place by some of our religion yet have it this ill fortune that it be more usual receive in those part of Hungary and Boheme where there be such infinite diversity of sect agree in nothing but in their union against the pope then in any other place the holy scripture itself in the act of the apostle mention the name of christian and the ancient primitive church do attribute unto the faithful the name of catholic and orthodox so as for such a fellow as Vorstius to affect new title for his religion it have sure no good relish his intention without doubt be no other then by this mean to make a distinction and in time a rapture betwixt himself and the orthodox professor of our religion and for proof that he be steadfast resolve to persist in all these novelty and not to retract any thing of that which he have write see what he say in the last page save one of his say preface for I be of opinion to use the word of that great Erasmus that there be nothing to be find in my book that can make any man the worse that read they as for his book which follow this preface it verify the proverb a cover fit for such a dish for it be so full of distinction and sophistical evasion so stuff with as it wear in some sort in my sense and such word as these as even in that point he have also a tincture of Bellarmine but God be unity itself and verity be one and naked and in our usual manner of speech we call it the simple verity but never be it yet call the double verity we have think good to set down here two place of his say book that thereby the reader may judge of the rest whereof one be in the twelve page in these word the argument which be use both by the father and by the modern divine for the eternal generation of Christ be either sophistical or frivolous these word as he say he be charge to have use and he can bethink he of any other evasion but to add the word some argument etc. now we shall desire thou good reader here to observe that this man condemn some argument which the father have gather out of the holy scripture to prove the eternal generation of Christ as deceitful and frivolous he will be sure howsoever not to allege any other argument either out of the father or of his own brain which shall be strong than those which he have reject and in the same fashion he behave himself throughout his whole book for we show you this but for a scantling in the other place he direct deny that ever he affirm in his other book that fear and desperation be incident to God his word be these in the eight page for I do never so much as name fear and desperation in any place and yet nevertheless let any man look upon his other book tract Theol. pag. 114. and pag. 450. and he shall find two several discourse of a good length concern these two point herein have no other shift he betake himself to a absolute and flat negative but to the intent that the reader may judge of his manner of speak through his whole last book entitle a christian and modest answer and how he play the sophister therein we have set down diverse of his phrase in manner of a table which we have cause to be extract out of his say book  1. estne and presence 1 1 pag. 22. lin. 23. Thesin 2 2 pag. 4. l. 19 3 3 pag. 22. l. 26. hypothesin be God essential immense and every where present it be in no place clear set down that the substance of God be simple and every way immense and infinite and there be many place in the holy scripture which I do not say clear affirm yet seem to affirm the contrary in the mean time it be one thing to seem and another thing to be indeed yet in my own sense I answer thus simple and positive it be howsoever not in that special manner and sort as the schoolman hold which opinion nevertheless I do not say be false but I say it have hitherto be somewhat weak prove and therefore in some sort doubtful  2. estne in 1 1 pag. 2. l. 28. 2 2 pag. 23. l. 12. 3 3 pag. 2. l. 29. and be there quantity in God there be but not a natural quantity but a supernatural nevertheless not possible to be perceive by we but mere spiritual be God infinite every thing that have a be have a certain and definite essence which may be apply in some kind unto God that God can right in every sense be say to be infinite see that infiniteness which be opposite to certain definitenesse can indeed be attribute unto God be God in a place he be but not in a natural place but which God after his divine manner do fill  5. 1 1 pag. 3. l. 34. 2 2 pag. 15. l. 6. have God a body if we will speak proper he have none yet be it no absurdity speak improper to ascribe a body unto God that be to say as the word body be take improper and general and yet not very absurd for a true substance in a large signification which be figurative and improper or if you will abusive be God compound of matter and form by no mean speak proper although it be true in a certain improper sense or if you please by a certain by the which the word body and as it be or as it be a composition may sometime seem to some and not without cause to be fit attribute to God be God unchangeable in his will as he be in his essence he be not unchangeable in his will as he be in his essence that be not alike unchangeable in the one as he be in the other  8. 1 1 pag 7. l. 8. be God subject to accident not to any true accident although God do by his free will take to himself and into himself certain accident so call in the large sense 1 1 pag. 7. l. 24. 2 2 pag. 8. l. 9 and 3 3 pag. 7. l. 22. do God conjecture of thing to come by discourse sometime in some sort he frame to himself a certain discourse and do as it be conjecture of thing uncertain but improper and metaphorical and without all imperfection and he do conjecture not in such sort as man do but after a mere divine manner the affection of love hatred etc. be they proper attribute unto God or not yes but so as you take it rather for a verity then for that property which be vulgar understand and know unto we no affection accompany with human infirmity be proper attribute unto God yet true and in his own kind proper that be to say as they be proper to his nature and not to we have the father a certain peculiar or as it be limit essence the word essence as well as have a very large signification and we may apply both of they safe in a good sense as well to the divine person as to the nature of the deity itself be those argument which the father have use to prove the eternal generation of Christ frivolous or no if this one word some be add to the begin of that position it be then true that the argument which the father have at any time apply to prove the eternal generation of Christ be either deceitful or frivolous whether do God see thing past and present more certain than thing to come note here my modesty in this word for in this place I deliver only a probable opinion and not a dogmatic assertion thing future contingent speak comparative may be say to be less certain even unto God than thing past and present by this may the reader manifest discern that there be nothing which a man speak in this fashion shall not be able to maintain and by this mean easy prove and certain his manner of excuse and evasion be frame just after the mould of the ancient heretic and namely of arrius and Paulus when they see themselves pinch with the argument of the orthodox church and have no power to resist the same also do more plain appear by a other little book which he have publish entitle theological position which book he have make of purpose to blind the world withal because they be indeed but the same thesis or position upon which he have dispute in his first wicked book that bear the title of God and his attribute for in the thesis themselves there be but little harm but in his disputation thereupon be couch all the horrible heresy and therefore in this book have he publish only his thesis which be justifiable and leave out his disputation upon the thesis wherein all the poison be contain it be moreover somewhat suspicious in such a taint person as he be that in a appendix which he have place at the end of his thesis he take occasion to name a number of heretic who be adversary to the doctrine of his thesis and those especial who have err concern the divinity humanity person or office of CHRIST as the ebionite cerinthian arrian praxians sabellian marcionite manichee docite apollinarist mennonite swenkfeldian nestorian monothelite eutychian monophysite jew millenary papist among which rabble he do not once make mention of Paulus nor of Photinius who succeed he as well in his bishopric as in his error yet nevertheless it be report that Vorstius in his heart be not very far from their erroneous opinion now in the preface of this little book he have take upon he very succinct to make answer to five article which he confess be lay to his charge by which answer in our opinion he discover himself very plain the first point be that he be once accuse as himself say of the Samosatenian heresy because he have sometime both write and receive letter from diverse of that sect which he confess he do indeed in his youth to this end that by that mean he may the more easy come by some of their book but that afterward he do forbear all correspondency with they first of all then we will be glad to know why he forget the heresy of in his appendix where he name so many other and yet confess in the preface of his say book that he himself be accuse of that error second to what end have he in his youth so great traffic with these heretic be it to enable he the better to confute they we hear he not say so much as indeed it be never his end sure this fellow will be a excellent cleanser of a pest-house for he fear no infection he dare handle any pitch and yet for all that the proverb be true he that walk in the sunshine shall be sunburn it follow then see his intention be not to arm himself against they that it must be of necessity to make himself worthy of their school the which he almost confess in the last word of his answer to that point where he say thus for I do not think it fit as many other do to rely in these case upon other man construction or rash to condemn any thing which concern matter of Faith to the second article of his accusation he confess that he give some of his Samosatenian book unto his scholar sure a goodly gift but the caution be pretty which he give withal unto they when he deliver they the book which be that they ought to read they with judgement not rash reject the doctrine common receive what a epithet be here for our holy orthodox faith to term it no otherwise then the doctrine common receive and as for his caution not rash to forsake the old doctrine it be no more than the turk will give unto any christian that shall sudden offer to become a Mahometist nay what Christian do ever solicit a pagan or heretic to be convert but with this caution who will persuade a man to receive the holy sacrament rash s. Paul command every man to examine himself diligent before he come to that holy table but on the other side a orthodox Christian will in this case have say to his scholar if you will read these wicked book read they with horror and detestation and with a intent to arm yourselves against such wile and subtlety of Satan and withal pray unto GOD to keep you constant in the holy catholic and orthodox faith that these heresy may have no power once to move you trust in his mercy and not in your own strength to the three article he confess that his scholar do publish book of the socinian heresy and his excuse be that it be without his knowledge but howsoever he condemn they not for have do it only this he say that they declare upon their oath they do not favour the heresy to the four point he confess that about ten year since he write a book for which title only a author so suspect as he be worthy of the faggot and all his excuse be that he write a epitome upon Bellarmine we doubt not but he do it for his recreation forsooth a pretty conceit yet it appear not by his word that he detest the subject of that book but say that no man can thereby conjecture what his opinion be of that argument no more than they can upon his epitome of Bellarmine which be likewise his work for to condemn it have be contrary to that which he avow in his other book never to repent himself of any thing that he have once write as already we have observe as for the five and last point he will neither confess nor deny the accusation only he say that a certain book entitle dominicus Lopez which be as we have hear a very blasphemous treatise be suppress by he for peace sake but he be so far from condemn it as that he allege the book have be maintain by other which in time shall appear two thing be here to be observe first that he suppress it for quietness sake not therefore for the wickedness of the subject the next that in his due time the truth thereof shall appear in which last point only we will willing join with he beseech our good GOD for his Christ sake that he will be please to discover the truth of this man intention as well for his own glory as to purge the scandal and to avoid the danger which may ensue unto Christendom by the darnel of heresy which he have sow it be therefore to be note that to all these five article his answer be so silly and weak as in three of they we have find he plain plead guilty blanch it only with some poor excuse and to the other two point his answer be doubtful yet neither condemn the act of his scholar nor the last wicked book call dominicus Lopez have now therefore brief lay open the subtlety frivolous distinction and excuse of the say Vorstius we will conclude this point with this protestation that if he have be our own subject we will have bid he Excrea spit out and force he to have produce and confess those wicked heresy that be root in his heart and in case he shall stand upon his negative we will enjoin he to say according to the ancient custom of the primitive church in the like case of heretic I renounce and from my soul detest they anathema Maranatha upon such and such heresy and not to say for peace sake I cause this book to be suppress and these book be to be read with great judgement and discretion s. Hierome like not that any man shall take it patient to be suspect of heresy and now to make a end of this discourse we do very hearty desire all good christian in general and my lord the state in particular to who the manage of this affair do most special belong to consider but two thing first what kind of people they be that slander we and our sincere intention in this cause and next what private interest we can possible have in respect of any worldly honour or advancement herein to engage ourselves in such sort as we have do concern the first point there be but three sort of people that seek to calumniate we upon this occasion that be to say either such as be infect with the same or the like heresy wherewith Vorstius be taint and and therefore do maintain the like cause or else such as be of the roman religion who in this confusion and liberty of prophesy will thrust in for a part conceive it more reasonable that their doctrine shall be tolerate by those of our religion than the doctrine of Vorstius or else such as for reason of state envy peradventure the good amity and correspondency which be betwixt we and the unite province touch our own interest the whole course of our life do sufficient witness that we have always be content with that portion which GOD have put into our hand without seek to invade the possession of any other beside in two of our book as well in our (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) as in the preface to our apology we have show the same inclination for in the first book speak of war we say that a king ought not to make any invasion upon another dominion until justice be first deny he and in the other book have show the usurpation of the pope above all the king and prince of Christendom our conclusion be that we will never go about to persuade they to assault he within his dominion but only to resume and preserve their own just privilege from his violent intrusion so as thanks be to GOD both our theoric and practic agree well together to clear we from this unjust and slanderous imputation and as for the state in particular it be very unlikely that we who have all our life time hold so strict a amity with they as for their defence we have be content to expose the life of many of our subject of both nation will now practice against then state and that upon so poor a subject as Vorstius especial that so damnable a thing can ever enter into our heart as under the veil and pretext of the glory of GOD to plot the advancement of our own private deseigne the reason which induce we to meddle in this business we have already declare we leave it now to his own proper judge to consider what a nursling they foster in their bosom a stranger breed in the socinian heresy as it be say often time accuse of heresy by the church of Germany one that have write so wicked and scandalous book maintain and serious protest in the preface of his apology to the state for the liberty of prophesy and twice or thrice insist upon that liberty in the preface of his modest answer a dangerous and pernicious liberty or rather licentiousness open a gap to all rapture schism and confusion in the church yea have have some disciple that be heretic themselves and other that accuse he of heresy and though there be no other cause then the silly and idle shift wherewith he seek to defend himself in his last book it be enough to convince he either to have maintain a bad cause and in that respect worthy of a far great punishment then to be put by his place of professor or at the least to be a person unworthy of the name of a professor in so famous a university for have so weak maintain a cause that be just for our part GOD be our witness we have no quarrel against his person he be a stranger bear far from our dominion he be a German and it be well know that all Germany be our friend and the most part of the great prince there be either near ally unto we or our confederate he do outward profess the same religion which we do he have write against Bellarmine and have not mention we either in speech or writing for any thing we know but with all the honour and respect that may be GOD know the worst that we do wish he be that he may sincere return into the high beat pathway of the catholic and orthodoxal Faith and for my lord the state see we have discharge our conscience we will now refer the manage of the whole action unto their own discretion for we be so far from prescribe they any rule herein as we shall be very well content so as the business be well do that there be even no mention at all make of our intercession in their public act or record their manner of proceed we leave absolute to their own wisdom so as CHRIST be preach let they use their own form in the name of god. for we desire that GOD shall so judge we at the last day as we affect not in this action any worldly glory beseech the creator so to open their eye to illuminate their understanding direct their resolution and above all to kindle their zeal sanctify their affection and at the last so to bless their action and their proceed in this cause as the issue thereof may tend to his glory to the comfort and solace of the faithful to the honour of our religion to the confusion and extirpation at the least profligation of heresy and in particular to the corroboration of the union of the say province a remonstrance for the right of king and the INDEPENDANCE of their crown against a oration of the most illustrious card of perron pronounce in the chamber of the three estate jan. 15. 1615. the preface I have no humour to play the curious in a foreign common wealth or unrequested to carry any hand in my neighbour affair it have more congruity with royal dignity whereof God have give i the honour to prescribe law at home for my subject rather than to furnish foreign kingdom and people with counsel howbeit my late entire affection to k. Henry 4 of happy memory my most honour brother and my exceed sorrow for the most detestable parricide act upon the sacred person of a king so complete in all heroical and princely virtue as also the remembrance of my own danger incur by the practice of conspiracy flow from the same source have wrought i to sympathize with my friend in their grievous occurrent no doubt so much more dangerous as they be less apprehend and feel of king themselves even when the danger hang over their own head upon who in case the power and virtue of my advertisement be not able effectual to work at least many million of child and people yet unborn shall bear i witness that in these danger of the high nature and strain i have not be defective and that neither the subversion of state nor the murder of king which may unhappy betide hereafter shall have so free passage in the world for want of timely advertisement before for touch my particular my rest be up that one of the maine for which God have advance i upon the lofty stage of the supreme throne be that my word utter from so eminent a place for God honour most shameful traduce and vilify in his own deputy and lieutenant may with great facility be conceive now touch France fair be the hope which i conceive of the state assemble in parliament at Paris that call to mind the murder of their noble king and the war of the league which follow the pope fulmination as when a great storm of hail pour down after a thunder-cracke and a world of writing address to justify the parricide and the dethroning of king they will have join head heart and hand together to hammer out some apt and wholesome remedy against so many fearful attempt and practice to my hope be add no little joy when I be give to understand the three estate have prefer a article or bill the tenor and substance whereof be concern the mean whereby the people may be unwitch of this pernicious opinion that pope may toss the french king his throne like a tennis ball and that kill of king be a act meritorious to the purchase of the crown of martyrdom but in fine the project be encounter with success clean coutrary to expectation for this article of the three estate like a sigh of liberty breathe her last serve only so much the more to enthral the crown and to make the bondage more grievous and sensible then before even as those medicine which work no ease to the patient do leave the disease in much worse term so this remedy invent and tender by the three estate do only exasperate the present malady of the state for so much as the operation and virtue of the wholesome remedy be overmatch with peccant humour then stir by the force of thwart and cross opposition yea much better have it be the matter have not be stir at all then after it be once on foot and in motion to give the truth leave to lie gasp and sprawl under the violence of a foreign faction for the opinion by which the crown of king be make subject unto the pope will and power be then avow in a most honourable assembly by the averment of a prelate in great authority and of no less learning he do not plead the cause as a private person but as one by representation that stand for the whole body of the clergy be there applaud and second with approbation of the nobility no resolution take to the contrary or in bar to his plea. after praise and thanks from the pope follow the print of his eloquent harangue or oration make in full parliament a set discourse maintain king to be deposeable by the pope if he speak the word the say oration be not only print with the king privilege but be likewise address to i by the author and orator himself who presuppose the read thereof will forsooth drive i to say lord cardinal in this high subject your honour have satisfy i to the full all this poise in the balance of equal judgement why may not i true and free affirm the say estate assemble in parliament have set royal majesty upon a doubtful chance or leave it rest upon uncertain term and that now if the doctrine there maintain by the clergy shall bear any pawme it may lawful be doubt who be king in France for I make no question he be but a titular king that reign only at a other discretion and who princely head the pope have power to bare of his regal crown in temporal matter how can one be sovereign that may be fleece of all his temporalty by any superior power but let man at a near sight mark the pith and marrow of the article propose by the three estate and they shall soon perceive the skilful architect thereof aim only to make their king a true and real king to be recognize for sovereign within his own realm and that kill their king may no long pass the muster of work acceptable to God but by the vehement instance and strong current of the clergy and noble this be bear down as a pernicious article as a cause of schism as a gate which open to all sort of heresy yea there it be maintain tooth and nail that in case the doctrine of this article may go for currant doctrine it must follow that for many aage pass in sequence the church have be the kingdom of Antichrist and the synagogue of Satan the pope upon so good issue of the cause have reason i trow to address his letter of triumph unto the nobility and clergy who have so far aprroove themselves faithful to his holiness and to vaunt withal that he have nip christian king in the crown that he have give they check with mate through the magnanimous resolution of this courageous nobility by who brave make head the three estate have be so valiant force to give ground in a scornful reproach he qualify the deputy of the three estate I have receive advertisement from diverse part that in the pope letter to the nobitie these word be extant howsoever they have be leave out in the impression and raze out of the copy of the say letter nebulone a sort or a number of knave the very dregs of the base vulgar a pack of people presume to personate well affect subject and man of deep understanding and to read their master a learned lecture now it be no wonder that in so good a office and loyal carriage towards their king the three estate have outgo the clergy for the clergy deny themselves to have any rank among the subject of the king they stand for a sovereign out of the kingdom to who as to the lord Paramount they owe suit and service they be bind to advance that monarchy to the body whereof they proper appertain as part or member as elsewhere I have write more at large but for the nobility the king right arm to prostitute and set as it be to sale the dignity of their king as if the arm shall give a thrust unto the head i say for the nobility to hold and maintain even in parliament their king be liable to deposition by any foreign power or potentate may it not pass among the strange miracle and rare wonder of the world for that once grant this consequence be good and necessary that in case the king once lawful depose shall stand upon the defensive and hold out for his right he may then lawful be murder let i then here free profess my opinion and this it be that now the french nobility may seem to have some reason to disrobe themselves of their title and to transfer they by resignation unto the three estate for that body of that three estate alone have carry a right noble heart in as much as the can neither be tickle with promise nor terrify by threaten from resolute stand to those fundamental point and reason of state which most concern the honour of their king and the security of his person of all the clergy the man that have most abandon or set his honour to sale the man to who France be least oblige be the lord cardinal of perron a man otherwise inferior to few in matter of learning and in the grace of a sweet style this man in two several oration whereof the one be pronounce before the nobility the other have audience before the three estate have set his best wit on work to draw that doctrine into all hatred and infamy which teach king to be indeposeable by the pope to this purpose he term the same doctrine a breeder of schism a gate that open to make way and to give entrance unto all heresy in brief a doctrine to be hold in so high a degree of detestation that rather than he and his fellow-bishop will yield to the sign thereof they will be content like martyr to burn at a stake at which resolution or obstinacy rather in his opinion I be in a manner amaze more than I can be move for the like bravado in many other forasmuch as he be many year together a follower of the late king even when the king follow a contrary religion and be depose by the pope as also because not long before in a certain assembly hold at the jacobin in Paris he withstand the pope nuntio to his face when the say nuntio labour to make this doctrine touch the pope temporal sovereignty pass for a article of Faith but in both oration he sing a contrary song and from his own mouth pass sentence of condemnation against his former course and profession i fuppose not without solid iudgeman as one that herein have well accommodate himself to the time for as in the reign of the late king he dare not offer to broach this doctrine such be his fore-wit so now he be bold to proclaim and publish it in parliament under the reign of the say king son who tender year and late succession to the crown do make he lie the more open to injury and the more facile to be circumvent such be now his afterwisedome of these two oration that make in presence of the nobility he have for fear of incur the pope displeasure cautelous suppress for therein he have be somewhat prodigal in affirm this doctrine maintain by the clergy to be but problematical and in take upon he to avouch that catholic of my kingdom be bind to yield i the honour of obedience whereas on the other side he be not ignorant how this doctrine of depose prince and king the pope hold for mere necessary and approve not by any mean allegiance to be perform unto i by the catholic of my kingdom yea if credit may be give unto the abridgement of his other oration publish wherein he parallel the pope power in receive honour in the name of the church with the power of the venetian duke in receive honour in the name of that most renown rebublike no marvel that when this oration be dispatch to the press he command the same to be geld of this clause and other like for fear of give his holiness any offensive distaste his pleasure therefore be and content withal that his oration impart to the three estate shall be put in print and of his courtesy he vouchsafe to address unto i a copy of the same which after i have peruse i forthwith well perceive what and how great discrepance there be between one man that perorate from the ingenuous and sincere disposition of a sound heart and a other that flaunt in flourish speech with inward check of his own conscience for every where he contradict himself and seem to be afraid lest man shall pick out his right meaning first in 12. several passage the l. card. seem to speak against his own conscience pag. 85. he grant this question be not hither to decide by the holy scripture or by the decree of the ancient church or by the analogy of other ecclesiastical proceed and nevertheless he confident do affirm that whosoever maintain this doctrine to be wicked and abominable that pope have no power to put king by their supreme throne they teach man to believe there have not be any church for many aage past and that indeed the church be the very synagogue of Antichrist second he exhort his hearer to hold this doctrine at least for problematical and not necessary and yet herein he call they to all humble submission unto the judgement of the pope and clergy by who the cause have be already put out of all question as out of all hunger and cold three he do aver in case this article be authorize it make the pope in good consequence to be the Antichrist pag. 99 and yet he grant that many of the french be tolerate by the pope to dissent in this point from his holiness provide their doctrine be not propose as necessary and material to faith as if the pope in any sort give toleration to hold any doctrine contrary to his own and most of all that doctrine which by consequence infer himself to be the Antichrist four he protest forwardness to undergo the flame of martyrdom rather than to sign this doctrine which teach king crown to sit fast on their head then to be stir by any papal power whatsoever and yet say withal the pope wink at the french by his toleration to hold this dogmatic point for problematical and by this mean the martyrdom that he affect in this cause will prove but a problematical martyrdom whereof question may grow very well whether it be to be muster with grievous crime or with phrenetical passion of the brain or with deserve punishment five he denounce anathema discharge malediction like hailshot against parricide of king and yet elsewhere he lay himself open to speak of king only so long as they stand king but who do not know that a king depose be no long king and so that limb of Satan which murder Henry the 3 then un-kinged by the pope do not stab a king to death six he do not allow a king to be make away by murder and yet he think it not much out of the way to take away all mean whereby he may be able to stand in defence of his life seventh pag. 95.97 he abhor kill of king by apposted throat-cutting for fear lest body and soul shall perish in the same instant and yet he do not mislike their kill in a pitch field and to have they slaughter in a set battle for he presuppose no doubt out of his charitable mind that by this mean the soul of a poor king so dispatch out of the way shall instant fly up to heaven eight he say a king depose retain still a certain internal habitude and politic impression by virtue and efficacy whereof he may be once reform and become a new man be restore to the lawful use and practice of regality whereby he will bear we in hand that when a foreign prince have invade and ravenous seize the kingdom into his hand he will not only take pity of his predecessor to save his life but will also prove so kind-hearted upon fight of his repentance to restore his kingdom without fraud or guile nine he say every where in his discourse that he deal not in the cause otherwise then as a problematical discourser and without any resolution one way or other and yet with might and main he contend for the opinion that leave the state and crown of king controulable by the pope refute objection propound the authority of pope and council by name the Lateran council under Innocent 3 as also the consent of the church and to cross the church judgement be in his opinion to bring in schism and to leave the world without a church for many hundred year together which to my understanding be to speak with resolution and without all hesitation ten he acknowledge none other cause of sufficient validity for the depose of a king beside heresy apostasy and infidelity nevertheless that pope have power to displace king for heresy and apostasy he prove by example of king who the pope have curb with deposition not for heresy but for matrimonial cause for civil pretence and for lack of capacity eleventh he allege every where passage as well of holy scripture as of the father and modern history but so impertinent and with so little truth as hereafter we shall cause to appear that for a man of his deep learning and knowledge it seem not possible so to speak out of his judgement last whereas all this have be huddle and heap together into one mass to curry with the pope yet he suffer diverse point to fall from his lip which may well distaste his holiness in the high degree as by name where he prefer the authority of the council before that of the pope and make his judgement inferior to the judgement of the french as in fit place hereafter shall be show again where he represent to his hearer the decree of pope and council already pass concern this noble subject and yet affirm that he do not debate the question but as a Questionist and without resolution as if a cardinal shall be afraid to be positive and to speak in peremptory strain after pope and council have once decide the question or as if a man shall perorate upon hazard in a cause for the honour whereof he will make no difficulty to suffer martyrdom add hereunto that his lordship have always take the contrary part heretofore and this total must needs arise that before the three estate his lip look one way and his conscience another all these point by the discourse which be to follow and by the rip up of his oration which by God assistance i will undertake tend to the reproach of king and the subversion of kingdom i confident speak it shall be make manifest yet do i not conceive it can any way make for my honour to enter the list against a cardinal for i be not ignorant how far a cardinal hat come under the crown and sceptre of a king for well i wot unto what sublimity the scripture have exalt king when it style they god whereas the dignity of a cardinal be but a late upstart invention of man in the preface to my apology as i have elsewhere prove but i have embark myself in this action move thereunto first by the common interest of king in the cause itself then by the l. cardinal who speak not in this oration as a private person but as one represent the body of the clergy and nobility by who the cause have be win and the garland bear away from the three estate again by my own particular because he be please to take i up for a sour of dissension and a persecutor under who the church be hardly able to fetch her breath yea for one by who the catholic of my kingdom be compel to endure all sort of punishment and withal he term this article of the three estate a monster with a fish tail that come swim out of England last of all by the present state of France because France be now reduce to so miserable term that it be now become a crime for a Frenchman to stand for his king it be a necessary duty of her neighbour to speak in her cause and to make trial whether they can put life into the truth now die and ready to be bury by the power of violence that it may resound and ring again from remote region i have no purpose once to touch many pretty toy which the ridge of his whole book be sow withal such be his allegation of Pericles agesilaus Aristotle Minos the druid the french lady Hannibal Pindarus and poetical fable all resemble the red and blue flower that pester the corn when it stand in the field where they be more noy some to the grow crop then beautiful to the behold eye such petty matter nothing at all beseem the dignity of the assembly and of the main subject or of the orator himself for it be no decorum to enter the stage with a Pericles in his mouth but with the sacred name of God nor shall he have marshal the passage of a royal poet after the example of a heathen orator neither will i give any touch to his conceit of the roman conquest 2 pag. 4. which the l. cardinal bestow in the list of God grace and temporal blessing as a recompense of their zeal to the service and worship of idol as if God be a recompencer of wickedness or as if the forcible eject of tenant out of their farm and other possession may be reckon among the blessing of matter of faith and in case man shall believe the contradictory of the say point they be not bind for such belief to undergo the solemn curse of the church and the loss of communion whereas Aristotle (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) sound both one thing (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) provide the word (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) or do stand before as of who all school have borrow their term have teach we that every proposition be contradictory bind the believer thereof to anathema and loss of communion there be a confuse heap or bundle of other like toy which my purpose be to pass over in silence that i may now come to cast anchor as it be in the very bottom and substance of the cause a remonstrance for the right of king and the INDEPENDANCE of their crown against a oration of the most illustrious cardinal of perron pronounce in the chamber of the three estate the 15. of januar. 1615. the l. cardinal even in the first passage of his oration have lay a firm foundation that ecclesiastic in France be more deep oblige to the king than the nobility and three estate his reason because the clergy do sweet enjoy their dignity and promotion with all their infinite wealth of the king mere grace without all danger and with fair immunity whereas the other two order hold their office by a chargeable and burdensome title or tenure even to the great expense of their blood and of their substance but see now how loose and weak a frame he have erect and pin together upon his firm and solid foundation the three estate be to lay all care to provide remedy against apposted cutthroat upon the clergy and the say remedy as he bold affirm must be derive from the law of conscience which may carry a effectual act or operative efficacy upon the soul and not from civil or temporal punishment now this consequence limp like a lame cripple after the premise for it be no usual and common matter to see man that be deep in obligation perform their duty and covenant with most fidelity again be it grant the clergy have well hitherto demonstrate their careful watch over the life and honour of their prince yet be it not for spiritual punishment thunder by ecclesiastic to bind the hand of the civil magistrate nor to stop the current of temporal punishment which ordinary do carry a great force and virtue to the bridle of the wicked than the apprehension of God judgement the three estate therefore by who all the officer of France be proper represent as to who the administration of justice and protection of the king right and honour do appertain can deserve no blame in carry so watchful a eye by their wholesome remedy to provide for the safety of the king and for the dignity of his crown for if the clergy shall not stand to their tackle but shrink when it come to the push of their duty who shall charge themselves with careful foresight and prevention of mischief shall not the people now have not all the calamity which the three estate have seek provident to prevent have they not all spring from the clergy as from their proper and natural fountain from whence do the last civil war wherein a world of blood be not more profuse then prodigious and unnatural spill and wherein the parricide of king Henry 3 be impious and abominable commit from whence do those bloody war proceed but from the depose of the say king by the head of the church be they not prelate curate and confessor be they not ecclesiastic who partly by seditious preachment and partly by secret confession pour many a jar of oil upon this flame be not he that kill the forename king be not he one of the clergy be not with point in controversy now his rule indubitable be two the first it be not lawful to murder king for any cause whatsoever this he confirm by the example of Saul as he say depose from his throne who Constan Sess. 15. likewise he confirm the same by a decree of the council hold at Constance his other point indubitable the king of France be sovereign in all temporal sovereignty within the french kingdom and hold not by fealty either of the pope as have receive or oblige their crown upon such tenure and condition or of any other prince in the whole world which point nevertheless he take not for certain and indubitable but only according to human and historical certainty now a three point he make to be so full of controversy and so far within the circle of disputable question as it may not be draw into the rank of classical and authentical point for fear of make a certain point doubtful by shuffle and jumble therewith some point in controversy now the question so disputable as he pretend be this a christian prince break his oath solemn take to God both to live and to die in the catholic religion say this prince turn Arrian or mahometan fall to proclaim open war and to wage battle with jesus Christ whether may such a prince be declare to have lose his kingdom and who shall declare the subject of such a prince to be quit of their oath of allegiance the l. cardinal hold that such a prince may be lawful remove from his throne by the pope and by the council and suppose the contrary doctrine be the very quintessence or spirit of truth yet may it not in case of faith be have serve we with the same dish over and over again there we shall see the l. cardinal make way to the dispatch of king after deposition that Saul be not depose as he have presume that in the council this he affirm if any shall condemn or wrap under the solemn curse the abettor of the pope power to unking lawful and sovereign king the same shall run upon four dangerous rock of apparent incongruity and absurdity first he shall offer to force and entangle the conscience of many devout person for he shall bind they to believe and swear that doctrine pag. 14. the contrary whereof be believe of the whole church and have be believe by their predecessor second he shall overturn from top to bottom the sacred authority of holy church and shall set open a gate unto all sort of heresy by allow lay of unholy finger upon the holy censor for perfume three he shall make way to a schism not possible to be put by and avoid by any human providence for this doctrine be hold and profess by all other catholic how can we declare it repugnant unto God word how can we hold it impious how can we account it detestable but we shall renounce communion with the head and other member of the church yea we shall confess the church in all aage to have be the synagogue of Satan and the spouse of the devil last by work the establishment of this article which work a establishment of king crown he shall not only work the intend remedy for the danger of king out of all the virtue and efficacy thereof by weaken of doctrine out of all controversy in pack it up with a disputable question but likewise in stead of secure the life and estate of king he shall draw both into far great hazard by the train or sequence of war and other calamity which usual wait and attend on schism the l. cardinal spend his sharp and to prosecute worshipper of image with all extremity upon this occasion pope Gregory 2. then tread in the step of his predecessor when he perceive the city of Rome to be but weak provide of far the lord cardinal whereunto my answer for satisfaction be that degrade a emperor from his imperial dignity and reduce a city to revolt against her master that a man at last may carry the piece himself and make himself lord thereof be two several action of special difference if the freehold of the city have be convey to some other by the pope deprive the emperor as proprietary thereof this example may have challenge some credit at least in show but so to invade the city to his own use and so to seize on the right and authority of another what be it but open rebellion and notorious ambition for it be far from ecclesiastical censure when the spiritual pastor of soul forsooth pull the cloak of a poor sinner from his back by violence or cut his purse and thereby appropriate a other man good to his private use it be to be observe withal that when the emperor be not of sufficient strength and pope have power to beard and to brave emperor than these papal practice be first set on foot this emperor notwithstanding turn head and peck again his lieutenant enter Rome and Gregory 3. successor to this Gregory 2. be glad to honour the same emperor with style and title of his lord witness two several epistle of the say Gregory 3. write to Boniface and subscribe in this form Dat. 10. 7. date the ten alend of December in the reign of our most pious and religious lord Augustus Leo crown of God the great emperor in the ten year of his reign the l. cardinal with no less abuse allege pope Zacharie by who the french as he affirm be absolve of the oath of all egiance wherein they stand bind to Childeric their king and for this instance he stand upon the testimony of Paulus Aemilius and du Tillet a pair of late writer but by author more near that age wherein Childeric reign it be more true testify that it be a free and voluntary act of the french only ask the advice of pope Zacharie but require neither leave nor absolution ado bishop of Vienna in his chronicle have it after this manner the french follow the counsel of ambassador and of pope Zachary elect Pepin their king and establish he in the kingdom Trithemius in his abridgement of annal thus Childeric as one unfit for government be turn out of his kingdom with common consent of the estate and peer of the realm so advise by Zacharie pope of Rome Godfridus of Viterbe in the 17. part of his chronicle and guauguin in the life of Pepin affirm the same and be it not a easy matter to work Pepin by counsel to lay hold on the kingdom when he can not be hinder from fasten on the crown and have already seize it in effect howsoever he have not yet attain to the name of king moreover the rudeness of that nation then want knowledge and school either of divinity or of academical science be a kind of spur to make they run for counsel over the mountain which nevertheless in a cause of such nature they require not as necessary but only as decent and for fashion sake the Popealso for his part be well apay by this mean to draw Pepin unto his part as one that stand in some need of his aid against the Lombard and the more because his lord the emperor of Constantinople be then bring so low that he be not able to send he sufficient aid for the defence of his territory against his enemy but have Zacharie to deal plain not stand upon the respect of his own commodity more than upon the regard of God fear he will never have give counsel unto the servant under the pretend colour of his master dull spirit so to turn rebel against his master the law provide guardian or overseer for such as be not well in their wit they never deprive and spoil they of their estate they punish crime but not disease and infirmity by nature yea in France it be a very ancient custom when the king be trouble in his wit to establish a regent who for the time of the king disability may bear the burden of the kingdom affair so be the practice of that state in the case of Charles 6. when he fall into a frenzy who the pope notwithstanding his most grievous and sharp fit never offer to degrade and to be short what reason what equity will bear the child to be punish for the father debility yet such punishment be lay upon Childerics whole race and house who by this practice be all disinherit of the kingdom but shall we now take some view of the l. pag. 25. cardinal excuse for this exemplary fact the cause of Childerics depose as the l. cardinal say do near concern and touch religion for Childerics imbecility bring all France into danger to suffer a most woeful shipwreck of christian religion upon the barbarous and hostile invasion of the saracen admit now this reason have be of just weight and value yet consideration shall have be take whether some one or other of that royal stem and of the king own successor near of blood be not of better capacity to rule and manage that mighty State the fear of uncertain and accidental mischief shall not have drive they to fly unto the certain mischief of actual and effectual deposition they shall rather have set before their eye the example of Charles martel this pippin father who in a far more eminent danger when the saracen have already master and subdue a great part of France valiant encounter and withal defeat the saracen rule the kingdom under the title of steward of the king house the principal officer of the crown without affect or aspire to the throne for all that great step of advantage especial when the saracen be quite break and no long dreadful to the french nation in our own Scotland the sway of the kingdom be in the hand of wall during the time of Bruse his imprisonment in England who then be lawful heir to the crown this wall or vallas have the whole power of the kingdom at his beck and command his edict and ordinance to this day stand in full force by the deadly hatred of Bruse his mortal enemy it may be conjecture that he may have be provoke and inflame with to truss the kingdom in his talon and notwithstanding all these incitement he never assume or usurp other title to himself then of governor or administrator of the kingdom the reason he have not be bring up in this new doctrine and late discipline whereby the church be endow with power to give and to take away crown but now as the l. cardinal will bear the world in hand the state of king be bring to a very dead lift the pope forsooth must send his physician to know by way of inspection or some other course of art whether the king brain be crack or sound and in case there be find any debility of wit and reason in the king than the pope must remove and translate the crown from the weak brain to a strong and for the act of the stratagem the name of religion must be pretend ho these heretic begin to crawl in the kingdom order must be take they be not suffer by their multitude and swarm like locust or caterpillar to pester and poison the whole realm or in a case of matrimony thus ho marriage be a sacrament touch the order of matrimony and relgion be wound by this device not only the king vice but likewise his natural disease and infirmity be fetch into the circle of religion and the l. cardinal have not do himself right in restrain from a subject of the greek emperor to turn subject unto Charlemagne let i see but one town that Charlemagne recover from the greek emperor by his right and title to his empire in the west no the one shall say that in this age the pope take the dukedom of Milan from the french king or the city of Rome from the emperor of Germany because their predecessor in former aage have be right lord and governor of they both it be one of the pope ordinary and solemn practice to take away much after the manner of his give for as he give what he have not in his right and power to give or bestow upon other what be already their own even so he take away from king and emperor the possession which they have not in present hold and possession after this manner he take the west from the greek emperor when they hold nothing in the west and lay no claim to any city or town of the west empire and what shall we call this way of deprivation but spoil a naked man of his garment and kill a man already with mandate into France command the king to release the bishop of Apamia then detain in prison for contumelious word tend to the king defamation and speak to the king own head in very deed this pope have conceive a secret grudge and no light displeasure against king Philip before namely because the king have take upon he the collation of benefice and other ecclesiastical dignity upon which occasion the pope send letter to the king of this tenor and style fear God and keep his commandment we will have thou know that in spiritual and temporal cause thou be subject unto ourself that collate of benefice and prebend do not in any sort appertain to thy office and place that in case as keeper of the spiritualty thou have the custody of benefice and prebend in thy hand when they become void thou shall by sequestration reserve the fruit of the same to the use and benefit of the next incumbent and successor and in case thou have heretofore collate any we ordain the say collation to be mere void and so far as herein thou have proceed to the fact we revoke the say collation we hold they for heretic whosoever be not of this belief a legate come to Paris and bring these brave letter by some of the king faithful servant they be violent snatch and pull out of the legate hand by the earl of Artois they be cast into the fire the good king answer the pope and pay he in as good coin as he have send Philip by the grace of God king of the french to Boniface call and bear himself the sovereign bishop little greeting or none at all may thy exceed sottishness understand that in temporal cause we be not subject unto any mortal and earthly creature that collate of benefice and prebend by regal right appertain to our office and place that appropriate their fruit when they become void belong to ourself alone during their vacancy that all collation by we heretofore make or to be make hereafter shall stand in force that in the validity and virtue of the say collation we will ever courageous defend and maintain all incumbent and possessor of benefice and prebend so by we collate we hold they all for sot and senseless whosoever be not of this belief the pope incense herewith excommunicate the king but no man dare publish that censure or become bearer thereof the king notwithstanding the say proceed of the pope assemble his prelate baron and knight at Paris ask the whole assembly of who they hold their fee with all other the temporalty of the church they make answer with one voice that in the say matter they disclaim the pope and know none other lord beside his majesty mean while the pope work with Germany and the low country to stir they up against France but Philip send William of nogaret into Italy William by the direction and aid of Sciarra his life all this notwithstanding the king present after from the successor of Boniface receive very ample and gracious bull in which the memory of all the former passage and action be utter abolish
