on their head be proclaim with sound of trumpet and every where disgrace with public derision so little reckon be make of the pope thunder canon in those day and what will they have do if the say bull have import sentence of deposition against king Charles the french church assemble at Tours in the year 1510. decree that Lewis 12 may with safe conscience contemn the abusive bull and unjust censure of pope julius the 2 and by arm may withstand the pope usurpation in case he shall proceed to excommunicate or depose the King More by a council hold at Pisa this Lewis declare the pope to be fall from the popedom and coin crown with a stamp of this inscription I will destroy the name of Babylon to this the l. of perron make answer that all this be do by the french as acknowledge these jar to have spring not from the fountain of religion but from passion of state wherein he condemn pope julius for give so great scope unto his public censure as to serve his ambition and not rather to advance religion he secret teach we beside that when the pope undertake to depose the king of France than the french be to sit as judge concern the lawfulness or unlawfulness of the cause and in case they shall find the cause to be unlawful then to disannul his judgement and to scoff at his thunderbolt John king of Navarre who realm be give by the foresay pope to Ferdinand king of Arragon be also wrap and entangle with strict band of deposition now if the french have be touch with no better feel of affection to their king than the subject of Navarre be to the Navarrois doubtless france have seek a new lord by virtue of the pope as the l. cardinal himself do acknowledge and confess unjust sentence but behold to make the say sentence against John seem the less contrary to equity the l. cardinal pretend the pope donation be not indeed the principal cause pag. 31. howsoever Ferdinand himself make it his pretence but his lo. give this for the principal cause that John d'albret have quit his alliance make with condition that in case the king of Navarre shall infringe the say alliance and break the league than the kingdom of Navarre shall return to the crown of Arragon this condition between king never make and without all show of probability serve to none other purpose from the cardinal mouth but only to insinuate and work a persuasion in his king that he have no right nor lawful pretention to the crown of Navarre and whatsoever he now hold in the say kingdom of Navarre be none of his own but by usurpation and unlawful possession thus his lordship french-born make himself a advocate for the spanish king against his own king and king of the french who shall be fain as he ought if this advocates plea may take place to draw his title and style of king of Navarre out of his royal title and to acknowledge that all the great endeavour of his predecessor to recover the say kingdom be dishonourable and unjust be it possible that in the very heart and head city of France a spirit and tongue so licentious can be brook what shall so great blasphemy as it be of the king freehold be pour forth in so honourable a assembly without punishment or fine what without any contradiction for the king right and on the king behalf I may perhaps confess the indignity may be the better bear and the pretence allege may pass for a poor excuse if it serve his purpose never so little for how do all this touch or come near the question in which the pope usurpation in the depose of king and the resolution of the french in resist this tyrannical practice be the proper issue of the cause both which point be never a whit more of the less consequence and importance howsoever Ferdinand in his own justification stand upon the foresay pretence thus much be confess and we ask no more pope julius take the kingdom from the one and give it unto the other the french thereupon resist the pope and declare he to be fall from the papacy this noble spirit and courage of the french in maintain the dignity and honour of their king crown breed those ancient custom which in the sequence of many aage have be observe and keep in use this for one that no legate of the pope nor any of his rescript nor mandate be admit and receive in France without licence from the king and unless the legate impart his faculty to the king attorney general to be peruse and verify in court of parliament where they be to be tie by certain modification and restriction unto such point as be not derogatory from the king right from the liberty of the church and from the ordinance of the kingdom when cardinal Balua Bochellus contrary to this ancient form enter France in the year 1484. and there without leave of the king do execute the office and speed certain act of the pope legate the court upon motion make by the king attorney general decree a commission to be inform against he by two councillor of the say court and inhibit his further proceed to use any faculty or power of the pope legate upon pain of be proclaim rebel in the year 1561. johannes Tanquerellus bachelor in divinity by order of the court be condemn to make open confession that he have indiscreet and rash without consideration defend this proposition the pope be the vicar of Christ a monarch that have power both spiritual and secular and he may deprive prince which rebel against his commandment of their dignity which proposition howsoever he protest that he have propound the same only to be argue and not judicial to be determine in the affirmative Tanquerellus nevertheless be compel open to recant here the l. cardinal answer the history of Tanquerellus be from the matter because his proposition treat neither of heresy nor of infidelity but I answer the say proposition treat of both forasmuch as it make mention of disobedience to the pope for I suppose he will not deny that whosoever shall stand out in heresy contrary to the pope monitory proceed he shall show but poor and simple obedience to the pope moreover the case be clear by the former example that no pope will suffer his power to cast down king to be restrain unto the cause of heresy and infidelity in the heat of the last war raise by that holy-prophane league admonitory bull be send by pope Gregory 14. from Rome 1591. by these bull king Henry 4. as a heretic and relapse be declare incapable of the crown of France and his kingdom be expose to havoc and spoil the court of parliament be assemble at Tours the 5. of August decree the say admonitory bull to be cancel tear in piece and cast into a great fire by the hand of the public executioner the arrest itself or decree be of this tenor the court due ponder and approve the conclude and unanswerable reason of the king attorney general have declare and by these present do declare the admonitory bull give at Rome the 1. of March 1591. to be of no validity abusive seditious damnable full of impiety and imposture contrary to the holy decree right franchise and liberty of the french church do ordain the copy of the say bull seal with the seal of Marsilius and sign Septilius lamprius to be rend in piece by the public executioner and by he to be burn in a great fire to be make for such purpose before the great gate of the common hall or palace etc. then even than the l. of perron be firm for the better part and stand for his king against Gregory the pope notwithstanding the crime of eresie pretend against Henry his Lord all the former example by we allege be draw out of the time after school of divinity be establish in France for I think good to bound myself within those doole and limit of time which the l. cardinal himself have set who go not sincere to work and in good earnest where he tell we there be three instance as if we have no more object against papal power to remove king out of their chair of state page 47. by name the example of Philip the fair of Lewis 12 and of Tanquerellus for in very truth all the former example by we produce be no less pregnant and evident howsoever the l. cardinal have be please to conceal they all for fear of hurt his cause nay France even in the day of her sore servitude be never unfurnished of great divine by who this usurp power of the pope over the temporalty and crown of king have be utter mislike and condemn Robert earl of Flanders be command by pope Paschal 2. to persecute with fire and sword the clergy of liege who then adhere and stand to the cause of the emperor Henry 4. who the pope have ignominious depose Robert by the pope order and command be to handle the clergy of liege in like sort as before he have serve the clergy of Cambray who by the say earl have be cruel strip both of good and life the pope promise the say earl and his army pardon of their sin for the say execution the clergy of liege address answer to the pope at large they cry out upon the church of Rome and call she Babylon tell the pope home that God have command to give unto Caesar that which be Caesar that every soul must be subject unto the superior power that no man be exempt out of this precept and that every oath of allegiance be to be keep inviolable yea that hereof they themselves be not ignorant in as much as they by a new schism and new tradition make a separation and rent of the priesthood from the kingdom do promise to absolve of perjury such as have perfidious forswear themselves against their King and whereas by way of despite and in opprobrious manner they be excommunicate by the pope they give his holiness to understand that David heart have utter a good matter but paschal heart have spew up sordid and rail word like old bawd and spinster or webster of linen when they scold and brawl one with another final they reject his papal excommunication as a sentence give without discretion this be the voice and free speech of that clergy in the life time of their noble emperor but after he be thrust out of the empire by the rebellion of his own son instigate and stir up thereunto by the pope persuasion and practice and be bring unto a miserable death it be no matter of wonder that for the safeguard of their life the say clergy be drive to sue unto the pope for their pardon 3. Hildebert bishop of upon the river of Sartre live under the reign of king Philip the first affirm in his epistle 40. and 75. that king be to be admonish and instruct rather than punish to be deal with by counsel rather than by command by doctrine and instruction rather than by correction for no such sword belong to the church because the sword of the church be ecclesiastical discipline and nothing else consider lib. 1. cap. 6. Bernard write to pope Eugenius after this manner whosoever they be that be of this mind and opinion shall never be able to make proof that any one of the apostle do ever fit in quality of judge or divider of land I read where they have stand to be judge but never where they sit down to give judgement again your authority stretch unto crime not unto possession because you have receive the key of the kingdom of heaven not in regard of possession but of crime to keep all that plead by covin or collusion and not lawful possessor out of the heavenly kingdom a little after these base thing of the earth be judge by the king and prince of this world wherefore do you thrust your sickle into a other harvest wherefore do you encroach and intrude upon a other limit lib. 2. cap. 6. elsewhere the apostle be direct forbid to make themselves lord and ruler go thou then and be a lord usurp apostleship or be a apostle usurp lordship if thou needs will have both doubtless thou shall have neither johannes Maior doctor of Paris Dist. 24. quest. 3 the sovereign bishop have no temporal authority over king the reason because it follow the contrary be once grant that king be the pope vassal now let other man judge whether he that have power to dispossess king of all their temporalty have not likewise authority over their temporalty the same author in l. 4. sent. Dist. 24 fol. 214. the pope have no manner of title over the french or spanish king in temporal matter where it be further add that pope innocent 3. have be please to testify that king of France in temporal cause do acknowledge no superior for so the pope excuse himself to a certain lord of montpellier who in stead of sue to the king have petition to the pope for a dispensation for his bastard but perhaps as he speak it will be allege out of the gloss that he acknowledge no superior by fact and yet ought by right but I tell you the gloss be a aurelian gloss which mar the text among other argument mayor bring this for one this opinion minister matter unto pope to take away a other empire by force and violence which the pope shall never bring to pass as we read of Boniface 8. against Philip the fair say beside that from hence proceed war in time of which many outrageous mischief be do and that Gerson call they egregious flatterer by who such opinion be maintain in the same place Maior deny that Childeric be depose by pope Zacharie the word he depose say mayor be not so to be understand as it be take at the first blush or fight but he depose be thus expound in the gloss he give his consent unto those by who he be depose John of Paris regia and Papali cap. 10. be it grant that Christ be arm with temporal power yet he commit no such power to Peter a little after the power of king be the high power upon earth in temporal cause it have no superior power above itself no more than the pope have in spiritual matter this author say indeed the pope have power to excommunicate the king but he speak not of any power in the pope to put down the king from his regal dignity and authority he only say when a prince be once excommunicate he may accidental or by occasion be depose because his precedent excommunication incite the people to disarm he of all secular dignity and power the same John on the other side hold opinion that in the emperor there be invest a power to depose the pope in case the pope shall abuse his power almainus doctor of the sorbonic school Almain potesi Eccl and Quest. 3. cap. 8. civil and eccl. 5. vlt. it be essential in the lay-power to inflict civil punishment as death banishment and privation or loss of good but according to divine institution the power ecclesiastical can lay no such punishment upon delinquent nay more not lay in prison as to some doctor it seem probable but stretch and reach only to spiritual punishment as namely to excommunication all other punishment inflict by the spiritual power be mere by the law positive if then ecclesiastical power by God law have no authority to deprive any private man of his good how dare the pope and his flatterer build their power to deprive king of their sceptre upon the word of God the same author in a other place quaest 1. Eccles. and laic c. 12. and 14 be it grant that Constantine have power to give the empire unto the pope yet be it not hereupon to be infer that pope have authority over the kingdom of France because that kingdom be never subject unto Constantine for the king of France never have any superior in temporal matter a little after it be not in any place to be find that God have give the pope power to make and unmake temporal king he maintain elsewhere that Zacharie do not depose Childeric quaest 2. c. 8. and nond (@) but only consent to his depose and so depose he not as by authority in the same book take up the word of Occam who he style the doctor the emperor be the pope lord in thing temporal and the pope call he lord Quae. 3. c. 2. quaest 11. can Sacerd. as it be witness in the body of the Text. the lord cardinal have dissemble and conceal these word of doctor Almainus with many like place and have be please to allege Almainus recite occams authority in stead of quote almainus himself in those passage where he speak as out of his own opinion and in his own word a notable piece of sly and cunning conveyance for what heresy may not be father and fasten upon s. Augustine or s. Hierome if they shall be deem to approve all the passage which they allege out of other author and that be the reason wherefore the l. cardinal do not allege his testimony whole and perfect as they be couch in their proper text but clip and curtail thus he deal even in the first passage or testimony of Almainus he bring it in mangle and pare he hide and conceal the word add by Almainus to contradict and cross the word go before for Almainus make this addition and supply howsoever some other doctor do stand for the negative and teach the pope have power only to declare that king and prince be to be depose and so much appear by this reason because this ample and sovereign power of the pope may give he occasion to be puff up with great pride and the same fullness of power may prove extreme hurtful to the subject etc. the same Almainus bring in occams opinion in express term decide the question quaest 2. eccl. and laic cap. 12. and there join his own opinion with occam the doctor opinion say almainus do simple carry the most probability that a pope have no power neither by excommunication nor by any other mean to depose a prince from his imperial and royal dignity in cap. 9 10. and 11. and a little before have maintain the greek empire be never transport by the pope to the german and that when the pope crown the emperor he do not give he the empire no more than the archbishop of Reims when he crown the king of France do give he the kingdom he draw this conclusion according to occams opinion I deny that a emperor be bind by oath to promise the pope allegiance on the other side if the pope hold any temporal possession he be bind to swear allegiance unto the emperor and to pay he tribute the say Occam allege by Almainus do further aver that justinian be acknowledge by the pope for his superior in temporal cause for as much as diverse law which the pope be bind to keep and observe be enact by justinian as by name the law of prescription for a hundred year which law stand yet in force against the bishop of Rome and to the end that all man may clear see how great distance there be between occams opinion and the l. cardinal who towards the end of his oration exhort his hearer at no hand to dissent from the pope take you here a view of occam own word as they be allege by Almainus the doctor assoyle the argument of pope innocent quest. 1. cap. 14. by which the pope will prove out of these word of CHRIST whatsoever thoushalt bind etc. that fullness of power in temporal matter belong to the sovereign bishop for innocent say whatsoever except nothing but Occam assoyle innocent authority as not only false but also heretical and say withal that many thing be speak by innocent which by his leave savour and smell of heresy etc. the l. pag. 40. cardinal with less fidelity allege two place out of Thomas his sum the first in the second of his second Quest. 10. Art. 10. in the body of the article in which place let it be narrow examine Thomas will easy be find to speak not of the subjection of believe subject under infidel king as the lord cardinal pretend but of believe servant that live under master whether jew or infidel as when a jew keep servant which profess jesus Christ or as when some of the faithful keep in Caesar house who be not consider by Thomas as they be subject of the empire but as they be servant of the family the other place be take out of Quest. 11. and 2. Art. in the body of the article where no such matter as the l. cardinal allege can be find with like fidelity he take Gerson in hand pag. 44. who indeed in his book of ecclesiastical power and 12. consider do affirm when the abuse of secular power redound to manifest impugn of the faith and blaspheme of the creator then shall it not be amiss to have recourse unto the last branch of this 12. consider where in such case as aforesaid a certain regitive directive regulative and ordinative authority be commit to the ecclesiastical power his very word which make no mention at all of depose or of any compulsive power over sovereign prince for that form of rule and government whereof Gerson speak be exercise by ecclesiastical censure and excommunication not by loss of good of kingdom or of empire this place than be wrest by the l. cardinal to a contrary sense neither shall his lordship have omit that Gerson in the question of king subjection in temporal matter or of the dependence of their crown upon the pope power except always the king of France witness that which Gerson a little before the place allege by the cardinal have plain affirm now since Peter time say Gerson all imperial regal and secular power be not immediate to draw virtue and strength from the sovereign bishop as in this manner the most Christian king of France have no superior nor acknowledge any such upon the face of the earth now here need no great sharpness of wit for the search out of this deep mystery that if the pope have power to give or take away crown for any cause or any pretend occasion whatsoever the crown of France must needs depend upon the pope but for as much as we be now hit in with Gerson pag. 108.109.119 where the card. take char. 7. for charl. 6. we will examine the l. cardinal allegation towards the end of his oration take out of gerson famous oration make before Charles the 6. for the university of Paris where he bring in Gerson to affirm that kill a tyrant be a sacrifice acceptable to God but Gerson let it be diligent note there speak not in his own person he there bring in sedition speak the word of which word utter by sedition and other like speech you shall now hear what judgement Gerson himself have give when sedition have speak with such a furious voice I turn away my face as if I have be smite with death to show that I be not able to endure her madness any long and indeed when dissimulation on the one side and sedition on the other have suggest the device of two contrary extreme he bring forth discretion as a judge keep the mean between both extreme and utter those word which the l. cardinal allege against himself if the head say Gerson or some other member of the civil body shall grow to so desperate a pass that it will gulp and swallow down the deadly poison of tyranny every member in his place with all power possible for he to raise by expedient mean and such as may prevent a great inconvenience shall set himself against so mad a purpose and so deadly practice for if the head be grieve with some light pain it be not fit for the hand to smite the head no that be but a foolish and a mad part nor be the hand forthwith to chop off or separate the head from the body but rather to cure the head with good speech and other mean like a skilful and wise physician yea nothing will be more cruel or more void of reason then to seek to stop the strong and violent stream of tyranny by sedition these word i think do make very strong and express against butcher even of tyrannical king and whereas a little after the say passage he teach to expel tyranny he have not a word of expel the tyrant but only of break and shake off the yoke of tyranny yet for all that he will not have the remedy for the repress of tyranny to be fetch from the pope who presume to degrade king but from philosopher lawyer divine and personage of good conversation it appear now by all that have be say before that whereas Gerson in the 7. considerate against flatterer do affirm whensoever the prince do manifest pursue and prosecute his natural subject and show himself obstinate bend with notorious injustice to vex they of set purpose and with full consent so far as to the fact then this rule and law of nature do take place it be lawful to resist and repel force by force and the sentence of Seneca there be no sacrifice more acceptable to God than a tyrant offer in sacrifice the word do take place be so to be understand as he speak in another passage to wit with or among seditious person or else the word do take place do only signify be put in practice and so Gerson there speak not as out of his own judgement his lordship also shall not have balk and leave out Sigebertus who with more reason may have pass for french than Thomas and Occam who he put upon we for French Sigebertus in his chronicle upon the year 1088. speak of the emperor depose by the pope have word of this tenor this heresy be not creep out of the shell in those day that his priest who have say to the king and make a hypocrite to rule for the sin of the people shall teach the people they owe no subjection unto wicked king nor any allegiance notwithstanding they have take the oath of allegiance now after the l. cardinal have course in this manner through the history of the last aage which in case they all make for his purpose do lack the weight of authority in stead of search the will of God in the sacred oracle of his word and stand upon example of the ancient church at last leave the troop of his own allegation he betake himself to the sharpen and rebate of the point of his adversary weapon for the purpose he bring in his adversary the champion of king crown and make they to speak out of his own mouth for his lordship say it will be object after this manner pag. 52. and it may come to pass that pope either carry with passion or mislead by sinister information may without just cause fasten upon king the imputation of heresy or apostasy then for king-deposers he frame this answer that by heresy they understand notorious heresy and former condemn by sentence of the church moreover in case the pope have err in the fact it be the clergy part adhere to their king to make remonstrance unto the pope and to require the cause may be refer to the judgement of a full council the french church then and there be present now in this answer the l. cardinal be of another mind than Bellarmine his brother cardinal adverse Barclaium for he go thus far that a prince condemn by unjust sentence of the pope ought nevertheless to quit his kingdom and that his pastor unjust sentence shall not redound to his detriment provide that he give way to the say sentence and show himself not refractory but stay the time in patience until the holy father shall renounce his error and revoke his foresay unjust sentence in which case these two material point be to be presuppose the one that he who now have seize the kingdom of the prince displace will forthwith if the pope shall solicit and intercede return the kingdom to the hand of the late possessor the other that in the interim the prince unjust depose shall not need to fear the bloody murderer merciless blade and weapon but on the other side the pope power of so large a size as Bellarmine have shape be no whit please to the l. cardinal eye for in case the king shall be unjust depose by the pope not well inform he be not of the mind the kingdom shall stoop to the pope behest but will rather have the kingdom to deal by remonstrance and to refer the cause unto the council wherein he make the council to be of more absolute and supreme authority than the pope a strain to which the holy father will never lend his ear and yet doubtless the council require in this case must be universal wherein the french for so much as they stand firm for the king and his cause can be no judge and in that regard the l. cardinal require only the presence of the french church who see not here into what pickle the french cause be bring by this mean the bishop of Italy forsooth of Spain of Sicily of Germany the subject of sovereign many time at profess or privy enmity with France shall have the cause compremit and refer to their judgement whether the kingdom of France shall drive out her king and shall kindle the flame of seditious trouble in the very heart and bowel of the realm but be it not possible that a king may lack the love of his own subject and they take the vantage of that occasion may put he to his trump in his own kingdom be it not possible that calumniation whereby a credulous pope have be seduce may in like manner deceive some part of a credulous people be it not possible that one part of the people may cleave to the pope faction another may hold and stand out for the king rightful cause and civil war may be kindle by the spleen of these two side be it not possible that his holiness will not rest in the remonstrance of the french and will no further pursue his cause and whereas now a day a general council can be hold except it be call and assemble by the pope authority be it credible the pope will take order for the convocation of a council by who he shall be judge and how can the pope be precedent in a council where himself be the party implead and to who the sift of his own sentence be refer as it be to committee to examine whether it be denounce according to law or against justice but in the mean time while all these remonstrance and address of the council be on foot behold the royal majesty of the king hang as it be by loose gimmal and must stay the judgement of the council to who it be refer well what if the council shall happen to be two or three year in assemble and to continue or hold eighteen year like the council of Trent shall not poor France I beseech you be reduce to a very bad plight shall she not be in a very wise and warm take to be short his lordship whole speech for the untie of this knot not only surmount possibility but be stuff with ridiculous toy this I make manifest by his addition in the same passage if the pope deceive in fact shall rash and unjust declare the king to be a heretic then the pope declaration shall not be second with actual deposition unless the realm shall consent unto the king depose what need any man to be instruct in this doctrine who do not know that a king so long as he be uphold and maintain in his kingdom by his people can actual and effectual be depose from his throne he that speak such language and phrase in effect say and say no more than this a king be never deprive of his crown so long as he can keep his crown on his head a king be never turn and strip naked so long as he can keep his clothes on his back a king be never depose so long as he can make the strong party and side against his enemy in brief a king be king and shall still remain king so long as he can hold the possession of his kingdom and sitfast in his chair of estate howbeit let we here by the way take notice of these word utter by his lordship that for the depose of a king the consent of the people must be obtain for by these word the people be exalt above the king and be make the judge of the king depose but here be yet a great matter can. Papa Dist 40. for that pope may err in faith it be acknowledge by pope themselves for some of they have condemn pope honorius for a Monothelite s. Hierome and s. hilarius and s. athanasius do testify that pope liberius start aside and subscribe to Arrianisme pope John 23. be condemn in the council of Constance for maintain there be neither hell nor heaven diverse other pope have be taint with error in faith if therefore any pope heretical in himself shall depose a orthodox king for heresy can it be imagine that he which boast himself to bear all divine and human law in the privy coffer or casket of his breast will stoop to the remonstrance of the french and veil to the reason which they shall propound though never so justifiable and of never so great validity and how can he that may be infect with damnable heresy when himself be not always free from heresy be a judge of heresy in a king in this question some be of opinion that as a man the pope may fall into error but not as pope very good I demand then upon the matter wherefore the pope do not instruct and reform the man or wherefore the man do not require the pope instruction but whether a king be depose by that man the pope or by that pope the man be it not all one be he not depose other affirm the pope may err in a question of the fact but not in a question of the right a egregious gullery and imposture for if he may be ignorant whether jesus Christ die for our sin doubtless he may also be to seek whether we shall repose all our trust and assure confidence in the death of Christ consider with i the prophet of old they be all inspire and teach of God to admonish and reprove the king of judah and Israel they neither err in matter of fact nor in point of right they be as far from be blind and fetch over by deceitful calumniation as from be seduce by the paint show of corrupt and false doctrine as they never tread awry in matter of faith so they never whet the edge of their tongue or style against the faultless have it not be a trim device in their time to say that as esay and as Daniel they may have sink into heresy but not as prophet for doubtless in this case that esay will have take counsel of the prophet which be himself to be short if king be only so long to be take for king until they shall be declare heretic and shall be depose by the pope they continual stand in extreme danger to undergo a very heavy and unjust sentence their safe way be to know nothing and to believe by proxy lest if they shall happen to talk of God or to think of religion they shall be draw for heretic into the pope inquisition all the example hitherto produce by the lord cardinal on a row be of a latter date they lack weight be draw from the time of bondage and make the pope themselves witness in their own cause they descant not upon the point of deposition but only strike out and sound the note of excommunication and interdiction which make nothing at all to the music of the question and therefore he tell we in kindness as I take it more oftentimes then once or twice that he speak only of the fact as one that do acknowledge himself to be out of the right he relate thing do but never what shall be do which as the judicious know be to teach nothing the second inconvenience examine the second juconuenience like to grow pag. 86. as the lord cardinal seem to be half afraid if the article of the three estate may have pass with approbation be couch in these word layman shall by authority be strengthen with power to judge in matter of religion as also to determine the doctrine comprise in the say article to have requisite conformity with God word yeathey shall have it in their hand to compel ecclesiastic by necessity to swear preach and teach the opinion of the one as also by sermon and public writing to impugn the other this inconvenience he aggravate with swell word and break out into these vehement exclamation o reproach o scandal o gate set open to a world of heresy he therefore labour both by reason and by authority of holy scripture to make such usurp power of laic a foul shameful and odious practice in the whole his lordship toil himself in vain and make supposition of castle in the air for in prefer this article the three estate have bear themselves not as judge or umpire but altogether as petitioner request the say article may be receive into the number of the parliament book to be present unto the king and his counsel unto who in all humility they refer the judgement of the say article conceive all good hope the clergy and nobility will be please to join for the furtherance of their humble petition they be not so ignorant of state-matter or so unmindful of their own place and charge to bear themselves in hand that a petition put up and prefer by the three estate can carry the force of a law or statute so long as the other two order withstand the same and so long as the king himself hold back his royal consent beside the say article be not propound as a point of religious doctrine but for ever after to remain and continue a fundamental law of the commonwealth and state itself the due care whereof be put into their hand and commit to their trust if the king have ratify the say article with royal consent and have command the clergy to put in execution the content thereof it have be their duty to see the king will and pleasure fulfil as they be subject bind to give he aid in all thing which may any way serve to procure the safety of his life and the tranquillity of his kingdom which if the clergy have perform to the uttermost of their power they have not show obedience as underling unto the three estate but unto the king alone by who such command have be impose upon suggestion of his faithful subject make the more watchful by the negligence of the clergy who they perceive to be link with strict band unto the pope than they be unto their King here then the cardinal fight with mere shadow and move a doubt whereof his adversary have not so much as once think in a dream but yet according to his great dexterity and nimbleness of spirit by this device he cunning take upon he to give cardinal comprise under the name of laic who judgement it be not unlikely be apprehend much better by the clergy than the judgement of the three estate now these be the man who he term intruder into other man charge and such as open a gate for I wot not how many legion of heresy to rush into the church for if it be proper to the clergy and their head to judge in this cause of the right of king then the king himself his prince and nobility be debar and wipe of all judgement in the same cause no less than the representative body of the people well then pag. 61. the l. cardinal shower down like hail sundry place and testimony of scripture where the people be command to have their pastor in singular love and to bear they all respect of due observance be it so yet be the say passage of scripture no bar to the people for their vigilant circumspection to preserve the life and crown of their prince against all the wicked enterprise of man stir up by the clergy who have their head out Constantine and Valentinian by name the first refuse to meddle with judgement in episcopal cause the other forbear to judge of subtle question in divinity with protestation that he will never be so subtle inquisition the same answer meet with all the rest of the place produce by the l. orat. timme cardinal out of the father and that one for example out of Gregory nazianzenus be not cite by the cardinal with fair deal for Gregory do not board the emperor himself but his deputy or l. precedent on this manner (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) for we also be in authority and place of a ruler we have command aswell as yourself whereas the the l. cardinal with foul play turn the place in these term we also be emperor which word can bear no such interpretation as well because he to who the bishop then speak be not of imperial dignity as also because if the bishop himself a bishop of so small a city as Nazianzum have qualify himself emperor he shall have pass all the bound of modesty and have show himself arrogant above measure for as touch subjection due to christian emperor he free acknowledge a little before (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) that himself and his people be subject unto the superior power yea bind to pay they tribute the history of the same Gregory life do testify that he be draw by the arrian before the consul judgement seat and from thence return acquit without either stripe or any other kind of contumelious entreaty and use yet now at last up start a prelate who dare make this good father vaunt himself to be a emperor it be willing grant that emperor never challenge never arrogate to be sovereign judge in controversy of doctrine and faith nevertheless it be clear than the sun light at high noon that for moderation at synod for determination and order establish in council and for the discipline of the church they have make a good and a full use of their imperial authority the first council hold at Constantinople bear this title or inscription the dedication of the holy synod to the most religious emperor Theodosius the great to who will and pleasure they have submit these canon by they address and establish in council and there they also beseech the emperor to confirm and approve the say canon the like have be do by the council of Trullo by who the canon of the five and six council be put forth and publish this be not do because emperor take upon they to be infallible judge of doctrine but only that emperor may see and judge whether bishop who feel the prick of ambition as other man do do propound nothing in their convocation and consultation but most of all in their determination to undermine the emperor authority to disturb the tranquillity of the commonwealth and to cross the determination of precedent council now to take the cognizance of such matter out of the king hand or power what be it but even to transform the king into a stand image to wring and wrest he out of all care of himself and his kingly charge yea to bring he down to this base condition to become only a executioner and which I scorn to speak the unhappy hangman of the clergy will without any further cognizance not so much as of matter which most near touch himself and his royal estate I grant it be for divinity school to judge how far the power of the key do stretch I grant again that Clerics both may and aught also to display the colour and ensign of their censure against prince who violate their public and solemn oath do raise and make open war against jesus Christ I grant yet again that in this case they need not admit laic to be of their counsel nor allow they any scope or liberty of judgement yet all this make no bar to Clerics for extend the power of their key many time a whole degree further than they ought and when they be please to make use of their say power to deprive the people of their good or the prince of his crown all this do not hinder prince or people from take care for the preservation of their own right and estate nor from require Clerics to show their card and produce their chart and to make demonstration by scripture that such power as they assume and challenge be give they from God for to leave the pope absolute judge in the same cause wherein he be a party and which be the strong rampire and bulwark yea the most glorious and eminent point of his domination to arm he with power to unhorse king out of their seat what be it else but even to draw they into a state of despair for ever win the day or prevail in their honourable and rightful cause it be moreover grant if a king shall command any thing direct contrary to God word and tend to the subvert of the church that Clerics in this case ought not only to dispense with subject for their obedience but also express to forbid their obedience for it be always better to obey God then man howbeit in all other matter whereby the glory and majesty of God be not impeach or impair it be the duty of Clerics to ply the people with wholesome exhortation to constant obedience and to avert by earnest dissuasion the say people from tumultuous revolt and seditious insurrection this practice under the pagan emperor be hold and follow by the ancient christian by who godly zeal and patience in bear the yoke the church in time past grow and flourish in her happy and plentiful increase far great than popery shall ever purchase and attain unto by all her cunning device and sleight as namely by degrade of king by interdict of kingdom by apposted murder and by diabolical train of gunne-powder-mine the place of scripture allege in order by the cardinal pag 66. in favour of those that stand for the pope claim of power and authority to depose king be cite with no more sincerity than the former they allege these be his word that Samuel depose king Saul or declare he to be depose because he have violate the law of the jew religion his lordship avouch elsewhere that Saul be depose because he have seek profane to usurp the holy priesthood both false and contrary to the tenor of truth in the sacred history for Saul be never depose according to the sense of the word I mean depose in the present question to wit as depose be take for despoil the king of his royal dignity and reduce the king to the condition of a private person but Saul hold the title of king and continue in possession of his kingdom even to his die day 1. sam. 23.20 and 24.15 and 2. sam. 2.5 yea the scripture style he king even to the periodical and last day of his life by the testimony of David himself who both by God promise and by precedent unction be then heir apparent as it be to the crown in a manner then ready to gird and adorn the temple of his head for if Samuel by God commandment have then actual remove Saul from his throne doubtless the whole church of Israel have commit a gross error in take and honour Saul for their king after such deposition doubtless the prophet Samuel himself make know the lord ordinance unto the people will have enjoin they by strict prohibition to call he no long the king of Israel doubtless David will never have hold his hand from the throat of Saul 1. sam. 26.11 for this respect and consideration because he be the lord anoint for if Saul have lose his kingly authority from that instant when Samuel give he knowledge of his rejection then David lest otherwise the body of the kingdom shall want a royal head be to begin his reign and to bear the royal sceptre in the very same instant which be to charge the holy scripture with untruth in as much as the sacred history begin the computation of the year of David reign from the day of saul death true it be that in the 1. sam. cap. 15. Saul be denounce by god own sentence a man reject and as it be excommunicate out of the kingdom that he shall not rule and reign any long as king over Israel nevertheless the say sentence be not put in execution before the day when God execute upon Saul a exemplary judgement do strike he with death from whence it be manifest and clear 1. sam. 16.23 that when David be anoint king by Samuel that action be only a promise and a testimony of the choice which God have make of David for succession immediate after Saul and not a present establishment investment or instalment of David in the kingdom we read the like in 1. King cap. 19 where God command Elias the prophet to anoint Hasael king of Syria for can any man be so blind and ignorant in the sacred history to believe the prophet of Israel establish or sacred the king of Syria for this cause 2. sam. 2.4 when David be actual establish in the kingdom he be anoint the second time in the next place he bring in the pope champion use these word rehoboam be depose by Ahiah the prophet 1 King 12. from his royal right over the ten tribe of Israel because his father Solomon have play the in fall from the law of God this I say also be more than the truth of the sacred history do afoard for Ahiah never speak to rehoboam for aught we read nor bring unto he any message from the lord as for the passage quote by the l. cardinal out of 3. reg. chap. 11. it have not reference to the time of rehoboams reign but rather indeed to salomon time nor do it carry the face of a iudicatory sentence for the king depose but rather of a prophetical prediction for how can rehoboam before he be make king be deprive of the kingdom last of all but worst of all to allege this passage for a example of a just sentence in matter of depose a king be to approve the disloyal treachery of a servant against his master and the rebellion of brand in scripture with a mark of perpetual infamy for his wickedness and impiety he go on with a other example of no more truth 1. King 19 king Achab be depose by Elias the prophet because he embrace false religion and worship false god false too like the former king Achab lose his crown and his life both together the scripture that speak not according to man fancy but according to the truth do extend and number the year of achabs reign to the time of his death prediction of a king ruin be no sentence of deposition Elias never give the subject of Achab absolution from their oath of obedience never give they the least inkling of any such absolution never set up or place any other king in achabs throne that of the l. cardinal a little after pag. 68 be no less untrue that king Vzziah be drive from the conversation of the people by Azarias the priest and thereby the administration of his kingdom be leave no long in his power nor so for when God have smite Vzziah with leprosy in his forehead 2. Chro. 26. he withdraw himself or go out into a house apart for fear of infect such as be whole by his contagious disease the high priest smite he not with any sentence of deposition or denounce he suspend from the administration of his kingdom no the day of his reign be number in scripture to the day of his death and whereas the priest according to the law in the 13. of levit. judge the king to be unclean he give sentence against he not as against a criminal person and thereby within the compass of deposition but as against a disease body for the law inflict punishment not upon disease but upon crime hereupon whereas it be record by josephus in his antiquity antiq. l. 9 cap. 11. that Vzziah lead a private and in a manner a solitary life the say author do not mean that Vzziah be depose but only that he disburden himself of care to manage the public affair the example of Mattathias pag. 69. by who the jew be stir up to rebel against Antiochus be no better worth for in that example we Paul as by one who in the spirit of a apostle do foresee that rebellion against prince be to rise and spring from the city of Rome now in case the head of that church by warrant of any privilege contain in the most holy register of God holy word be exempt from the bind power of this general precept or rule do it not become his lordship to show by the book that it be a book case and to lay it forth before that honourable assembly who no doubt expect and wait to hear when it may fall from his learned lip but in stead of any such authentical and canonical confirmation he fly to a sleight shift and with a cavil be bold to affirm the foundation lay by those of our side do no way touch the knot of the controversy let we hear he speak it be not in controversy whether obedience be due to king by God law so long as they be king or acknowledge for king but our point controvert be whether by God law it be require that he who have be once recognize and receive for king by the body of estate can at any time be take and repute as no king that be to say can do no manner of act whereby he may loose his right and so cease to be salute King this answer of the l. cardinal be the rare devise evasion and start hole of the jesuite in who ear of delicate and tender touch king-killing sound very harsh but forsooth to un-king a king first and then to give he the stab that be a point of just and true descant for to kill a king once unking by deposition be not kill of a king for the present I have one of that jesuitical order in prison who have face enough to speak this language of ashdod and to maintain this doctrine of the jesuite college the l. cardinal harp upon the same string he can like subjection and obedience to the king while he sit king but his holiness must have all power and give order withal to hoist he out of his royal Seat I therefore now answer that in very deed the former passage of s. Paul and s. Peter shall come nothing near the question if the state of the question be such as he bring it make and forge in his own shop but certes the state of the question be not whether a king may do some act without draw once in a thousand year be it credible that in the time when pope be most debauch abandon themselves to all sort of corrupt and vicious course as be testify by their own flatterer and hinder from draw the pretend sword lest long custom of not use the sword so many aage may make it so to rust in the scabbard that when there shall be occasion to use the say sword it can not be draw at all and lest so long custom of not use the same shall confirm prescription to their great prejudice if weakness be a just let how be it come to pass that pope have enterprise to depose Philip the fair Lewis the 12 and elizabeth my predecessor of happy memory to let pass other in who experience have well prove how great inequality be between their strength yea for the most part from thence grow most grievous trouble and war which just recoil and light upon his own head as happen to Gregory the 7 and Boniface the 8 this no doubt be the reason wherefore the pope never set in for fear of such inconvenience to blast a king with lightning and thunder of deposition but when he perceive the trouble water of the kingdom by some strong faction settle in his estate or when the king be confine and border by some Princemore potent who thirst after the prey and be ever gape for some occasion to pick a quarrel the king stand in such estate be it not as easy for the pope to pull he down as it be for a man with one hand to thrust down a totter wall when the groundsel be rot the stud unpind and nod or bend towards the ground but if the king shall bear down and break the faction within the realm if he shall get withal the upper hand of his enemy out of the kingdom then the holy father present he with pardon never sue for never ask and in afather indulgence forsooth give he leave still to hold the kingdom that he be not able by all his force to wrest and wring out of his hand no more than the club of Hercules out of his fist how many worthy prince incense by the pope to conspire against sovereign lord their master and by open rebellion to work some change in their estate have miscarry in the action with loss of life or honour or both for example duke of Suevia be egg on by the pope against Henry 4 of that name emperor how many massacre how many desolation of city and town how many bloody battle ensue thereupon let history be search let just account be take and beside siege lay to city it will appear by true computation that Henry the 4 and frederic the fist fight above threescore battle in defence of their own right against enemy of the empire stir up to arm by the pope of Rome how much christian blood be then spill in these bloody battle it pass man wit pen or tongue to express and to give a little touch unto matter at home do not his holiness understand right well the weakness of papist in my kingdom do not his holmesse neverthesesse animate my papist to rebellion and forbid my papist to take the oath of allegiance do not his holiness by this mean draw so much as in he lie persecution upon the back of my papist as upon rebel and expose their life as it be upon the open stall to be sell at a very easy price all these example either joint or several be manifest and evident proof that fear to draw mischief and persecution upon the church have not bar the pope from thunder against emperor and king whensoever they conceive any hope by their fulmination to advance their greatness last of all I refer the matter to the most possess with prejudice even the very adversary whether this doctrine by which people be train up in subjection unto infidel or heretical king until the subject be of sufficient strength to mate their king to expel their king and to depose they from their kingdom do not incense the turkish emperor and other infidel prince to root out all the christian that draw in their yoke as people that wait only for a fit occasion to rebel and to take themselves engage for obedience to their lord only by constraint and servile fear let we therefore now conclude with Ozius in that famous epistle speak to Constantius a arrian heretic solite as he that by secret practice or open violence will bereave thou of thy empire shall violate God ordinance so be thou touch with fear lest by usurp authority over church matter thou tumble not headlong into some heinous crime where this holy bishop have not vouchsafe to insert and mention the l. cardinal exception to wit the right of the church always except and save when she shall be of sufficient strength to shake off the yoke of emperor neither speak the same holy bishop of private person alone or man of some particular condition and calling but he set down a general rule for all degree never to impeach imperial majesty upon any pretext whatsoever as his lordship first reason draw from weakness be exceed weak so be that which the l. cardinal take up in the next place the 2. pag. 77. he tell we there be very great difference between pagan emperor and christian prince pagan emperor who never do homage to Christ who never be by their subject receive with condition to acknowledge perpetual subjection unto the empire of Christ who never be bind by oath and mutual contract between prince and subject Christian prince who slide back by apostasy degenerate by Arrianisme or fall away by Mahometisme touch the latter of these two as his lordship say if they shall as it be take a oath and make a vow contrary to their first oath and vow make and take when they be install and contrary to the condition under which they receive the sceptre of their father if they withal shall turn persecutor of the catholic religion touch these I say the l. cardinal hold that without question they may be remove from their kingdom he tell we not by who but every where he mean by the pope touch king depose by the pope under pretence of stupidity as Childeric or of matrimonial cause as Philip i. or for collate of benefice as Philip the fair not one word by that point he easy glide and shuffle it up in silence for fear of distaste the pope on the one side or his auditor on the other now in allege this reason his lordship make all the world a witness that in depose of king the pope have no eye of regard to the benefit and security of the church for such prince as never suck other milk then that of infidelity and persecution of religion be no less noisome and pernicious vermin to the church then if they have suck of the church breast and as for the greatness of the sin or offence it seem to i there be very little difference in the matter for a prince that never do swear any religious obedience to jesus Christ be bind no less to such obedience then if he have take a solemn oath as the son that rebellious stand up against his father be in equal degree of sin whether he have swear or not swear obedience to his father because he be bind to such obedience not by any voluntary contract or covenant but by the law of nature the commandment of God to kiss the son who the father have confirm and ratify king of king do equal bind all king as well pagan as christian on the other side who deny who doubt that Constantius emperor at his first step or entrance into the empire do not swear and bind himself by solemn vow to keep the rule and to maintain the precept of the orthodox faith or that he do not receive his father empire upon such condition this notwithstanding the bishop of Rome pull not Constantius from his imperial throne but Constantius remove the bishop of Rome from his papal See and be it so that a oath take by a king at his consecration and after violate be a sufficient cause for the pope to depose a apostate or heretical prince then by good consequence the pope may in like sort depose a king who be neither dead in apostasy nor sick of heresy do neglect only the due administration of justice to his loyal subject for his oath take at consecration import likewise that he shall minister justice to his people a point wherein the holy father be hold short by the l. cardinal who dare prescribe new law to the pope and presume to limit his fullness of power within certain mere and headland extend the pope power only to the depose of christian king when they turn apostate forsake the catholic faith and not such prince as never breathe any thing but pure paganism and never serve under the colour of jesus Christ mean while his lordship forget that king Attabaliba be depose by the pope from his kingdom of Peru and the say kingdom be confer upon the king of Spain though the say poor king of Peru never forsake his heathen superstition and though the turn of he out of his terrestrial kingdom be no way to convert he unto the faith of Christ pag. 77. yea his lordship a little after tell we himself that be the turk possession in the conquest that he make over christian never so ancient yet by no long tract of time whatsoever can he gain so much as a thumb breadth of prescription that be to say the Turk for all that be but a disseisor one that violent and wilful keep a other man from his own and by good right may be dispossess of the same whereas notwithstanding the turkish emperor never favour nor savour christianity let we run over the example of king who the pope have dare and presume to depose and hardly will any one be find of who it may be true avouch that he have take a oath contrary to his oath of subjection to jesus Christ or that he have wilful cast himself into apostatical defection and certes to any man that weigh the matter with due consideration it will be find apparent false that king of France have be receive of their subject at any time with condition to serve JESUS CHRIST they be actual king before they come forth to the solemnity of their sacring before they use any stipulation or promise to their subject for in hereditary kingdom nothing more certain nothing more uncontrollable the king death instant make livery and seisin of the royalty to his next successor nor be it material to reply that a king succeed by right of inheritance take a oath in the person of his predecessor for every oath be personal proper to the person by who it be take and to God no live creature can swear that his own son or his heir shall prove a honest man well may the father and with great solemnity promise that he will exhort his heir apparent with all his power and the best of his endeavour to fear God and to practice piety if the father oath be agreeable to the duty of godliness the son be bind thereby whether he take a oath or take none on the other side if the father oath come from the puddle of impiety the son be bind thereby to go the contrary way if the father oath concern thing of indifferent nature and such as by the variety or change of time become either pernicious or impossible than it be free for the king next successor and heir prudent to fit and proportion his law unto the time present and to the best benefit of the commonwealth when I call these thing to mind with some attention I be out of all doubt his lordship be very much to seek in the right sense and nature of his king oath take at his coronation to defend the church and to persevere in the catholic faith for what be more unlike and less credible than this conceit that after Clovis have reign 15. year in the state of paganism and then receive holy baptism he shall become christian upon this condition that in case he shall afterward revolt from the faith it shall then be in the power of the church to turn he out of his kingdom but have any such conditional stipulation be make by Clovis in very good earnest and truth yet will he never have intend that his depose shall be the act of the roman bishop but rather of those whether peer or people or whole body of the state by who he have be advance to the kingdom let we hear the truth and this be the truth it be far from the customary use in France for their king to take any such oath or to use any such stipulation with their subject if any king or prince wheresoever do use a oath or solemn promise in these express term let i lose my kingdom or my life be that day my last both for life and reign when I shall first revolt from the christian religion by these word he call upon God for vengeance he use imprecation against his own head but he make not his crown to stoop by this mean to any power in the pope or in the church or in the people and touch inscription upon coin of which point his lordship speak by the way very the nature of the money or coin the stamp and mint whereof be one of the mark of the prince his dignity and sovereignty be not change by bear the letter of Christ name on the reverse or on the front such character of Christ name be advertisement and instruction to the people that in show and yield obedience unto the king they be obedient unto Christ and those prince likewise who be so well advise to have the most sacred name inscribe and print in their coin do take and acknowledge jesus Christ for supreme king of king the say holy character be no representation or profession that any king crown depend upon the church or can be take away by the pope the l. cardinal indeed so bear we in hand but he invert the word of jesus Christ and wring they out of the right joint for Christ without all ambiguity and circumlocution by the image and inscription of the money do direct and express prove Caesar to be free from subjection and entire sovereign now if such a supreme and sovereign prince at any time shall bandy and combine against God and thereby shall become a rebellious and perfidious prince doubtless for such disloyalty he shall deserve that God will take from he all hope of life eternal and yet hereby neither pope nor people have reason to be puff up in their power to deprive he of his temporal kingdom the l. page 76. cardinal say beside the champion of the pope power to depose king do expound that commandment of s. Paul whereby every soul be make subject unto the superior power to be a provisional precept or caution accommodate to the time and to stand in force only untll the church be grow in strength unto such a scantling that it may be in the power of the faithful without shake the pillar of christian state to stand in the breach and cautelous to provide that none but christian prince may be receive according to the law in deut thou shall make thou a king from among thy brother the reason whereupon they ground be this because Paul say it be a shame for christian to be judge under unjust infidel in mattrs or business which they have one against another for which inconvenience justinian after provide by law when he ordain that no infidel nor heretic may be admit to the administration of justice in the commonwealth in which word of the cardinal the word receive be to be observe especial and above the rest for by chap in that word he do nimble and with a trick of legier-demain transform or change the very state of the question for the question or issue of the cause be not about receive establish or choose a prince as in those nation where the kingdom go by election but about do homage to the prince when God have settle he in the kingdom and have cast it upon a prince by hereditary succession for that which be write thou shall make thou a king do no way concern and touch the people of France in these day because the make of their king have not of long time be tie to their election the passage therefore in Deuter. make nothing to the purpose no more than do justinian law for it be our free and voluntary confession that a christian prince be to have special care of the law and to provide that no unbeliever be make lord chiefe-iustice of the land that no infidel be put in trust with administration of justice to the people but here the issue do not direct we to speak of delegate of subordinate magistrate and such as be in commission from the prince but of the supreme prince himself the sovereign magistrate ordain by nature and confirm by succession our question be whether such a prince can be unthrone by the pope by who he be not place in the throne and whether the pope can despoil such a prince of that royalty which be never give he by the pope under any pretend colour and imputation of heresy of stupidity or infringe the privilege of monastery or transgress the law and line of holy matrimony now that s. Paul commandment which bind every soul in the band of subjection unto the high power be no precept give by way of proviso and only to serve the time but a stand and a perpetual rule it be hereby more than manifest s. Paul have ground this commandment upon certain reason not only constant and permanent by their proper nature but likewise necessary for every state condition and revolution of the time his reason because all power be ordain of God because resist of power be resist the ordinance of God because the magistrate bear the sword to execute justice because obedience and subjection to the magistrate be necessary not only for fear of his wrath or fear of punishment but also for conscience sake it be therefore a case ground upon conscience it be not a law devise by human wisdom it be not fashionable to the quality of the time apostolical instruction for the right inform of manner be not changeable according to time and season to use the l. cardinal language and to follow his fancy in the matter be to make way for two pestiferous mischief first let it be free and lawful for christian to hold the command rule of GOD for provisional caution and what follow man be lead into the broad way of impiety and the whole scripture be wipe of all authority then again for the other mischief the glorious triumph of most bless martyr in their unspeakable torment and suffering by the l. cardinal position shall be judge unworthy to wear the title and crown of martyrdom how so because according to his new fiction they have give place to the violence and fury of heathen magistrate not in obedience to the necessary and certain commandment of God but rather to a provisional direction accommodate to the humour of the time and therefore the l. cardinal have use none other clay wherewith to daub over his devise but plain falsification of holy scripture for he make the apostle say to the corinthian it be a shame for christian to be judge under unbelieve magistrate whereas in that whole context of Paul there be no such matter for when the apostle say I speak it even to your shame (@) (@) (@) (@) (@) he do not say it be a shame for a believer to be judge under a infidel but he make they ashamed of their ungodly course and unchristian practice that in sue and implead one another they lay their action of contention in the court of unbelieve judge the shame be not in bear that yoke which God have charge their neck withal but in devour and eat up one a other with writ of and with other process as also in uncover the shame in lay open the shameful part and prank play by christian before infidel to the great scandal of the church here I say the l. cardinal be take in a trick of manifest falsification if therefore a king when he sal to play the heretic deserve to be depose why shall not a cardinal when he fall to play the juggler with holy scripture deserve to be disrobe mean while the indifferent reader be to consider how great this doctrine be prejudicial and how full of danger to christian live under heretical or pagan prince for make it once know to the emperor of turk let he once get never so little a smack of this doctrine that christian live under his empire do
