article like a index of novelty new creep into the law and I have it ready to be consider of look to plowdens case and your old if you find it not there then I must say with CHRIST away with the new polygamy and maintain the ancient law pure and undefiled as it be before to the auditory I have but little to say yet that little will not be ill bestow to be say at this time since I have now renew and confirm my resolution to maintain my oath the law and justice of the land so do I expect that you my subject do submit yourselves as you ought to the observance of that Law and as I have divide the two former part of my charge so will I divide this your submission into three part for orderly division and method cause thing better to be remember first in general that you give due reverence to the law and this general divide itself into three first not to sue but upon just cause second be sue and judgement pass against you acquiesce in the judgement and do not tumultuate against it and take example from i who you have hear here protest that when ever any decree shall be give against i in my private right between i and a subject I will as humble acquiesce as the mean man in the land imitate i in this for in every plea there be two party and judgement can be but for one and against the other so one must always be displease three do not complain and importune i against judgement for I hold this paradox to be a good rule in government that it be better for a king to maintain a unjust decree then to question every decree and judgement after the give of a sentence for then suit shall never have end therefore as you come gape to the law for justice so be satisfy and content when judgement be pass against you and trouble not i but if you find bribery or corruption then come bold but when I say bold beware of come to complain except you be very sure to prove the justice of your cause otherwise look for to be execute upon you for your accuse of a upright judge deserve double punishment in that you seek to lay infamy upon a worthy person of that reverend call and be not till on with your own lawyer tale that say the cause be just for their own gain but believe the judge that have no hire but of i second in your plea presume not to meddle with thing against the king prerogative or honour some gentleman of late have be too bold this way if you use it the judge will punish you and if they suffer it I must punish both they and you plead not upon new puritanical strain that make all thing popular but keep you within the ancient limit of pleas. three make not many change from court to court for he that change court show to mistrust the justness of the cause go to the right place and the court that be proper for your cause change not thence and submit yourselves to the judgement give there thus have finish the charge to myself the judge and the auditory I be to crave your pardon if I have forget any thing or be enforce to break my method for you must remember I come not hither with a write sermon I have no book to read it out of and a long speech manifold business and a little leisure may well plead pardon for any fault of memory and true I know not if I have forget any thing or not and now have I deliver first my excuse why I come not till now next the reason why I come now three my charge and that to myself to you my lord the judge and to the auditory I have also a ordinary charge that I use to deliver to the judge before my council when they go their circuit and see I be come to this place you shall have that also and so I will make the old say true comb seldom combesore I mean by my long detain you at this time which will be so much the more profitable in this auditory because a number of the auditory will be inform here who may relate it to their fellow justice in the country my lord the judge you know very well that as you be judge with i when you sit here so be you judge under i and my substitute in the circuit where you be judge itinerant to do justice to my people it be a ancient and laudable custom in this kingdom that the judge go through the kingdom in circuit ease the people thereby of great charge who must otherwise come from all the remote part of the kingdom to Westminster hall for the find out and punish of offence past and prevent the occasion or offence that may arise I can give you no other charge in effect but only to remember you again of the same in substance which I deliver to you this time twelvemonth first remember that when you go your circuit you go not only to punish-and prevent offence but you be to take care for the good government in general of the part where you travel as well as to do justice in particular betwixt party and party in cause criminal and civil you have charge to give to justice of peace that they do their duty when you be absent aswell as present take a account of they and report their service to i at your return as none of you will hold it sufficient to give a charge except in take the account you find the fruit of it so I say to you it will not be sufficient for you to hear my charge if at your return you bring not a account to the harvest of my sow which can be do in general but in make to i a particular report what you have do for a king have two office first to direct thing to be do second to take a account how they be fulfil for what be it the better for i to direct as a angel if I take not account of your do I know not whether misunderstanding or slackness breed this that I have no account but in general of that I give you in particular in charge the last year therefore I now charge you again that at your next return you repair to my chancellor and bring your account to he in writing of those thing which in particular I have give you in charge and then when I have see your account as occasion shall serve it may be I will call for some of you to be inform of the state of that part of the country where your circuit lay of these two part of your service I know the ordinary legal part of be the more profitable to you but the other part of justice be more necessary for my service therefore as CHRIST say to the Pharisee as the most principal yet I will say which that you may the better do I have allow you a day more in your circuit than my predecessor have do and this you shall find that even as a king let he be never so godly wise righteous and just yet if the subaltern magistrate do not their part under he the kingdom must needs suffer so let the judge be never so careful and industrious if the justice of peace under they put not to their help hand in vain be all your labour for they be the king eye and ear in the country it be a ancient custom that all the judge both immediate before their go to their circuit and immediate upon their return repair to the lord chancellor of England both to receive what direction it shall please the king by his mouth to give unto they as also to give he a account of their labour who be to acquaint the king therewith and this good ancient custom have likewise be too much slack of late and therefore first of all I be to exhort and command you that you be careful to give a good account to i and my chancellor of the duty perform by all justice of peace in your circuit which government by justice be so laudable and so high esteem by i that I have make Scotland to be govern by justice and constable as England be and let not gentleman be ashamed of this place for it be a place of high honour and great reputation to be make a minister of the king justice in service of the commonwealth of these there be two sort as there be of all company especial where there be a great number that be good and bad justice for the good you be to inform i of they that I may know they thank they and reward they as occasion serve for I hold a good justice of peace in his country to do i as good service as he that wait upon i in my privy chamber and as ready will I be to reward he for I account he as capable of any honour office or preferment about my person or for any place of council or state as well as any courtier that be near about i or any that have deserve well of i in foreign employment yea I esteem the service do i by a good justice of peace three hundred mile yea six hundred mile out of my sight as well as the service do i in my presence for as God have give i large limit so must I be careful that my providence may reach to the far part of they and as law can be honour except honour be give to judge so without due respect to justice of peace what regard will be have of the service therefore let none be ashamed of this office or be discourage in be a justice of peace if he serve worthy in it the chancellor under i make justice and put they out but neither I nor he can tell what they be therefore we must be inform by you judge who can only tell who do well and who do ill without which how can the good be cherish and maintain and the rest put out the good justice be careful to attend the service of the king and country for thanks only of the king and love to their country and for no other respect the bad be either idle slowbelly that abide always at home give to a life of ease and delight like lady than man and think it be enough to contemplate justice when as virtus in contemplative justice be no justice and contemplative justice be fit to be put out another sort of justice be busybody and will have all man dance after their pipe and follow their greatness or else will not be content a sort of man these proud spirit must know that the country be ordain to obey and follow GOD and the king and not they another sort be they that go seldom to the king service but when it be to help some of their kindred or alliance so as when they come it be to help their friend or hurt their enemy make justice to serve for a shadow to faction and tumultuate the country another sort be gentleman of great worth in their own conceit and can be content with the present form of government but must have a kind of liberty in the people and must be gracious lord and redeemer of their liberty and in every cause that concern prerogative give a snatch against a monarchy through their puritanical itch after popularity some of they have show themselves too bold of late in the low house of parliament and when all be do if there be not a king they will be less care for then other man and now have speak of the quality of the justice of peace I be next to speak of their number as I ever hold the midway in all thing to be the way of virtue in eschew both extremity so do I in this for upon the one part a multitude of justice of peace in the country more than be necessary breed but confusion for although it be a old proverb that many hand make light work yet too many make slight work and too great a number of justice of peace will make the business of the country to be the more neglect every one trust to another so as nothing shall be well do beside the breed of great corruption for where there be a great number it can hardly be but some will be corrupt and upon the other part too few justice of peace will not be able to undergo the burden of the service and therefore I will neither have too few nor too many but as many in every country as may according to the proportion of that country be necessary for the perform of the service there and no more as to the charge you be to give to the justice I can but repeat what former I have tell you yet in so good a business and as I begin with fulfil the proverb a jove so will I begin this charge you be to give to the justice with church-matter for GOD will bless every good business the better that he and his church have the precedence that which I be now to speak be anent recusant and papist you never return from any circuit but by your account make unto i I both conceive great comfort and great grief comfort when I hear a number of recusant in some circuit to be diminish grief to my heart and soul when I hear a number of recusant to be in other circuit increase I protest unto you nothing in the earth can grieve i so much as man fall away from religion in my day and nothing so much joy i as when that religion increase under i GOD be my witness I speak nothing for vainglory but speak it again my heart be grieve when I hear recusant increase therefore I wish you judge to take it to heart as I do and prevent it as you can and make i know to my people as I be there be three sort of recusant the first be they that for themselves will be no recusant but their wife and their family be and they themselves do come to church but once or twice in a year enforce by law or for fashion sake these may be formal to the law but more false to GOD then the other sort the second sort be they that be recusant and have their conscience mislead and therefore refuse to come to church but otherwise live as peaceable subject the three sort be practise recusant these force all their servant to be recusant with they they will suffer none of their tenant but they must be recusant and their neighbour if they live by they in peace must be recusant also these you may find out as a fox by the foul smell a great way round about his hole this be a high pride and presumption that they for who soul I must answer to GOD and who enjoy their life and liberty under i will not only be recusant themselves but infect and draw other after they as I have say in parliament house I can love the person of a Papist be otherwise a good man and honest breed never have know any other religion but the person of a apostate Papist I hate and sure for those polypragmaticke papist I will you will study out some severe punishment for they for they keep not infection in their own heart only but also infect other our good subject and that which I say for recusant the same I say for priest I confess I be loath to hang a priest only for religion sake and say mass but if he refuse the oath of allegiance which let the pope and all the devil in hell say what they will yet as you find by my book and by diverse other be mere civil those that so refuse the oath and be polypragmaticke recusant I leave they to the law it be no persecution but good justice and those priest also that out of my grace and mercy have be let go out of prison and banish upon condition not to return ask i no question touch these quit i of they and let i not hear of they and to they I join those that break prison for such priest as the prison will not hold it be a plain sign nothing will hold they but a halter such be no martyr that refuse to suffer for their conscience Paul notwithstanding the door be open will not come forth and Peter come not out of the prison till lead by the angel of God but these will go forth though with the angel of the devil I have give order to my lord of Canterbury and my lord of London for the distinction etc. of the degree of priest and when I have a account from they then will I give you another charge concern they another thing that offend the realm be abundance of alehouse and therefore to avoid the give occasion of evil and to take away the root and punish the example of vice I will have the infamous ale house pull down and a command to all justice of peace that this be do I may complain of alehouse for receipt of stealer of my deer but the country may complain for steal their horse ox and sheep for murder cut of purse and such like offence for these be their haunt devour beast as lion and bear will not be where they have no den nor covert so there will be no thief if they have not their receipt and these alehouse as their den another sort be a kind of alehouse which be house of haunt and receipt for debauch rogue and vagabond and idle sturdy fellow and these be not proper alehouse but base victualler such as have nothing else to live by but keep house of receipt for such kind of customer I have discover a strange pack of late that within ten or twelve mile of London there be ten or twelve person that live in spite of i go with pistol and walk up and down from harbour to harbour kill my deer and so shift from hold to hold that they can be apprehend for rogue you have many good act of parliament Edward the six though he be a child yet for this he in his time give better order than many king do in their age you must take order for these beggar and rogue for they so swarm in every place that a man can go in the street nor in the high way norany where for they look to your house of correction and remember that in the chief justice pophams time there be not a wander beggar to be find in all Somersetshire be his native country have a care also to suppress the build of cottage upon commons which be as bad as alehouse and the dweller in they do common steal dear coney sheep ox horse break house and do all manner of villainy it be true some ill justice make gain of these base thing take a account of the justice of peace that they may know they do these thing against the will of the King I be likewise to commend unto you a thing very necessary highway and bridge because no commonweal can be without passage I protest that as my heart do joy in the erection of school and hospital which have be more in my time then in many aage of my predecessor so it grieve i and it be wonderful to see the decay of charity in this how scant man be in contribute towards the amendment of highway and bridge therefore take a care of this for that be do to day with a penny that will not be do hereafter with a hundred pound and that will be mend now in a day which hereafter will not be mend in a year and that in a year which will not be do in our time as we may see by Paul steeple another thing to be care for be the new building here about the city of London concern which my proclamation have go forth and by the chief justice here and his predecessor popham it have be resolve to be a general nusans to the whole kingdom and this be that which be like the spleen in the body which in measure as it overgrowes the body waste for be it possible but the country must diminish if London do so increase and all sort of people do come to London and where do this increase appear not in the heart of the city but in the suburb not give wealth or profit to the city but bring misery and surcharge both to city and court cause dearth and scarcity through the great provision of victual and fuel that must be for such a multitude of people and these building serve likewise to harbour the worst sort of people as alehouse and cottage do I remember that before Christmas be twelvemonth I make a proclamation for this cause that all gentleman of quality shall depart to their own country and house to maintain hospitality among their neighbour which be equivocal take by some as that it be mean only for that Christmas but my will and meaning be and here I declare that my meaning be that it shall always continue one of the great cause of all gentleman desire that have no calling or errand to dwell in London be apparent the pride of the woman for if they be wife than their husband and if they be maid than their father must bring they up to London because the new fashion be to be have no where but in London and here if they be unmarried they mar their marriage and if they be marry they lose their reputation and rob their husband purse it be the fashion of Italy especial of Naples which be one of the rich part of it that all the gentry dwell in the principal town and so the whole country be empty even so now in England all the country be get into London so as with time England will only be London and the whole country be leave waste for as we now do imitate the french fashion in fashion of clothes and lackey to follow every man so have we get up the italian fashion in live miserable in our house and dwell all in the city but let we in God name leave these idle foreign toy and keep the old fashion of England for it be wont to be the honour and reputation of the english nobility and gentry to live in the country and keep hospitality for which we be famous above all the country in the world which we may the better do have a soil abundant fertile to live in and now out of my own mouth I declare unto you which be in this place be equal to a proclamation which I intend likewise short hereafter to have public proclaim that the courtier citizen and lawyer and those that belong unto they and other as have plea in term time be only necessary person to remain about this city other must get they into the country for beside the have of the country desolate when the gentry dwell thus in London diverse other mischief arise upon it first if insurrection shall fall out as be late see by the leveller gather together what order can be take with it when the country be unfurnished of gentleman to take order with it next the poor want relief for fault of the gentleman hospitality at home three my service be neglect and the good government of the country for lack of the principal gentleman presence that shall perform it and last the gentleman lose their own thrift for lack of their own presence in see to their own business at home therefore as every fish live in his own place some in the fresh some in the salt some in the mud so let every one live in his own place some at court some in the city some in the country special at festival time as Christmas and Easter and the rest and for the decrease of new building here I will have the builder restrain and commit to prison and if the builder can be find than the workman to be imprison and not this only but likewise the building to be cast down I mean such building as may be overthrow without inconvenience and therefore that to be do by order and direction there may be many other abuse that I know not of take you care my lord the judge of these and of all other for it be your part to look unto they I hear say robbery begin to abound more than heretofore and that some of you be too merciful I pray you remember that mercy be the king not you and you be to do justice where true cause be and take this for a rule of policy that what vice most abound in a commonwealth that must be most severe punish for that be true government and now I will conclude my speech with GOD as I begin first that in all your behaviour aswell in your circuit as in your bench you give due reverence to GOD I mean let not the church nor churchman be disgrace in your charge nor papist nor puritan countenance countenance and encourage the good churchman and teach the people by your example to reverence they for if they be good they be worthy of double honour for their office sake if they be faulty it be not your place to admonish they they have another forum to answer to for their misbehaviour next procure reverence to the king and the law inform my people true of i how zealous I be for religion how I desire law may be maintain and flourish that every court shall have his own jurisdiction that every subject shall submit himself to law so may you live a happy people under a just king free enjoy the fruit of peace and justice as such a people shall do now I confess it be but a as they say in the school that I be come hither yet though this be the first it shall not with the grace of GOD be the last time of my come now my choice be take away for have once be here a mean occasion may bring i again and I hope I have ever carry myself so and by God grace ever will as none will ever suspect that my come here will be to any partial end for I will ever be careful in point of justice to keep myself unspotted all the day of my life and upon this my general protestation I hope the world will know that I come hither this day to maintain the law and do justice according to my oath
